title: Response and Error Codes
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, [contact Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

Docusign CLM uses standard HTTP response codes and error objects to communicate the results of Docusign CLM API calls. Note that there are subsets of codes that only apply to certain operations, such as authentication or file downloads.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
The CLM API returns these response codes for successful requests:

**200 - OK**: Success response.

**201 - Object Created**: The POST request to create an object via the Object API was successful.

**202 - Request Accepted for Asynchronous Processing**: This is the standard response for a POST request to the Task API. The response object contains an `Href` property indicating the URL to which you can make a GET request to find the status of the task.

**204 - No Content**: The server has completed its work, but there is nothing to return to the user. This code is returned in response to delete requests that eliminate the object from the system. Note that soft deletes such as deleting a document or folder only move the object to the trash, and therefore return a 200 response code instead of a 204 response code.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
These response codes only apply when downloading documents through the [Content API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/content-api/):

**205 - Partial Content**: The download was incomplete.

**406 - Not Acceptable**: The accept header for the request is not supported.

**416 - Requested Range Not Satisfiable**: The byte range in the request is invalid.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
The API returns these authorization and security response codes:

**401 - Unauthorized**: There is an invalid or missing authentication token in the request. This is also the standard response for an invalid authentication request.

**403 - Forbidden**: The user is not authorized to perform the operation. The server understood the operation request, but the user does not have rights in Docusign CLM to perform this operation. If the user does not have permissions to see the object or the object does not exist, a 404 response code is returned.

**429 – Rate Limit Exceeded**: The API rate limit has been exceeded. See  [API Rules and Resource Limits](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/rules-and-limits/) for more information.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
The following error codes are returned:

**400 - Bad Request**: The client has sent a request object that the server could not parse. This error is often caused by malformed JSON in the request.

**404 - Not Found**: Returned when the requested object does not exist. This is also returned when the object exists in Docusign CLM, but the user does not have access to it based on their security profile. When this occurs, the error message is "Object does not exist or the user does not have access rights".

**422 - Unprocessable entity**: Returned for validation errors where the Docusign CLM application will not accept the request, such as using an invalid character in a document name. See [Validation errors](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/response-error-codes/#validation-errors) for more information.

**500 - Internal Server Error**: An unexpected server error occurred. This response code generally indicates a system error that must be addressed by Docusign. If the error persists, contact Docusign Support.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
When an error response code is returned, the response body contains the REST API error object:

```
{
  "Error": {
    "HttpStatusCode": 401,
    "UserMessage": "Access Denied",
    "DeveloperMessage": "Access Denied",
    "ErrorCode": 103,
    "ReferenceId": "b797912a-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-6febc4e07875"
  }

}
```

The error object contains the following properties:

- `HttpStatusCode`: The HTTP response status code.
- `UserMessage`: A user-friendly message that you can display to the end user in UI-based applications. In some cases, this value is the same as the `developerMessage`.
- `DeveloperMessage`: Technical error information that developers can use for debugging.
- `ErrorCode`: The Docusign CLM error code for the error message.
- `ReferenceId`: A Docusign CLM internal code for the error instance. Refer to this value when communicating with Docusign CLM support about the error.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
Responses to validation errors include an array of validation error objects. For example, this response is returned when you try to create a folder with a name that contains invalid characters:

```
{
  "Error": {
    "HttpStatusCode": 422,
    "UserMessage": "Validation Error",
    "DeveloperMessage": "See the list of validation errors",
    "ErrorCode": 101,
    "ReferenceId": "430df1ca-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-89fd73642d25"
  },
  "ValidationErrors": [
    {
      "PropertyName": "Name",
      "UserMessage": "Names cannot contain the following characters: \ / : * ? " < > |",
      "DeveloperMessage": "Names cannot contain the following characters: \ / : * ? " < > |",
      "ErrorCode": 1001
    }
  ]
}
```

The validation error object contains the following properties:

- `PropertyName`: The property in the request that did not pass validation.
- `UserMessage`: A user-friendly message that you can display to the end user in UI-based applications. In some cases, this value is the same as the developer message.
- `DeveloperMessage`: Technical error information that developers can use for debugging.
- `ErrorCode`: The Docusign CLM error code for the error message.

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
Docusign CLM returns the following application error codes, along with a `UserMessage` and `DeveloperMessage` that you can use to investigate and resolve issues:

**100** - General Error

**101** - Validation Error

**103** - AccessDenied

**104** - Not Found

**105** - Failed To Save

**106** - Conflict

**107** - Invalid Folder Filter

**108** - Invalid System Folder

**109** - Folder Not Found

**110** - Invalid Sort Property

**111** - Search Criteria Missing

**112** - Too Many Items Requested

**113** - Parent Folder Required

**114** - Cannot Parse Uid From Uri

**115** - Cannot ParseId From Uri

**116** - File Data Required

**117** - Document Not Found

**118** - Manager Not Found

**119** - Document Not Yet Available

**120** - Uploads Not Allowed

**121** - Downloads Not Allowed

**122** - Can Not Update Lock On Version

**123** - Can Not Update Version

**124** - Invalid Page

**125** - Not Supported

**126** - Workflow Not Found

**127** - Share Link Folder Or Document Missing

**128** - Can Not View Lock On Version

**129** - MultiPart File Without Name

**130** - File Too Big

**131** - Aborted Completed Workflow

**132** - Signaled Completed Workflow

**133** - Invalid Filter Property

**134** - Required Property Missing

**135** - Missing Search Task

**136** - Missing Change Security Task

**137** - Security Object Required

**138** - Invalid Workflow Signal

**139** - Missing Document Process Tracking Task

**140** - Folder Required

**141** - Document Required

**142** - Scope Required

**143** - Invalid Group Type

**144** - Signature Task Not Found

**145** - Content Disposition With Filename Required

**146** - Unauthorized

**147** - Only One Document Allowed For External Review

**148** - New External Review Is Not Enabled

**149** - External Review Is Not Enabled

title: Response and Error Codes
text:
Validation errors occur when the request is syntactically correct, but the rules configured in the Docusign CLM application do not allow the request to be completed. The API returns several validation error codes that you can display to the end user:

**1001** - Invalid Name

**1002** - Attribute Validation Failed

**1003** - Invalid Selection

**1004** - Invalid Parameters Or Access Denied

**1005** - Attribute Group Does Not Exist

**1006** - Attribute Field Does Not Exist

**1007** - Invalid Role

**1008** - Change Security Allowed Properties

**1009** - Invalid Access Type

**1010** - Invalid Email

**1011** - Invalid Document Href

**1012** - Document Is Checked Out

**1013** - At Least One Document Required For External Review

**1014** - Check Out Permission Denied

**1015** - Date Must Be In The Future

**1016** - Invalid Sender

**1017** - NotOutForExternalReview

title: API Rules and Resource Limits
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, [contact Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

The Docusign CLM API has set rate limits on API usage to preserve resources. You can exceed the API rate limits in one of the following ways:

1. Exceeding the maximum number of requests per account per hour. This number is specified by the `X-RateLimit-Limit` header returned in the response to the rate limit error.
2. Exceeding the maximum number of concurrent requests for a single account. The maximum is 6,250 calls per 15-minute interval.

The rate limits should not be reached with normal API usage. If a rate limit is exceeded, a 429 HTTP response code is returned with additional information, including the maximum number of requests allowed, and the date and time when requests for the account can resume.

title: API Rules and Resource Limits
text:
The following error objects are returned for requests that exceed the rate limits.

**Exceeded the maximum number of requests per account per hour**

```
{

  "Error":

  {

    "HttpStatusCode":429,

    "UserMessage":"Rate limit exceeded.",

    "DeveloperMessage":"Rate limit exceeded.",

    "ErrorCode":103,

    "ReferenceId":"5594dbc0-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-9721d441a1aa"

  }
```

**Exceeded the maximum number of concurrent requests for a user**

```
{

  "Error":

  {

    "HttpStatusCode":429,

    "UserMessage":"You may only have XX concurrent requests open.",

    "DeveloperMessage":"User request forbidden due to XX concurrently open requests.",

    "ErrorCode":103,

    "ReferenceId":"7b45dd15-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-4fe5df8e950a"

  }
```

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, [contact Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

Salesforce clients must authenticate by using Web Token (JWT) Grant. However, before you authenticate, you first need to configure your application’s connection to Docusign CLM.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
text:
Before you can authenticate your Salesforce client, you must:

- Have a [Salesforce developer account](https://developer.salesforce.com/signup).
- Have a [Docusign developer account](https://go.docusign.com/o/sandbox).
- Have created and configured an app in your Docusign Developer Account by following the steps in [Building a Docusign integration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/build-integration/).
- Have a Docusign CLM account.
- Have installed a package that creates custom metadata and written [Apex](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.apexcode.meta/apexcode/apex_intro_what_is_apex.htm) code that uses this package. To get started quickly, you can download [this example package](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-clm-apex). For more information about Apex, see the [Apex Toolkit Overview](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/salesforce/salesforce101/apex-toolkit/).
- Configure your Salesforce client to connect to Docusign.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
text:
To authenticate your Salesforce client, you will need this information:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Data element** | **Description** |
| API Account ID | A globally unique identifier [(GUID)](http://guid.one/) value that identifies your account. To find your API Account ID, log in to your Docusign eSignature account and select **Admin**, then select [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=apiIntegratorKey) from the sidebar. Copy the **API Account ID** value. |
| User ID | To find your User ID, log in to your Docusign eSignature account and select **Admin**, then select [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=apiIntegratorKey) from the sidebar. Copy the **User ID** value. |
| Integration Key | An [integration key](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/configure-app/) that identifies your app. To find your integration key, log in to your Docusign eSignature account and select **Admin**, then select [Apps and Keys](https://admindemo.docusign.com/authenticate?goTo=apiIntegratorKey) from the sidebar. Under **My Apps / Integration Keys**, copy the integration key for your Salesforce client. |
| Private Key | The private key that you created and saved when you created and configured your app in your Docusign Developer Account by following the steps in [Building a Docusign integration](https://developers.docusign.com/platform/build-integration/). |

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
heading_path: Configure your Salesforce client to connect to Docusign
text: To configure your Salesforce client to connect with Docusign, you need to perform the following steps in the Salesforce web application.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
heading_path: Configure your Salesforce client to connect to Docusign
Set up custom metadata in Salesforce
Deploy the custom metadata and Apex package to Salesforce
text:
1. If you have not already downloaded the [custom metadata and Apex package](https://github.com/docusign/code-examples-clm-apex), download it now.
2. Deploy the files in the package to Salesforce.org. You can use [Workbench](https://workbench.developerforce.com/login.php) for the installation.
   1. Zip all of the files so that the folders and the .XML file in the **src** folder are at the root of the zip file.
   2. Log in to Workbench and select **Migration > Deploy**.
   3. Select the ZIP file that you downloaded. Check the **Single Package** checkbox and select **Next**.
   4. Select **Deploy**.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
heading_path: Configure your Salesforce client to connect to Docusign
Set up custom metadata in Salesforce
Add your Docusign information to Salesforce
text:
After you have successfully deployed the source files to your Salesforce organization, you need to add your Docusign information, such as your Docusign API User ID and API Account ID, to Salesforce as custom metadata. These values are used to generate the access token and connect to Docusign.

Log in to the Salesforce web application use these steps to add your Docusign information:

1. Select **Setup > Custom Metadata Types**.
2. Under **DocusignRESTSettings**, select **Manage Records**.
3. Enter the values for your Docusign instance in the following settings:
   - **DSAccount**: Enter the Docusign API Account ID.
   - **DSUserName:** Enter the Docusign User ID.
   - **RequestIntegratorKey**: Enter the Integration Key.
   - **RequestPrivateKey**: Enter the Private Key you created when you created and configured your app in your Docusign Developer Account.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
heading_path: Configure your Salesforce client to connect to Docusign
Set up Remote Site URLs in Salesforce
text:
Next, in the **Remote Site Settings** area of the Salesforce web application, add the following Docusign URLs as **Remote Site URLs**:

1. `https://account-d.docusign.com` for demo accounts.
2. `https://account.docusign.com` for production accounts.
3. The appropriate REST API URL for your environment. You can find this in CLM Admin in the **Integrations** section of the **System Domains** page.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
heading_path: Obtain consent to impersonate the user for API calls
text:
Next, you need to ask the Docusign user to grant consent for your app to perform actions on their behalf. For service integrations, you can set up a service user and grant consent on this user's behalf.

**Note**: You will need to request special CLM-required scopes.

To complete this step, open this URI in a browser:

```
https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth?response_type=code&scope=signature%20impersonation%20spring_read%20spring_write&client_id=YOUR_INTEGRATION_KEY&redirect_uri=https://localhost.com
```

Make sure that:

1. For the `client_id`, you substitute the correct Integration Key for `YOUR_INTEGRATION_KEY`.
2. The value for the `redirect_uri` parameter matches the redirect URI configured for the Integration Key in your Docusign Developer Account.

When you open the URL in your browser, a consent screen displays. Select **Accept**.

![Screenshot of the prompt for the user to grant consent](https://developers.docusign.com/img/clm-api/consent.jpg?v=2023061320)

After you select **Accept**, you will be redirected to the redirect URI you specified.

title: Salesforce Client Authentication
text:
To get started with using CLM objects, see [Object API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/object-api/).

title: Task API
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, [contact Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

The CLM Task API exposes Docusign CLM services as tasks that can be executed and monitored programmatically. It offers methods for generating documents, manipulating documents, advanced search, and other long-running document operations. JSON is the supported format for requests and responses.

Task API operations are based on a task object. However, the properties associated with the task object vary depending on the operation you are invoking. In general, you only need to include a subset of task properties to invoke a Task API method. See the [API reference](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/) for details.

title: Task API
text:
Tasks are asynchronous, and are invoked and monitored in the following way:

1. To initiate a task operation, a POST request is made to a task endpoint. The request passes in a task object containing the subset of properties that the specific endpoint requires.
2. All POST requests to initiate a task will return a result object. The structure of the result object varies based on the task being invoked, but all result objects have some commonalities:
   1. An `Href` property that contains a URI that uniquely identifies the task invocation. You can make a GET request to this URI to check the status of the task and retrieve the results. For a longer-running task, you can periodically poll GET requests to this URI to check the current status and get the results of the task.
   2. A `Status` property that indicates the state or result of the operation. Statuses for most tasks include: `Processing`, `Success`, and `Failure`. Some tasks may also have specialized statuses. See the API reference for more information.
   3. Result properties that vary based on the task.
3. Some tasks allow you to cancel the operation. To cancel a task operation, make a DELETE request to the task URI.

**Note:** Although the Docusign CLM Advanced Workflow engine is a long-running series of tasks, it is not exposed via the Task API. The engine’s workflows, work items, and queues are considered objects and are exposed in the [Object API](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/object-api/).

title: Task API
text:
**Example search request for "Contract" in the document name**

Headers: `Accept: 'application/json'`

URI: `https://apina11.springcm.com/v2/documentsearchtasks`

Method: POST

JSON:

```
{
  "Title":"Contract"
}
```

title: Go-Live
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

When you are ready to launch your app in production, you will need to promote your application’s integration key from your developer account to a production Docusign account by passing a Go-Live review, similar to the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) process for the eSignature API.

Before you can begin the Go-Live process, you must have a paid production Docusign account with a plan that includes Docusign CLM.

When you are ready to start Go-Live review for your application, follow the steps described on the [Go-Live](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/go-live/) overview page for Docusign eSignature.

**Note:** If your application fails Go-Live review, you may be required to bring it into compliance with the [API Rules and Resource Limits](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/clm101/rules-and-limits/) before you can Go-Live.

After the form is processed (which takes up to three business days), your integration key will be copied into production, enabling your app to call production CLM API endpoints.

title: API Categories
text:
This API is separated into major categories. Each category covers one or more resources and the resources' methods.

| Category | Summary |
| --- | --- |
| [Content](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/) | API methods for uploading, downloading, and versioning documents in Docusign CLM.  Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales). |
| [Objects](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/) | API methods that expose Docusign CLM services that are invoked as asynchronous tasks, such as zipping files or running searches.  Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales). |
| [Tasks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/) | API methods that expose Docusign CLM services that are invoked as asynchronous tasks, such as zipping files or running searches.  Developing with the CLM API is only available for CLM customers with a production account. To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API, contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales). |

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Content Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [DraftDocument](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/draftdocument/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId}/draft ```  Draft document |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId} ```  Download a document by ID |
| [GetGeneratedDocument](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/getgenerateddocument/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/generateddocuments/{documentId} ```  Download a Docusign CLM task generated document by ID |
| [Head](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/head/) | HEAD  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId} ``` |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId} ```  Check in a document |
| [Upload](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/content/content/upload/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{folderId}/documents ```  Upload a document |

title: : Get
text:
Specify file type of document (image, PDF) in ACCEPT header. Do not specify an ACCEPT header for native format

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| documentId \* | string | ID of document to be drafted |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| zoom | integer | Percentage of zoom for image retrieval. Defaults to 100. |
| page | integer | Page number of document for image retrieval. Defaults to 1. |

\* Required

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.

contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetGeneratedDocument

GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/generateddocuments/{documentId}
```

text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| documentId \* | string | ID of asynchronous task responsible for the document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

\* Required

title: : Head
text:
Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Head
text:
HEAD

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{documentId}
```

title: : Head
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| documentId \* | string | ID of document to be drafted |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| zoom | integer | Percentage of zoom for image retrieval. Defaults to 100. |
| page | integer | Page number of document for image retrieval. Defaults to 1. |

\* Required

text:

| Query Parameters |  |  |

| name | string | Name of document. |

\* Required

title: : Get
text: [AttributeGroups](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/attributegroups/)

title: : Get
text:
Get an attribute group

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/attributegroups/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of attribute group. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (Attributes): Attributes contained within the group. |

\* Required

title: : Get
text:

title: : GetAllAttributeGroups
text: [AttributeGroups](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/attributegroups/)

title: : GetAllAttributeGroups
text:
Get the attribute groups of the current account

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAllAttributeGroups
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/attributegroups
```

title: : GetAllAttributeGroups
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id} ```  Delete a clause document. |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id} ```  Get a clause document. |
| [GetAllClauses](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/getallclauses/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses ```  Get all the clause documents in the current account. |
| [GetClauseOptions](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/getclauseoptions/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id}/options ```  Get the options of a clause document. |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id} ```  Update a clause document. [Partial] |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses ```  Create a clause document. |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id} ```  Update a clause document. [Full] |

title: : GetAllClauses
text:
Get all the clause documents in the current account.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAllClauses
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/clauses
```

title: : GetAllClauses
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetClauseOptions
text: [Clauses](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/clauses/)

title: : GetClauseOptions
text:
Get the options of a clause document.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetClauseOptions
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/clauses/{id}/options
```

title: : GetClauseOptions
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of clause document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: Contacts Resource
text:
Contacts resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Contacts Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id} ```  Delete a contact |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id} ```  Get a contact |
| [GetAllContacts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/getallcontacts/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts ```  Get the contacts of the current account |
| [GetGroups](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/getgroups/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id}/groups ```  Get the groups of a contact |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id} ```  Update a contact |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts ```  Create a contact |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id} ```  Update a contact |

title: : GetAllContacts
text:
Get the contacts of the current account

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAllContacts
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/contacts
```

title: : GetAllContacts
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetGroups
text: [Contacts](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/contacts/)

title: : GetGroups
text:
Contacts can only be members of distribution groups.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetGroups
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/contacts/{id}/groups
```

title: : GetGroups
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of contact. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : Get
text:
Get a Document Generation Configuration

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/doclauncherconfigurations/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of the configuration |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (DestinationFolder): The folder where generated documents will be saved |

\* Required

title: : GetAllConfigurations
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetDocumentPreview
text:
Gets the document preview.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocumentPreview
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documentPreviews/{id}
```

title: : GetDocumentPreview
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | The identifier. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| extractPages | boolean | if set to true [extract pages]. |
| extractOverlays | boolean | if set to true [extract overlays]. |
| processOverlays | boolean | if set to true [process overlays]. |

\* Required

text: [DocumentPreview](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentpreview/)

title: : GetDocumentPreviewPageImageStatus
text:
Gets the document preview page image statuses.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocumentPreviewPageImageStatus
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documentPreviews/{id}/pageImageStatuses
```

title: : GetDocumentPreviewPageImageStatus
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | The identifier. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id} ```  Permanently deletes a document reminder |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id} ```  Get document reminder with specified id |

| [GetRecipientUserItems](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/getrecipientuseritems/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id}/recipientusers ```  Get the document reminders user recipients |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id} ```  Update a document reminder |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders ```  Create a new document reminder |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documentreminders/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id} ```  Update a document reminder |

Developing with the CLM API is only available

title: : GetRecipientContactItems
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | id of the document reminder |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text:

/v2/{accountId}/documentreminders/{id}/recipientgroups
```

title: : GetRecipientGroupItems
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

\* Required

title: Documents Resource
text:
Documents resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Documents Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id} ```  Move a document to the trash folder. |
| [DeleteLock](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/deletelock/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/lock ```  Release a lock on a document |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id} ```  Get a document by ID |
| [GetByPath](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getbypath/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents ```  Get a document by path |
| [GetCustomData](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getcustomdata/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/customdata ```  Query a CSV document by ID |
| [GetDocumentProcessTrackingActivities](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getdocumentprocesstrackingactivities/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/documentprocesstrackingactivities ```  Get the workflow user steps containing a document |
| [GetDocumentReminders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getdocumentreminders/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/documentreminders ```  Get the document reminders for a document |
| [GetDocumentWorkItems](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getdocumentworkitems/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/workitems ```  Get workitems associated with the document |
| [GetExternalReviewFromDocument](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getexternalreviewfromdocument/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/reviews ```  Gets the external review associated with the document |
| [GetHistoryItems](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/gethistoryitems/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/historyItems ```  Get the history of a document |
| [GetLock](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getlock/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/lock ```  Get the lock status of a document |
| [GetRelatedDocuments](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getrelateddocuments/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/relateddocuments ```  Get related documents |
| [GetShareLinks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getsharelinks/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/sharelinks ```  Get the share links of a document |
| [GetVersions](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/getversions/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/versions ```  Get the versions of a document |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id} ```  Update a document |
| [PatchLock](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/patchlock/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/lock ```  Update a lock on a document |
| [PostLock](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/postlock/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/lock ```  Create a lock on a document |
| [PublishDocument](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/publishdocument/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/publish ```  Publishes document by ID. Returns document information. |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id} ```  Update a document |
| [PutLock](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/putlock/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/lock ```  Update a lock on a document |

title: : Get
text:
Get a document by ID

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (ParentFolder): Folder object in which the document resides. (Path): Full folder path to the document in the account. (HistoryItems): History of actions performed on the document. (Versions): Previous versions of the document. (Lock): If the document is locked and by whom. (EosParentInfo): Information about the external object storage folder that contains the document. (ShareLinks): All of the share links that have been created for the document. (DocumentProcessTrackingActivities): Workflow user steps containing the document. (AttributeGroups): Attribute groups saved on the document. |

\* Required

title: : GetDocumentProcessTrackingActivities
text: [Documents](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/)

title: : GetDocumentProcessTrackingActivities
text:
Get the workflow user steps containing a document

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocumentProcessTrackingActivities
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/documentprocesstrackingactivities
```

title: : GetDocumentProcessTrackingActivities
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetDocumentReminders
text:
Get the document reminders for a document

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocumentReminders
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/documentreminders
```

title: : GetDocumentReminders
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

Developing with the CLM API is only available

title: : GetHistoryItems
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/historyItems
```

title: : GetHistoryItems
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |

title: : GetVersions
text: [Documents](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/documents/)

title: : GetVersions
text:
Get the versions of a document

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetVersions
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documents/{id}/versions
```

title: : GetVersions
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of document. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: Folders Resource
text:
Folders resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Folders Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id} ```  Move a folder to the trash folder |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id} ```  Get a folder by ID |
| [GetByEos](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getbyeos/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/eos ```  Get a folder by eosObjectType and eosObjectId |
| [GetByPath](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getbypath/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/path ```  Get a folder by path |
| [GetBySystemType](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getbysystemtype/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/type ```  Get a folder by path or system folder type |
| [GetChildFolders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getchildfolders/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}/folders ```  Get the folders in a folder |
| [GetDocuments](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getdocuments/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}/documents ```  Get the documents in a folder |
| [GetShareLinks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/getsharelinks/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}/sharelinks ```  Get the share links of a folder |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id} ```  Update a folder |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders ```  Create a folder |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/{id} ```  Update a folder |
| [Search](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/search/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/folders/search ```  Search for folders |

title: : Get
text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : Get
text:
Get a folder by ID.

**Note:** Unlike the CLM UI, the API only returns security information explicitly defined for the folder. Inherited security information is not returned.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of folder. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | Valid values:   - `ParentFolder`: The folder object in which the folder resides. - `Documents`: The collection of documents contained within the folder. - `Folders`: The collection of folders contained within the folder. - `Path`: The full folder path to the folder in the account. - `EosParentInfo`: Information about the external object storage folder that contains the folder. - `Eosinfo`: Information about the folder's external object storage properties, if applicable. - `ShareLinks`: All of the share links that have been created for the folder. - `AttributeGroups`: Attribute groups saved on the folder. - `Security`: Permissions set on the folder by role, group, and user. Inherited security information is not returned. |

\* Required

title: : GetByEos
text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : GetByEos
text:
Get a folder by eosObjectType and eosObjectId

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetByEos
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/eos
```

title: : GetByEos
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| eosObjectType \* | string | External object type. |
| eosObjectId \* | string | External object ID. |
| expand | array | (ParentFolder): Folder object in which the folder resides. (Documents): The collection of documents contained within the folder. (Folders): The collection of folders contained within the folder. (Path): Full folder path to the folder in the account. (EosParentInfo): Information about the external object storage folder that contains the folder. (Eosinfo): Information about the folder's external object storage properties, if applicable. (ShareLinks): All of the share links that have been created for the folder. (AttributeGroups): Attribute groups saved on the folder. (Security): Permissions set on the folder by role, group, and user. |

\* Required

title: : GetByPath
text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : GetByPath
text:
Get a folder by path

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetByPath
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/path
```

title: : GetByPath
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| path | string | Path to folder in the account. |
| expand | array | (ParentFolder): Folder object in which the folder resides. (Documents): The collection of documents contained within the folder. (Folders): The collection of folders contained within the folder. (Path): Full folder path to the folder in the account. (EosParentInfo): Information about the external object storage folder that contains the folder. (Eosinfo): Information about the folder's external object storage properties, if applicable. (ShareLinks): All of the share links that have been created for the folder. (AttributeGroups): Attribute groups saved on the folder. (Security): Permissions set on the folder by role, group, and user. |

\* Required

text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : GetChildFolders
text:
Get the folders in a folder

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetChildFolders
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}/folders
```

title: : GetChildFolders
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of folder. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetShareLinks
text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : GetShareLinks
text:
Get the share links of a folder

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetShareLinks
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/{id}/sharelinks
```

title: : GetShareLinks
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of folder. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text: [Folders](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/folders/)

title: : Search
text:
Search for folders

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Search
text:
POST

```
/v2/{accountId}/folders/search
```

title: : Search
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| search \* | string | Search criterion. |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text:

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetUsers
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/groups/{id}/groupmembers
```

title: : GetUsers
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of group. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,

title: MailingLists Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id} ```  Delete a mailing list |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id} ```  Get a mailing list |
| [GetAllmailinglists](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/getallmailinglists/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists ```  Get the mailing lists in the current account |
| [GetUsers](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/getusers/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id}/groupmembers ```  Get the members of a mailing list |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id} ```  Update a mailing list |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists ```  Create a mailing list |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/mailinglists/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id} ```  Update a mailing list |

text:
Get a mailing list

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/mailinglists/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of mailing list. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (GroupMembers): The members of the mailing list, as a collection of users. |

\* Required

title: : GetAllmailinglists
text:
Get the mailing lists in the current account

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAllmailinglists
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/mailinglists
```

title: : GetAllmailinglists
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: Members Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id} ```  Get a member |
| [GetCurrent](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrent/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current ```  Get the current member |
| [GetCurrentPermissionSets](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentpermissionsets/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/permissionsets ```  Get the permission sets that the current member has assigned |
| [GetCurrentRecentDocuments](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentrecentdocuments/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/recentdocuments ```  Get the recently accessed documents of the current member |
| [GetCurrentWatchedDocuments](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentwatcheddocuments/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/watcheddocuments ```  Get the watched documents of the current member |
| [GetCurrentWatchedDocumentsProcessTrackingActivities](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentwatcheddocumentsprocesstrackingactivities/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/watcheddocumentsprocesstrackingactivities ```  Get the watched documents process tracking activities of the current member |
| [GetCurrentWorkflowActivities](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentworkflowactivities/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/workitems ```  Get the workflow inbox of the current member |
| [GetCurrentWorkflowQueues](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getcurrentworkflowqueues/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/workflowqueues ```  Get the workflow queues of the current member |
| [GetGroups](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getgroups/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id}/groups ```  Get the groups of a user |
| [GetGroupsCurrent](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getgroupscurrent/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current/groups ```  Get the groups of the current user |
| [GetMembers](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getmembers/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members ```  Get the members in the current account |
| [GetPermissionSetsForMember](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getpermissionsetsformember/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id}/permissionsets ```  Get the permission sets that the member has assigned |
| [GetWorkflowActivities](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getworkflowactivities/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id}/workitems ```  Get the workflow inbox of a member |
| [GetWorkflowQueues](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/getworkflowqueues/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id}/workflowqueues ```  Get the workflow queues of a member |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id} ```  Update a member |
| [PatchCurrent](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/patchcurrent/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current ```  Update the current member |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/{id} ```  Update a member |
| [PutCurrent](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/putcurrent/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/members/current ```  Update the current member |

title: : Get
text: [Members](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/)

title: : Get
text:
Get a member

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/members/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of member. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (ManagedBy): The member who manages this member. (ManagedUsers): The users whom this member manages. (WorkItems): The member's workflow inbox. (RecentDocuments): Recently accessed documents by this member. (WorkflowQueues): Details regarding this member's workflow queues. (Groups): The list of this member's group membership. (PortalOnly) member can only access Docusign CLM though portal. (ExemptFromUserSync) member is ignored by Salesforce member sync. (PermissionSets): permission sets that the member is assigned to |

\* Required

title: : Get
text:

title: : GetCurrent
text: [Members](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/)

title: : GetCurrent
text:
Get the current member

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetCurrent
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/members/current
```

title: : GetCurrent
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (ManagedBy): The member who manages this member. (ManagedUsers): The users whom this member manages. (WorkItems): The member's workflow inbox. (RecentDocuments): Recently accessed documents by this member. (WorkflowQueues): Details regarding this member's workflow queues. (Groups): The list of this member's group membership. (PortalOnly) member can only access Docusign CLM though portal. (ExemptFromUserSync) member is ignored by Salesforce member sync. (PermissionSets): permission sets that the member is assigned to |

\* Required

title: : GetCurrentPermissionSets
text: [Members](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/)

title: : GetCurrentPermissionSets
text:
Get the permission sets that the current member has assigned

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetCurrentPermissionSets
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/members/current/permissionsets
```

title: : GetCurrentPermissionSets
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text: [Members](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/members/)

title: : GetCurrentWatchedDocuments
text:
Get the watched documents of the current member

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetCurrentWatchedDocuments
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/members/current/watcheddocuments
```

title: : GetCurrentWatchedDocuments
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetWorkflowActivities
text:
Get the workflow inbox of a member

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetWorkflowActivities
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/members/{id}/workitems
```

title: : GetWorkflowActivities
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of member. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required
