title: Parties Resource
text:
Parties resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Parties Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties/{id} ```  Delete a party |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties/{id} ```  Get a party by ID |
| [GetAllParties](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/getallparties/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties ```  Get all parties in account with paging |
| [Patch](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/patch/) | PATCH  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties/{id} ```  Partial update party |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties ```  Create party |
| [Put](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/parties/put/) | PUT  ```  /v2/{accountId}/parties/{id} ```  Update a Party |

title: : GetAllParties
text:
Get all parties in account with paging

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAllParties
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/parties
```

title: : GetAllParties
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetAll

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text:

Gets an External review

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/reviews/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | Id of the external review |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (Documents) List of Document object for the associated external review |

\* Required

title: ShareLinks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/sharelinks/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/sharelinks/{id} ```  Delete a share link |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/sharelinks/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/sharelinks/{id} ```  Get a share link |

text:
Delete a share link

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Delete
text:
DEL

```
/v2/{accountId}/sharelinks/{id}
```

title: : Delete
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of share link. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

\* Required

text: [ShareLinks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/sharelinks/)

title: : Get
text:
Get a share link

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/sharelinks/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of share link. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (Folder): The object representing the folder which the link shares. (Document): The object representing the document which the link shares. |

\* Required

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetWorkItems
text:
Get the work items of a workflow queue

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetWorkItems
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/workflowqueues/{id}/workitems
```

title: : GetWorkItems
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of workflow queue. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text:
Workflows resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: Workflows Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workflows/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/workflows/{instanceId} ```  Stop a workflow instance |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workflows/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/workflows/{instanceId} ```  Get a workflow instance |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workflows/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/workflows ```  Start a workflow instance by definition name |
| [PostSignal](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workflows/postsignal/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/workflows/{instanceId}/signal ```  Signal a workflow instance |

text: [WorkItems](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workitems/)

title: : Get
text:
Get a work item

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : Get
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/workitems/{id}
```

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of work item. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (Assignee): The user to whom the item is assigned. (Documents): The documents involved in the work item. (AssigneeInstructions): Explicit instructions provided to the assignee. (Workflow): The workflow instance containing this item. (Selections): Possible choices for completing a human step. Used in conjunction with the Selected property. |

\* Required

text: [WorkItems](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/objects/workitems/)

title: : GetDocuments
text:
Get the documents of a work item

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocuments
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/workitems/{id}/documents
```

title: : GetDocuments
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id \* | string | ID of work item. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: Tasks Category
text:
API methods that expose Docusign CLM services that are invoked as asynchronous tasks, such as zipping files or running searches.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: BulkWorkflowTasks Resource
text:
BulkWorkflowTasks resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: BulkWorkflowTasks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/bulkworkflowtasks/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/bulkWorkflowTasks/{taskId} ```  Delete workflows via bulk job |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/bulkworkflowtasks/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/bulkWorkflowTasks/{id} ```  Get a bulk workflow task by id |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/bulkworkflowtasks/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/bulkWorkflowTasks ```  Send a task to start workflows via bulk job |

title: : Post
text: [ChangeSecurityTasks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/changesecuritytasks/)

title: : Post
text:
Submit an asynchronous task to change the security on a given folder. Set security by user, group, and/or role. Possible values for AccessType: InheritFromParentFolder, NoAccess, View, ViewCreate, ViewEdit, ViewEditDelete, ViewEditDeleteSetAccess, Custom.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: DocLauncherTasks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/doclaunchertasks/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/doclaunchertasks/{taskId} ```  The ID is returned by the doclaunchertasks POST method. |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/doclaunchertasks/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/doclaunchertasks ```  Submit an asynchronous task to create an instance of a DocLauncher using the configuration and data specified in your POST. |

title: : GetDocumentLoadResult
text:
The ID is returned by the DocumentLoadTask POST method.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetDocumentLoadResult
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documentsloadtasks/{taskid}/result
```

title: : GetDocumentLoadResult
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| taskid \* | string | ID of DocumentLoadTask. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: DocumentProcessTrackingTasks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentprocesstrackingtasks/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentprocesstrackingactivitytasks/{taskid} ```  Fetch active documents by reference ID |
| [GetHistory](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentprocesstrackingtasks/gethistory/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentprocesstrackinghistorytasks/{taskid} ```  Fetch document process history by reference ID |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentprocesstrackingtasks/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentprocesstrackingactivitytasks ```  Fetch active documents by folder |
| [PostHistory](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentprocesstrackingtasks/posthistory/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentprocesstrackinghistorytasks ```  Fetch document process history by document |

title: : Get
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| taskid \* | string | Reference ID of DocumentProcessTrackingActivityTask. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

text: [DocumentProcessTrackingTasks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentprocesstrackingtasks/)

title: : GetHistory
text:
You must POST a DocumentProcessTrackingHistoryTask before you can retrieve it through this route.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetHistory
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documentprocesstrackinghistorytasks/{taskid}
```

title: : GetHistory
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| taskid \* | string | Reference ID of DocumentProcessTrackingHistoryTask. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: DocumentSearchTasks Resource
text:
DocumentSearchTasks resource

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: DocumentSearchTasks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Get](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentsearchtasks/get/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentsearchtasks/{taskid} ```  Retrieve a previous document search |
| [GetSearchResult](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentsearchtasks/getsearchresult/) | GET  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentsearchtasks/{taskid}/result ```  Retrieve a result of a search |
| [Post](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/documentsearchtasks/post/) | POST  ```  /v2/{accountId}/documentsearchtasks ```  Search for documents |

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
The ID is returned by the documentsearchtasks POST method.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/documentsearchtasks/{taskid}/result
```

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| taskid \* | string | ID of DocumentSearchTask. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (AttributeGroups): Attribute groups saved on the document. |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : Post
text:
Submit an asynchronous task to merge a document template with XML data. User must provide either XML data, an attribute document or a content document. If no destination folder is specified, an HREF to download the document will be provided.

text: [FolderSearchTasks](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/foldersearchtasks/)

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
The ID is returned by the foldersearchtasks POST method.

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/foldersearchtasks/{taskid}/result
```

title: : GetSearchResult
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| taskid \* | string | ID of FolderSearchTask. |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expand | array | (AttributeGroups): Attribute groups saved on the folders. |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: : GetAll
text:
Get a list of available process definitions

Developing with the CLM API is only available
for CLM customers with a production account.
To purchase a plan to gain access to the CLM API,
contact [Docusign Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales).

title: : GetAll
text:
GET

```
/v2/{accountId}/processdefinitions
```

title: : GetAll
text:
| Path Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| accountId \* | string | The ID of the Docusign account. |

| Query Parameters |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| pageSortParams.offset | integer | The number of objects at the beginning of the collection to skip |
| pageSortParams.limit | integer | The number of objects per page |
| pageSortParams.sortProperty | string | The property of the collection object on which to sort |
| pageSortParams.sortDirection | string | ASC for ascending. DESC for descending |
| pageSortParams.filter | string | The property of the collection object on which to filter |
| pageSortParams.filterExact | boolean | If true, filtering will perform an exact match instead of the default contains match |
| pageSortParams.caseInsensitive | boolean | If FilterExact is true and this is true, filtering will perform an exact match but will ignore case |

\* Required

title: SignatureTasks Resource
text:
| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Delete](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/clm-api/reference/tasks/signaturetasks/delete/) | DEL  ```  /v2/{accountId}/signaturetasks/{taskid} ```  Cancel an outstanding electronic signature task |

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
How can contract management software help your team save time and money?

[Get Started](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales)

449% ROI from using Docusign CLM

2,200 enterprises trust Docusign for contract lifecycle management

90% reduction in time spent generating a new sales contract

85% reduction in errors

- Accelerate cycle times

  AI-powered tools help you automate tasks, streamline complex workflows, and eliminate bottlenecks—driving smarter, more efficient execution.
- Optimize agreement value

  Standardize contract management processes and increase oversight while saving your legal team time.
- Eliminate unnecessary risks

  Incorporate compliance requirements into contract management. Unlock the full value of agreements and find answers to critical business questions.

[Guide to Contract Lifecycle Management](https://www.docusign.com/sites/default/files/resource_event_files/guide_to_contract_lifecycle_management.pdf)

![](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/6IW1hokmFcOd7U8Rgn64cv/15b7228ddcfc2ed43ee8ec629b0076ec/ui-clm-assigned-to-me.png?fm=avif&q=90&w=500)

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
Generate contracts in a click with dynamic templates and use AI to flag non-compliant clauses, suggest approved language, and draft new clauses on demand.

- Auto-populate new agreements with data from systems like Salesforce
- Set conditional rules for things like review of non-standard terms
- Allow Legal to define a library of pre-approved clauses

![CLM product image of a master subscription and service agreement showing collaboration through user comments.](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/2Rjt2tF5bDNYSk94h1jBog/32363269af4c2de1a1c740eebdf0aa70/Clm-Collaborate.png?fm=avif&q=90&w=500)

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
Stay aligned with AI-Assisted Review, custom playbooks, and seamless integrations with your team’s everyday tools.

- Automate contract routing for internal and external review with detailed version control
- Complete reviews fast with comments and tasks that notify users and let them take action via email and Slack
- Accelerate review cycles with AI-assisted negotiation and summarization capabilities

![CLM Essentials standalone](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/7mdGMIjejdmn7SE6ZFVNK6/70c0f3bdbcc006e08833e8f0d91757c3/ui-docusign-clm-manage-contracts.svg)

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
Eliminate blockers and reduce risk with automated workflows built for your business processes—flexible and adaptive to ensure contracts get the right review at the right time.

- Use a drag-and-drop editor to design contract processes
- Use 100+ pre-configured contract management workflow steps to generate, review, approve, send for signature, store contracts, and more
- With pre-built connectors and rich APIs, use CLM with the tools your team is already using

![CLM product image showing all contracts and centralized location.](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/EIbQYc5DUxS7hl8ROzWP4/03a0a5b99e4920ad55791baba1f648cd/ui-clm-documents.svg)

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
Leverage an AI-powered repository to unlock valuable agreement insights and keep track of key milestones.

- Provide a central, accessible source of truth for your legal department and other users
- Manage obligations, renewals and more with agreement reports
- Search and filter agreements by keyword, concept and meta-data

![Docusign product UI](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/1Mj0YC5SxEhu9gjf45PWnk/6c5fef1454f38c32daf97ca3347d5ff2/ui-sap-ariba-insight-connector.svg)

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
Use AI and analytics built for contracts to save time, reduce risk and make contracts more discoverable.

- Extract, analyze and report on key contract data points and legal topics with over 100 pre-trained AI models
- Conditionally drive workflows with analytics, risk scores and contract content for faster contract cycles
- Automate review and surface insights that help close sales deals faster, reduce procurement costs and speed legal review

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
- Vendor trust and track record

  [Learn More](https://www.docusign.com/gartner-magic-quadrant-contract-lifecycle-management)
- Seamless integrations, industry-leading partners

  Maximize existing investments and empower your whole organization with role-based user experiences and interoperability with key line-of-business tools like Salesforce and SAP Ariba.

  [Learn More](https://www.docusign.com/integrations)
- Configurability and customization

  Realize a return on investment faster with easy-to-adopt contract management capabilities and templated best practices, balanced with the customization required to meet your evolving business needs.

  [Learn More](https://www.docusign.com/blog/making-business-flow-more-efficiently-kores-contracting-transformation)
- Advanced AI capabilities

  Accelerate contracting with AI-assisted review and negotiation, automatic extraction of key terms, and generative AI-powered Q&A—all driven by Iris, Docusign’s purpose-built AI engine trained on decades of contract data to deliver accurate, secure, and trusted agreement intelligence.

  [Learn more](https://www.docusign.com/blog/ai-assisted-review)
- Robust services and support

  Improve your digital transformation process and accelerate adoption with services and support capabilities from the world’s largest ecosystem of agreement experts.

  [Learn More](https://www.docusign.com/customer-success)

text:
- [![SAP Ariba logo.](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/75CnYyy5hsiAapqsY5o6pZ/16b74cb02b14746b05c4f7507930aa7c/sap_ariba.jpg?fm=avif&q=90&w=500)](https://assets.ctfassets.net/3fcisxc3a6xz/7fGrUwhC02mcUB48QVdrAk/6a2391350cdf7c917baabc936b0133d1/CLM_Connector_for_SAP_Ariba-Datasheet.pdf)

  SAP Ariba

  Eliminate friction in source-to-pay agreement processes. Create new supplier agreements directly from Ariba, easily ingest third-party documents, and seamlessly manage and track workflow tasks across systems.

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
Learn more about contract lifecycle management
text:
Get contract lifecycle management software resources.

[View More Resources](https://www.docusign.com/products/clm/toolkit)

- ### [Considering CLM solutions](https://www.docusign.com/blog/how-to-choose-a-contract-management-solution "Considering CLM solutions")

  Find out how to choose a contract management software for your business.
- ### [Contract lifecycle management 101](https://www.docusign.com/blog/what-is-contract-lifecycle-management "Contract lifecycle management 101")

  Understand how CLM systems work and learn about implementation best practices.
- ### [Tips for integration](https://www.docusign.com/blog/best-practices-contract-management-system-integrations "Tips for integration")

  Learn about best practices for integrating other systems with CLM.

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
- ###

  Gartner defines contract lifecycle management (CLM) as the “applications used to manage contracts from inception through time management and eventual renewal or termination.” In simpler terms, contract lifecycle management refers to the methods you use to prepare, sign, store, and execute your business agreements.
- ###

  Contract management software are applications that help businesses and departments manage contracts more efficiently. Unlike manual document management strategies, CLM software is designed to help you easily store and find contracts, vendor information, standard contract clauses, and contract templates.
- ###

  According to the WCC Association, the cost of inefficient contract management can amount to more than 9% of a company's total annual revenue. Docusign CLM customers see an average of 449% ROI in their business operations. Contract management can help a companies achieve significant business results, such as, accelerating revenue recognition, increasing employee productivity, reducing fixed costs—through building capabilities and legally compliant self-service features to make doing business easier.
- ###

  An important part of analyzing contract lifecycle management (CLM) workflows is breaking down the contract lifecycle into recognizable stages. The [five critical stages of a contract's lifecycle](https://www.docusign.com/blog/five-stages-contract-management) are contract creation, negotiation, routing, approval/signature, and storage.
- ###

  Contract management is often long and opaque, and the additional inefficiency of these processes lengthens the payback period of contracts. Inefficient contract management also makes it difficult to track supplier contract overruns across the enterprise. In addition, contracting processes are prone to human error and introduce different types of risks: incoming contracts may contain terms that put a business at high risk or even block the progress of a deal. Thanks to the AI ​​capabilities [of contracts](https://www.docusign.com/blog/contract-ai) and analytics, you can reduce risk, speed up negotiations, and improve team performance.
- ###

  Effective contract management can help companies save time and money, minimize risk and increase sales potential. Digital contract management allows companies to get contracts signed and in progress much faster than in the past, while increasing contract visibility and follow-up. This results in improved efficiency of contracting processes, which saves time and improves the time to value of sales contracts.

  CLM software that leverages AI can also ease the pressure on legal departments, allowing you to focus on more interesting and productive tasks. What's more, it can identify new opportunities and risks that would otherwise go unnoticed.
- ###

  Docusign CLM software integrates with applications you already use, such as Salesforce, Coupa, or SAP Ariba. [Learn more about our integrations](https://www.docusign.com/integrations)
- ###

  With Docusign CLM, you can store, label, and organize contracts in one searchable repository designed specifically for all agreements. This makes it easier to quickly locate existing agreements, compile status reports, and extract insights to reduce risk and improve business function.
- ###

  Once a contract is generated, changes or modifications often need to be made as part of the contract negotiation process, before the final version is ready for signing: this is the red review process. The objective is to produce a final document that satisfies all parties. The term “redlining” comes from the old way of changing contracts: manually, with a red pen on paper.
- ###

title: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
heading_path: CLM: Manage your contracts with a trusted industry leader
text:
red review process. The objective is to produce a final document that satisfies all parties. The term “redlining” comes from the old way of changing contracts: manually, with a red pen on paper.
- ###

  Contract management software like Docusign CLM lets you send contracts for review, track changes across multiple versions, and keep an audit trail of who did what and when. It also helps you store, label, and organize all contracts in one, searchable place, with granular permissions controls. With powerful CLM software, you can create reports on workflows and contracts to uncover insights and monitor all contract processes.

title: Additional Methods for Uploading Documents to Docusign CLM
heading_path: Additional Methods for Uploading Documents to Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the additional methods for uploading documents and content to Docusign CLM.

Several additional methods can be used to upload content and documents to Docusign CLM. These methods can be used extensively in your implementation but
are typically used for a special purpose. Some of these methods might require extra
installations of third-party or Docusign CLM applications to enable
functionality.

Note: Some content upload methods are available only to CLM.CM customers.
To determine which version of Docusign CLM you have, see [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify your account type in Docusign CLM.").

Inbound E-Mail
:   Sends an email to Docusign CLM by using a folder-specific email
    address. The email and its attachments will be added as new content to the folder. This is
    typically used when receiving content from outside of an organization from non-Docusign CLM users.

Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP)
:   Adds content to Docusign CLM by using a third-party SFTP client. This
    method is typically used to upload large numbers of files or to retain a folder hierarchy
    for the inbound files.

Web Services/API
:   Accesses Docusign CLM from outside software programs by using the Docusign CLM API.

title: Move and Copy Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Move and Copy Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to move and copy documents within folders in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, users can move documents between
locations; cut and paste documents between locations; and copy and paste documents
between folders.

Use this procedure to move and copy one or more documents in Docusign CLM

Tip: In addition to the method that this
procedure describes, other methods exist as follows:

- You can move documents by selecting and dragging them between folders on the
  Documents page.
- You can select the checkboxes for documents on the
  Documents page, and select the
  Cut, Copy, or
  Paste options from the Edit menu.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Select the checkbox for each document to manage.
4. Select File > Move or Copy.

   The Move or Copy page opens.
5. Select the Select link and choose a folder destination

   The product shows your selection in the
   Destination field.
6. Perform one of the following actions to move or copy documents:

   - Select Move to move the selected documents to the
     selected folder.

   - Select Copy to copy the selected documents to the
     selected folder.

   You have moved or copied selected documents to a new
   location.

title: Preference Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the My Preferences page in Docusign CLM. Users can use this page in CLM to
configure their account settings.

You can use the My Preferences page in Docusign CLM to configure your
CLM account settings.

Note: You will only be able to configure settings that your CLM
administrator has enabled for your Docusign CLM account.

Localization Settings
:   You can configure your language and region settings in your CLM account allowing
    you to manage you work in your native date and time formats. For more
    information about configuring localization preferences in CLM, see [Configure Your Localization Preferences in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=dts1576610029141.html&_LANG=enus "You can configure your language and region settings in your Docusign CLM account allowing you to manage you work in your native date and time formats.").

Email Settings
:   You can configure your email signature in your CLM account. For more information
    about configuring your email signature in CLM, see [Configure Your Email Signature in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=don1659213582980.html&_LANG=enus "You can configure your email signature in your Docusign CLM account.").

Tasks
:   You can configure the page you return to after completing a task in CLM. For
    more information about configuring your task preferences in CLM, see [Configure Your Task Preferences in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=zve1659728465428.html&_LANG=enus "You can configure your task preferences in Docusign CLM. You can instruct the product to return to your inbox after you complete a task in Docusign CLM.").

Notification Preferences
:   You can configure your notification preferences in CLM. For more information about
    configuring your notification preferences in CLM, see [Enable or Disable CLM Slack
    Notification (User)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dtv1597882236545&topicId=nqj1648676511957.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about search in Docusign CLM. You can execute searches, apply filtering, save
searches, customize search results, and export search results.

In Docusign CLM, administrators and users can search for documents and content.

Note: The default search behavior is the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results."). On the Account
Settings page, the Enable Modernized Search Experience
setting is selected by default. If an administrator disables the setting, the product provides
a [legacy search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=tgh1708707979539.html&_LANG=enus "Read about legacy search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results."). We recommend using the
modernized search.

When performing searches, you can also perform any of the following activities:

- Apply filtering to refine a search
- Save a search query
- Customize columns for search results
- Export search results
- Download documents from search results

title: Edit a Widget on Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Edit a Widget on Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to edit widgets on your dashboard in Docusign CLM.

You can make changes to widgets on a Docusign CLM
dashboard.

1. Locate the widget you want to edit.
2. In the widget, select Edit Widget from the
   Edit Properties
   ![Edit Properties](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Overflow-Horizontal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.87uY0Tv-Aln49ejCFf1f26qoPhg6wwIc0dcHB_ihrec&_LANG=enus "Edit Properties") dropdown.
3. Make any necessary changes, and select Save.

You have edited the widget on your dashboard.

title: Configure Your Email Signature in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure Your Email Signature in Docusign CLM
text:
You can configure your email signature in your Docusign CLM account.

You can configure your email signature in your Docusign CLM account.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select My Preferences from the user profile menu.
3. Configure your email signature in the Email Settings
   section.
4. Select Save.

   You have configured your email signature.

title: Configure Your Localization Preferences in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure Your Localization Preferences in Docusign CLM
text:
You can configure your language and region settings in your Docusign CLM account
allowing you to manage you work in your native date and time formats.

You can configure your language and region settings in your Docusign CLM account. This
allows you to manage your work in your native date and time formats.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select My Preferences from the user profile menu.
3. Select a language from the Language dropdown menu.

   Note: The
   Language setting controls the language displayed in the user
   interface. By default, this field is set to the CLM account default.
4. Select a time zone from the Time Zone dropdown menu.

   Note: The Time
   Zone setting formats timestamps and notifications.
5. Select a region from the Region dropdown menu.

   Note: The
   Region setting formats dates and times. By default, this field
   detects your localization settings from your web browser.
6. Select Save.

   You have configured your localization settings.

title: Filters for Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Filters for Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Look up filters available in advanced search

Note:

- This topic applies to the legacy search experience. By default, the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.") is
  enabled in account settings. We recommend using modernized search.
- Advanced search is available in CLM and CLM+ but not in CLM Essentials.

In Docusign CLM advanced search, you can use filters to narrow or expand search results. These
filters can be configured from the Filters tab on the side
panel in advanced search. Current filters display once a filter is applied. You can
clear configured filters by selecting Reset from the side
panel.

Has the words
:   Specifies words that you want to include in your search. This searches the
    title, description, modified by, attribute value, attribute group names, and
    file content. For example, you can search for all folders that contain “New
    Hire” in the name.

Doesn't have the words
:   Specifies words that you do not want to include in your search. This searches the name,
    description, modified by, attribute value, attribute group names, and the
    content of a file. For example, you can exclude folders that contain “New
    Hire” in the name.

Include Documents, Folders, Documents, and Folders
:   Includes documents, folders, or both in your search results.

Location
:   Specifies the folder you want to target your search. Limits your search to a
    specific folder.

Search all document versions
:   Searches all document versions for the criteria you define. By default,
    Docusign CLM searches only the most recent version.

Search includes trash
:   Includes trashed items when searching documents and folders. Search results will include
    any matching items that the product finds in the trash folder. By default,
    this filter checkbox is unselected.

    Note: If you select
    the checkbox, you can also select Trash in the Location selection box.
    This configuration searches only for documents and folders that are in
    the trash.

Properties
:   Limits your search to files that contain keywords within a specific
    property. These properties include name, description, document content, and
    attributes.

Document Type
:   Limits your search by document type. (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, Image,
    Other) Default search behavior includes all document types unless you
    otherwise specify.

Modified
:   Limits your search to items modified by a specific user or modified during a
    specific date range. Default search behavior includes all results regardless
    of modified by or modified date.

Attributes
:   Include documents with specific attribute groups or exclude documents
    without an attribute group applied.

Add Attribute Field
:   You can add attribute fields as search filters. For more information about
    adding an attribute field as a filter, see [Add an Attribute Field Filter to Advanced Search in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=wjt1648749200312.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add an attribute field as a search filter to Docusign CLM Advanced Search.").

title: Document Annotation in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to annotate documents in Docusign CLM.

Note: This documentation covers legacy document preview experiences for
Docusign CLM. As of July 1, 2025, most customers have been upgraded to
Document Review as their default document preview experience in Docusign
CLM. For more information on deprecation of document preview experiences, see [Docusign Document Preview Experience Changes FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ssg1729007411846&topicId=twq1729007565221.html&_LANG=enus). For more information on features and enhancements available in Document Review, see [Document Review in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=eyk1666363075054.html&_LANG=enus).

In Docusign CLM, users can collaborate using annotations or comments on documents. For legacy
preview users, document annotations can be made through pinpoints. Pinpoints enable users to
add comments to documents without changing the content of the document itself. Comments can be
created and viewed when the document is opened in the legacy Document Preview. Multiple users
can add comments to the document, enabling collaboration around the content.

If interactive preview is enabled for Docusign CLM, users can annotate using comments.
Comments can be created by any user with access to the document, and can be used to create
tasks. Tasks can be assigned or reassigned to task groups or users.

title: Widgets for Docusign CLM Dashboards
text:
Look up the functions of widgets in Docusign CLM dashboards.

You can use widgets to organize information for your business purposes. The following table
describes widgets that can be configured on Docusign CLM dashboards:

| Widget | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Folder | Displays the contents of any folder that the user has access to. This widget allows you to search folders and documents. You can also execute document actions. You can configure the widget to show specific folders or all folders in the account. |
| Folder watchlist | Displays document tasks in a specified folder that are assigned to users as part of a workflow. To use this widget, folders must be added to the watchlist through a workflow step. Note: For more information, see the Add Documents to Watched Documents List step description in [Automated Document Management Workflow Steps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=hpj1576610096401.html&_LANG=enus). |
| My watchlist | Displays document tasks that are assigned to users as part of a workflow. To use this widget, documents must be added to the watchlist through a workflow step. Only documents that have active tasks get displayed in the widget. After the task gets completed, the document no longer displays in the widget. Note: For more information, see the Add Documents to Watched Documents List step description in [Automated Document Management Workflow Steps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=hpj1576610096401.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Recent documents | Displays the 10 most recently accessed, viewed, or modified documents by the current user. You can also perform document actions on any of the listed documents. |
| Report | Displays a selected report. This widget allows some reports to be exported in CSV or XLSX format. Note: For more information about the widget, see [Visualize Document Reports on the Dashboard in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lye1642705772274&topicId=yfw1603415758563.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Saved Search | (Available in [modernized search](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus)  ) Displays the results from a saved search. The person who adds the widget selects the saved search to display. From an existing widget, you can perform the following activities:  - Select a document from the results to open the document   preview in a new tab - Select Open to access the full page   of search results for the saved search   Note: You can edit the widget to change to a different saved search. After saving your changes, you must refresh the browser page. |
| Search | Enables selection of a saved search and displays search results on the dashboard. Note: For more information on search in Docusign CLM, see [Search in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=aug1576609932447.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Workflow inbox | Displays workflow tasks that are assigned to the current user. This widget can be configured to show tasks from specific workflows. |

title: Document Check-In and Check-Out in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Document Check-In and Check-Out in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about check-in and check-out of documents for editing or changes in Docusign CLM.

Users can check-in and check-out documents for editing and changes In Docusign CLM. Checked-out documents are locked by a user, preventing edits,
changes, or revisions by other users. Only the person who has checked-out the document can
edit it. Users that have the correct permissions for the document will still be able to view
and download it.

Once all changes have been made, users can check-in the document to save the revised document
to Docusign CLM. Checked-in documents are saved as a new version of that
document, and preserved in the version history in Document Review.

Note:

- Check-ins, check-outs, and document versions are preserved as part of document version
  history in Document Review. For more information on version history and comparing document
  versions, see [Compare Document Versions With Document
  Review in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=shn1666363438998.html&_LANG=enus).
- Docusign Edit can be used to check-out and make changes to a selected
  document. For more information, see [Edit Documents With
  Docusign Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=vhz1658776840526.html&_LANG=enus).

title: View My Preferences in Docusign CLM
heading_path: View My Preferences in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to view and change your preferences in Docusign CLM.

You can view your preferences and user profile in Docusign CLM. Your preferences and user
profile indicate your permission levels, groups, and localization settings.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select My Preferences from the user profile menu.

   The My Preferences screen displays.
3. Select or make changes to your preferences, and view your profile on Docusign CLM:

   - Change your localization, region settings, and email signature in the
     General section.
   - View your user personas, permission profile, and groups in the
     My Profile section.
   - Change settings for notifications in the Notification
     Settings section.
4. Once all changes have been made, select Save.

   You have successfully viewed and made changes to your preferences in
   Docusign CLM.

title: Export Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Export Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to export search results from Docusign CLM. Exporting search results lets you
save or share the information as needed.

title: Export Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Export Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
This task applies to the legacy search experience. By
default, the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.") is
enabled in account settings. We recommend using modernized search.

After performing a search in Docusign CLM, you can export the search results to your computer
as a CSV file. Exporting search results lets you save or share the information as
needed.

Note: The downloaded CSV file contains the columns as they are
configured on the Search Results page. To learn about
customizing the columns in search results, see [Customize Columns on the Search Results Page in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jjn1648752647865.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the columns on the Search Results page in Docusign CLM.").

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Navigate to the Search Results page:
   1. Select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.IQlqJ_U75RSvTpgC6McxGgU6SBbQglyzYbeCWlFWCWU&_LANG=enus) in
      the navigation header.
   2. Select Go to Advanced Search.

   The Search Results page opens.
3. Perform a search:
   1. Complete fields on the Filters panel to set up
      your search criteria.
   2. Select Search.

      The product displays search results.
4. (Optional) Select Customize Columns
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.IQlqJ_U75RSvTpgC6McxGgU6SBbQglyzYbeCWlFWCWU&_LANG=enus), and[customize the columns for the search
   results](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jjn1648752647865.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the columns on the Search Results page in Docusign CLM.").
5. Select Actions > Export Search Results.

The product exports the search results to your computer as a CSV
file.

title: Folder History Actions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Folder History Actions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about each of the logged folder history events in Docusign CLM.

Here is a list of logged Folder History actions that will display when folder history is
viewed in Docusign CLM:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| - When a folder is created - When a folder is moved - When a folder is renamed - When a folder description is set, changed, or cleared - When a folder is moved to the trash - When attributes are added, changed or removed on a folder | - When a fax number is assigned to or unassigned from a folder - When pinpoints are enabled or disabled on a folder - When an email address is added, changed or removed - When a folder is shared - When a folder share is accessed - When security is set, changed or removed on a folder |

Folder history can be filtered by:

- Folder Activity
- Attribute Changes
- Security Activity
- Share Activity

title: Restore Deleted Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Restore Deleted Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to recover deleted documents from the trash in Docusign CLM.

If a document was mistakenly deleted or needs to be
restored, you can recover it from the trash in Docusign CLM. Deleted content is
organized into folders by date of deletion (in YYYYMMDD
format).

Note:

- Users can only delete CLM files and folders to which they have at least
  View, Edit & Delete access.
- CLM does not restore deleted content to its original location. You must cut
  deleted content from the trash folder and paste it to a selected
  location.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents view opens.
3. Select the Trash folder.
4. Select the YYYYMMDD folder with the date that the content
   was deleted.

   You see any documents that are in the folder.
5. (Optional) View the document's history to see who deleted the document and see
   its original location:
   1. Select the checkbox next to the document, and select View > History.
   2. Select Done when finished reviewing the
      history.

      The product redisplays the YYYYMMDD
      folder.
6. Select the checkbox next to the file to restore, and select Edit > Cut.
7. Navigate to the folder or location where the file will be restored, and select Edit  >  Paste.

A message indicates that the content has been moved to the new
location. You have successfully restored deleted content from the trash to a folder in
Docusign CLM. The restored content is available in the Documents
view and search results in Docusign CLM.

title: Add a Widget to Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add a Widget to Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to add widgets to your dashboard in Docusign CLM. You can use widgets to
organize information for your business purposes.

In Docusign CLM, you can use widgets to create
visibility on document information and reports. Widgets help you organize information for your
business purposes.

Note: To learn more about widgets and their functions
in Docusign CLM, see [Widgets for Docusign CLM Dashboards](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=fgc1642618158282.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the functions of widgets in Docusign CLM dashboards.").

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Dashboard in the navigation header.

   The dashboard page opens.
3. Select Add > Widget.

   The Add Widget menu opens at the bottom of the
   screen.
4. Select a widget, and drag it onto the dashboard.

   The product might display a dialog box for you to configure widget properties.
   If not, the product adds the widget to the dashboard.
5. Configure the widget properties (if prompted), and select
   Save.

You have added a widget to your dashboard in Docusign CLM.

title: Compare Document Versions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Compare Document Versions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to compare document versions in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, you can compare two versions of a Microsoft Word
document. Comparing document versions enables users to identify the differences between
versions. Differences are highlighted, enabling quick acceptance or rejection of
changes.

This feature can help eliminate potential oversights in manual comparisons and can help
shorten revision cycles.

Note: Documents that are compared cannot be any larger than 128
MB.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Select the checkbox next to the document you want to compare.
4. Select File > Compare Documents.

   A comparison dialog box opens.
5. Select the checkboxes for the two document versions to compare, and select
   Next.

   The Compare dialog box displays.
6. Configure your options for the document comparison merge:

   - To save the document comparison as a new version of the current document, select
     New version of.
   - To save the document comparison as a new document, select New document
     named:.
7. Select checkboxes for the document comparison options to be included in the new
   document:

   - Compare formatting compares changes in fonts, style, size,
     and other formatting properties
   - Compare headers/footers compares changes in headers and
     footers of a document
   - Compare comments compares changes in comments on a
     document
   - Show moves shows text that has been moved within the document
     body
8. Once all options have been chosen, select Compare. To select a
   different document for comparison, select Back.

   You have successfully created a document comparison. Depending on the options
   selected in step 6, the comparison will be saved as a new document, or a
   version.

title: Customize Columns for Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Columns for Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to customize the displayed columns for search results in Docusign CLM.
Administrators and users can customize columns to present the most relevant information as
needed.

title: Customize Columns for Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Columns for Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by
default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator disables
the setting.

You can control which columns get displayed on the search results page in Docusign CLM. You
can add the following items as visible columns in search results.

- Document metadata from the system

  Examples include agreement ID, created
  date, document ID, document file type, document location, modified by,
  modified date, object ID, object type, and party
- Standard document attributes
- Attribute fields from any custom attribute group

Column customizations persist unless you use a different browser. You can also select
a visible column name to sort the search results based on that column. For example,
you can display a column for the Expiration Date attribute. You can then sort the
results based on document expiration dates.

Note:

- You cannot move the Name column.
- You cannot sort results based on the Created Date column.
- If you customize columns and then launch a saved search, the saved search
  retains its original columns.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.umky_U4jV2rO2cBoGk21zl7O7CTOHlEx9Cuza-yYsvY&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. [Execute a search by entering search criteria
   and optional filters](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xto1707493278474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later use.").

   The product displays search results.
4. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.umky_U4jV2rO2cBoGk21zl7O7CTOHlEx9Cuza-yYsvY&_LANG=enus)
   (Customize Columns) icon on the search results page.

   The Columns dialog opens. The dialog lists columns
   that are currently visible on the search results page. Each listed column has a
   switch that is blue and right-aligned.
5. (Optional) Add columns:
   1. Select the Find by name text box.

      The product displays all available items. Items that are already
      visible have checkmarks next to them in the list.
   2. Scroll through the list, or type text in the text box, then select the
      desired item.

      The product adds the selected item to the Visible
      list.
6. (Optional) Hide or rearrange columns by performing any of the following actions
   in the Columns dialog:
   - Hide a column by selecting the right-aligned blue switch to remove the
     column.
   - (Optional) Rearrange a column’s order by selecting the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Drag-and-Move.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.umky_U4jV2rO2cBoGk21zl7O7CTOHlEx9Cuza-yYsvY&_LANG=enus) icon next to the
     column and dragging the column to a new location.

   The product updates the list of columns. You are now ready to save your
   customizations.
7. Select Save to apply your customizations.

The product updates the columns. Your search results page
now shows your column customizations.

title: Modernized Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.

Docusign CLM provides a modernized search experience to help you efficiently search for documents and content. While executing searches, you can also perform the following activities:

- Apply filtering criteria to narrow a search or locate specific items
- Save a search query for later use

  Note: Administrators can save a search that all users can use.
- Customize which columns of information get displayed in search results
- Export search results

Note:

- Modernized search is not available for CLM.CM account types. This search experience is available only for Docusign CLM accounts.
- On the Account Settings page, the Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by default. If you disable the setting, the product provides a [legacy search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=tgh1708707979539.html&_LANG=enus "Read about legacy search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results."). We recommend using the modernized search.
- A keyword search must contain at least four characters to return results. For examples of search keywords, see the documentation about [search examples for modernized search](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ref4fbe94ff-7ffb-40ec-9987-3ff38ad5e53e.html&_LANG=enus "Look up search examples for modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can review these examples to understand how the product interprets search keywords and produces search results.").

title: Modernized Search in Docusign CLM
text:
When Docusign CLM executes searches, the following behaviors apply:

- The product searches based on the beginning of words. The search is not based on the middle of words or the last characters of words. We recommend using complete words for all search words. If you specify multiple words, the product cannot find matches if a word is incomplete.

**Examples:** Searching for contra finds contractual and contracts. However, searching for tract does not find contract. Searching for Fontara Corporation produces results that match that name. However, searching for Fontara Corp produces no results for the full name.

- When a user performs a search, the results include only content that the user has access to.
- Docusign CLM ignores the following articles, pronouns, and prepositions in search indexing and search results: a, an, and, are, as, at, be, but, by, for, if, in, into, is, it, no, not, of, on, or, such, that, the, their, then, there, these, they, this, to, was, will, with.

title: Modernized Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: OCR and Text Extraction for Creating Searchable Content
text:
When you upload a document, Docusign CLM uses the following technologies to make content searchable:

Optical Character Recognition (OCR)
:   Identifies characters in scanned documents.

    Note: The results of text search depend on the quality of the uploaded image. Handwritten text on images is *not* processed by OCR.
:   Docusign CLM uses OCR when the following document types get uploaded:

    - PDF documents containing less than 150 text characters (including spaces)
    - JPEG documents
    - JPG documents
    - PNG documents
    - TIF documents
    - TIFF documents

    Note: If a PDF that you are uploading already has extracted text, Docusign CLM does not run OCR on the PDF.

Text extraction
:   Scans and extracts text. Docusign CLM uses text extraction when the following document types get uploaded:

    - DOCX documents
    - PDF documents containing at least 150 text characters and up to 500 pages

    Note: Text does not get extracted from documents with more than 500 pages. If your document exceeds 500 pages, you can split the document into multiple files. Splitting the document ensures that your text gets extracted.

title: View Document History in Docusign CLM
heading_path: View Document History in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to view the history of a document in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users
can see any changes that occurred on a document across revisions.

In Docusign CLM, users can see the history of a
selected document. This feature enables users to see any changes that occurred on a document
across revisions.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Navigate to the folder containing the document that you want to view.
4. Select the checkbox for that document, and select View > History.

   The document history page opens.
5. (Optional) Perform any of the following actions to manipulate the history data:

   - Sort entries by selecting the column header for any field.
   - Filter activity records by selecting the checkboxes for document action types.
6. Select Done to return to the document list.

   You have viewed the history of a selected document.

title: Check-Out, Edit, and Check-In a Document in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Check-Out, Edit, and Check-In a Document in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to check-out a document for edits and upload a new version for check-in in
Docusign CLM. Checking-out a document prevents other users from editing it
for the duration of the check-out. Checking-in a document uploads a new version of the document
to Docusign CLM.

Checking-out a document In Docusign
CLM prevents other users from editing or changing the selected document. Once the changes to
the document have been completed, users should check-in the document to unlock it. Checked-in
documents are saved to Docusign CLM as a new version of the document.

Note:
Docusign Edit can also be used to check-out and make
changes to a selected document. For more information, see [Edit Documents With Docusign Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=vhz1658776840526.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents from the Navigation Header.

   The Documents screen displays.
3. Select the checkbox for the document that you want to check-out.
4. Select Edit  >  Check Out.

   A notification appears, indicating that you have checked-out the selected
   document. The ![Locked document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Lock.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg0NTQsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.Tz1G96k7fONGAcvkZE-w4BgEZ9bWnnNB3TRFejrrk94&_LANG=enus "Locked document") icon appears next to the document name, indicating that it has been
   checked-out.
5. (Optional) Select File  >  Download to download a copy of the selected document to your computer.

   Note: A document can also manually be checked-in by selecting
   Cancel Check Out.
6. Once all changes have been made, select Edit  >  Check In.

   The Check In screen displays.
7. Select your options:

   - To upload a new copy of the selected document, select Upload,
     and choose a document.
   - To select a document that is already in Docusign CLM, choose
     Select, and select the checkbox for the document.
8. (Optional) Add a comment in the Description field for version
   history.
9. Select Save.

   A message displays, indicating that check-in was successful. You have
   successfully checked-out and checked-in a document in Docusign CLM. The
   document will be unlocked for editing.

title: Customize Columns on the Search Results Page in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Columns on the Search Results Page in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to customize the columns on the Search Results page in Docusign CLM.

title: Customize Columns on the Search Results Page in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Columns on the Search Results Page in Docusign CLM
text:
This task applies to the legacy search experience. By
default, the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.") is
enabled in account settings. We recommend using modernized search.

You can customize the columns that are displayed on the Search Results
page in Docusign CLM. You can also add account attributes as columns. Column
customizations display only in your instance of CLM. The customizations persist
until the next time you customize them.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Navigate to the Search Results page:
   1. Select Search
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNjIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.54aWC8VMdnUqN60-8OZntG9RkU_o1OFG_iBjnUKLDPw&_LANG=enus) in the
      navigation header.
   2. Select Go to Advanced Search.

   The Search Results page opens.
3. Select Customize Columns
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNjIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.54aWC8VMdnUqN60-8OZntG9RkU_o1OFG_iBjnUKLDPw&_LANG=enus).

   The Customize Columns dialog opens.
4. (Optional) Reorder columns by dragging columns and dropping them in the
   organization.
5. (Optional) Remove columns by deselecting the checkbox next to a column
   name.
6. Add columns:
   1. Select Add Columns.
   2. Select an attribute to add.
   3. Select Apply.

      The attribute is added as a column.
7. Select Apply to save your customizations.

The Search Results page gets refreshed. The
page now displays the column customizations that you configured.

title: Resume a Document Upload in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Resume a Document Upload in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to resume a document upload in Docusign CLM. For example, you can resume an
upload that was interrupted because of a network issue.

Document uploads might get interrupted. For example,
a browser timeout or network issue may cause an interruption. You can resume exactly where the
upload stopped.

Note: You can resume only when the original upload method
was drag-and-drop or the File > Upload menu action. The browser must support HTML5. If the following conditions are
not met, the upload starts from the beginning (rather than resuming):

- The document name, file size, and modified date must match the partially uploaded
  document.
- The document must be uploaded by the same user and from the same browser as the
  partially uploaded document.
