- You must resume the upload within 72 hours.

1. Open the web browser that you previously used to perform the upload.
2. Log on to Docusign CLM.
3. Navigate to the folder where you want to upload the document.
4. Upload the document through drag-and-drop or the File > Upload menu action.

The resumed file uploads into the selected folder.

title: Identify the Site ID for Your Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Identify the Site ID for Your Docusign CLM Account
text:
Learn how to identify the site (and region) that your Docusign CLM
account is associated with. Identifying the site is useful to estimate when your site might
receive product features that roll out incrementally.

In Docusign CLM, you can identify the site (and region) that your account is
associated with. Identifying the site is useful to estimate when your site might
receive product features that roll out incrementally.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.

   In the web browser, an address bar shows a URL that contains one of the
   following site ID values:
   - eu11 (Europe site)
   - eu21 (Europe site)
   - s1.eu (Europe site)
   - s2.eu (Europe site)
   - na11 (North America site)
   - na21 (North America site)
   - s1.us (North America site)
   - s2.us (North America site)
   - us11 (FedRAMP site for U.S. federal agencies)
   - us12 (FedRAMP site for U.S. federal agencies)
   - us13 (FedRAMP site for U.S. federal agencies)
2. Inspect the address bar URL to locate the site ID value.

You have identified the site ID for your Docusign CLM account. You can [review the order in which
sites typically receive product features that get incrementally rolled
out](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ttd1716521535621.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how Docusign releases product features incrementally to sites. This process provides a way to release product functionality efficiently on a fairly predictable schedule.").

title: Identify the Site ID for Your Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Identify the Site ID for Your Docusign CLM Account
text:
**Examples of Identifying a Site ID for Your CLM Account**

The following examples describe how to identify a site ID:

- You log in to Docusign CLM. The address bar includes the
  text “uatna11.springcm.com.” You are in the UAT environment, and your site
  is na11.
- You log in to Docusign CLM. The address bar includes the
  text “s1.us.demo.clm.docusign.com.” You are in the UAT environment, and your
  site is s1.us.

title: Download Documents from Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to download documents from Docusign CLM. Documents can be
downloaded in their original format or converted to PDF.

In Docusign CLM, you can download a copy of a stored document to your local machine. You
can download the document in its original format or download it as a PDF.

Documents can also be downloaded when the document is locked or checked out by another
user. In all cases, the copy of the document in Docusign CLM remains
unaffected in its native file format.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents view opens.
3. Navigate to the folder that contains one or more documents to download.
4. Select the checkbox next to one or more documents, and perform one of the following
   menu selections:

   - Select File > Download to download files in their original format.
   - Select File > Download as PDF to download documents in PDF format.

   The selected document is downloaded to your computer. You have successfully
   downloaded one or more documents from Docusign CLM.

title: Document Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about document management in Docusign CLM. Users with access to a document can
perform actions to manage the document.

After a document gets uploaded to Docusign CLM, users with access to the document can manage
the document. Users can also keep documents organized by creating and managing folders.
Document management can include any of the following activities:

- Moving and copying documents between folders
- Editing documents and commenting in documents
- Sending documents to parties for review
- Assigning tasks for review
- Comparing document versions

title: Upload a Document to Docusign CLM
heading_path: Upload a Document to Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to upload a document to Docusign CLM.

Uploading a document to Docusign CLM enables a robust
set of features for document management. Once a document has been uploaded, users can
take advantage of all of the features available in Docusign CLM. Users can collaborate
through comments and pinpoints, move documents as needed, and send documents for
review.

Note:

- There are multiple methods for uploading a document to Docusign CLM. For
  more information on other methods for uploading a document, see [Additional Methods for Uploading Documents to Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ahu1576610036723.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the additional methods for uploading documents and content to Docusign CLM.").
- Folders cannot be uploaded using drag and drop into Docusign CLM.
- A maximum of 20 files can be uploaded using drag and drop.
- Uploads to Docusign CLM that are interrupted can be resumed under the
  following conditions:
  - Document name, file size, and modified date must match.
  - The document must be uploaded by the same user.
  - The document must be uploaded from the same browser.
  - The user must resume the upload within 72 hours.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents from the navigation header.

   The Documents window displays.
3. To upload documents through the File menu:
   1. Navigate to the location or folder to upload the document, and select File  >  Upload.

      The Browse window displays, allowing you to select documents for
      upload.
   2. Select the documents you want to upload to Docusign. Once all documents
      have been chosen, select Open.
4. To upload documents through drag and drop:
   1. Locate the documents to upload to Docusign CLM on your computer.
   2. Navigate to the location or folder in Docusign CLM where you want to
      upload the documents.
   3. Drag the documents from the location on your computer onto the folder
      in Docusign CLM.

Docusign CLM begins uploading the selected documents to the
chosen directory. You have uploaded documents to Docusign CLM.

title: Configure a Saved Search Using Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure a Saved Search Using Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to save a pre-configured search. Administrators can use these steps to create
a saved search for users.

title: Configure a Saved Search Using Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure a Saved Search Using Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
text:
This task applies to the legacy search experience. By default,
the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.") is enabled in
account settings. We recommend using modernized search.

Note: Advanced search is available in CLM and CLM+ but not in CLM
Essentials.

Docusign CLM administrators can configure and save searches. After an administrator saves a
search, users can access the search from the Saved Search tab on the
side panel in advanced search.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Navigate to advanced search:
   1. Select Search
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.0-nK8bWm7u6KqQE1ODOsurJTn3fm8J6yA9jn3YU5WVU&_LANG=enus) in the navigation
      header.
   2. Select Go to Advanced Search.

   The Search Results page opens.
3. Configure search filters in the Filters tab on the side
   panel.
4. Select Search to execute the search.

   The product displays search results.
5. Select Actions > Save Search.

   The Save Search dialog opens.
6. Type a name for the search, and select Save.

The product saves the search. This saved search is now available to all users.

title: Export Search Results from Docusign CLM
heading_path: Export Search Results from Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to export search results from Docusign CLM. Exporting search results lets
administrators and users save or share the information as needed.

title: Export Search Results from Docusign CLM
heading_path: Export Search Results from Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by
default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator disables
the setting.

After performing a search in Docusign CLM, you can export the search results to your computer
as a CSV file. Exporting search results lets you save or share the information as
needed.

Note: The CSV file contains the columns that are configured on the search
results page. To make adjustments, you can customize columns on the search results
page.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.0bXAMSRLA2erA1COmG8DjzDCQiiSap5kIMZblX_ioRU&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. [Execute a search by entering search criteria
   and optional filters.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xto1707493278474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later use.")

   The product displays search results.
4. Export results by performing *one* of the following actions:
   - Export all results by selecting
     ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Overflow-Vertical.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.0bXAMSRLA2erA1COmG8DjzDCQiiSap5kIMZblX_ioRU&_LANG=enus)
      > Export as CSV.
   - Export specific results by selecting the checkbox for each listed item
     and selecting Export as CSV above the list.

You have exported the search results to your computer as a
CSV file.

title: Merge PDFs in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to merge PDF documents in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, you can merge two
or more PDFs into a single PDF document. PDFs are merged in the order that they appear in
Document Review.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Select the checkboxes for the PDFs that you want to merge.
4. Select File > Merge as PDF.

   The Merge Documents window opens.
5. Set up your merge as needed:
   1. Specify a name for the merged file.
   2. (Optional) Drag and drop documents to arrange them in the order that you want them
      merged.
   3. (Optional) Select Delete source documents after merge completes
      successfully.
   4. (Optional) Select the Select Folder link, and choose a
      different folder location for the merged PDF.

      The target folder gets displayed.
6. Select Merge.

   A message confirms that the documents were submitted for merging.
7. Refresh the product page until you see the merged PDF.

You have successfully merged PDF documents in Docusign CLM.

title: Email Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to send documents through email in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, you can send documents as email
attachments or share links to documents and folders. When you share a document through an
email, the action gets tracked in the document history. You can send Docusign CLM emails to
mailing lists or single users.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents view opens.
3. Select the checkboxes for documents to send by email, and select Send  >  Email.

   A page opens where you can complete fields for sending the email. The documents
   that you selected are displayed in the Attachments
   field.
4. Complete all required fields, and complete other fields as needed.

   Tip: To include additional documents as attachments,
   choose Select Items to navigate to other documents and select
   them.
5. Select Send.

The product schedules the email to be sent to the specified
recipients. The selected documents get included in the email as attachments.

title: Dashboards in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about dashboards and dashboard widgets in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users
can use dashboards to consolidate and organize information.

In Docusign CLM, administrators and users can use dashboards to consolidate and organize
information.

Administrators can create shared dashboards to control the visibility of folders, reports,
and document-linked tasks. Shared dashboards can also be applied globally for an entire
Docusign CLM account.

Users can use personal dashboards and widgets for visibility on document information and
reports.

title: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to apply filters to search results in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can apply filtering to refine and narrow the results.

title: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator disables the setting.

When performing a search in Docusign CLM, you can use filters to refine the search results. For example, you can filter your search based on file location, contract type, or object type.

You can add the following filters to refine the search results:

- Document metadata from the system

  Examples include agreement ID, created date, document ID, document file type, document location, modified by, modified date, object ID, object type, and party ID.
- Standard document attributes
- Attribute fields from any custom attribute group
- Filters that control whether search results include documents, folders, or both

Note: In modernized search, administrators can hide groups of attributes from searches. Users will not see hidden attributes as filters in searches or in search results. When creating or modifying an attribute group, the administrator can select the Hide attributes from search checkbox. If you hide a group, the Hidden from Search column displays a checkbox for that group on the Attribute Groups page.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.FRj1NRgJfl3SWZH5jFZD1mq8TOK80OrVyOSWADae3Ds&_LANG=enus) icon in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent searches and saved searches.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
   - Execute a search by entering search criteria.
   - Select a recent search query or saved search from the list.

   The product displays search results.
4. (Optional) Activate filters to control whether the results include documents, folders, or both:
   1. Select Change above the search results.
   2. Select one of the following options:

      - Documents Only (enabled by default)
      - Documents and Folders
      - Folders Only

   The product adds the filter and updates the search results.
5. Select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Filter.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.FRj1NRgJfl3SWZH5jFZD1mq8TOK80OrVyOSWADae3Ds&_LANG=enus) All Filters on the search results page.

   The All Filters dialog opens.
6. Add each filter:
   1. (Optional) Type text in the Find by filter name text box to search all attribute fields in your CLM account.

      The product lists matching attributes.
   2. Select an attribute, configure its properties, and select Apply.

      Tip: To search for items in the trash folder, select the In trash filter to enable it. You do not need to configure properties for this filter.
   3. (Optional) Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Star.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.FRj1NRgJfl3SWZH5jFZD1mq8TOK80OrVyOSWADae3Ds&_LANG=enus) (star) icon next to a filter to save it as a "starred" filter.

      The product darkens the icon and adds the filter to the Starred tab. The first four filters on the Starred tab appear as visible filters on any search page or search results page. You can select the visible filter and configure it as needed.

title: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Apply Filtering to Refine a Search in Docusign CLM
text:
filter to the Starred tab. The first four filters on the Starred tab appear as visible filters on any search page or search results page. You can select the visible filter and configure it as needed.

      Note: You can deselect a ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Star-Filled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.FRj1NRgJfl3SWZH5jFZD1mq8TOK80OrVyOSWADae3Ds&_LANG=enus) icon next to a filter to remove the filter from the Starred tab. You can also select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Drag-and-Move.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.FRj1NRgJfl3SWZH5jFZD1mq8TOK80OrVyOSWADae3Ds&_LANG=enus) icon to drag a filter to a new order in the list.
   4. Select Save on the All Filters dialog.

The product adds the filters and updates the search results. You finished applying filtering to your search results. You can apply more filters if needed.

title: Document Upload and Download Limits in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Document Upload and Download Limits in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about document upload and download limits in Docusign CLM. Understand these limits
when uploading and downloading documents between your computer and Docusign CLM.

Documents can be uploaded and downloaded between your computer and Docusign CLM. The
following table outlines the document upload and download limits, based on the method of
transfer.

| Method | Maximum Document Size | Maximum Number of Documents | Maximum File Size with Multiple Documents |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Drag-and-drop | 1.5 GB | 20 | 1.5 GB times the number of documents uploaded, up to the limit of space left in the account |
| Download through the user interface | 1.5 GB | 20 | 1.5 GB times the number of documents downloaded |
| Salesforce widget drag-and-drop upload | 1.5 GB | 20 | 1.5 GB times the number of documents uploaded, up to the limit of space left in the account |
| Salesforce widget upload | 1.5 GB | 20 | 1.5 GB times the number of documents uploaded, up to the limit of space left in the account |
| SFTP upload or download | Unlimited, up to the space limit in the account (pending network connection and bandwidth) | Unlimited | Unlimited, up to the space limit in the account (pending network connection and bandwidth) |
| Inbound email | Limited to the size that the inbound client will allow (typically less than 10 MB) | Limited to the number of attachments you can add to an email | Limited to the size that the inbound client will allow (typically less than 10 MB) |
| Sync upload or download | Whatever can be successfully transmitted in 2 hours | Up to 5 concurrent transfers (upload or download) | Whatever can be successfully transmitted in 2 hours |

title: Delete Documents and Folders in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Delete Documents and Folders in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to delete documents and folders in Docusign CLM.

When you no longer want documents or folders, you can
delete these items in Docusign CLM.

Note: Users can delete only the
CLM files and folders to which they have at least view, edit, and delete
access.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents view opens.
3. Select the checkbox for the document or folder that you want to delete.
4. Select File > Delete.

   A confirmation message displays.
5. Select OK.

The product moves the item to the trash. Deleted items no longer
appear in the Documents view. Deleted documents no longer appear in
search results, unless you have a filter to include the trash in your search.

title: Search Examples for Modernized Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Search Examples for Modernized Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Look up search examples for modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can review these examples to understand how the product interprets search keywords and produces search results.

This topic shows examples of searches in the modernized search interface in Docusign CLM. Each example describes how the product interprets the search keywords and describes the search results.

Note: A keyword search must contain at least four characters to return results.

| Example Search Keywords | Explanation | Search Results |
| --- | --- | --- |
| conf | The search can find all words that start with these letters. | The search finds all words starting with the letters c-o-n-f. Example results are confident, Confidential, and confidentiality, as well as conflict or confirm. |
| "force majeure event" | Enclosing the keywords in quotation marks finds the *exact* phrase. You do not want contracts that just mention "force" and "majeure" separately. Use this method for any of the following purposes:   - Locating a precisely defined specific concept - Finding section headers, clause titles, and so on - Finding a specific combination of words or terms in a specific sequence | The search finds contracts containing the exact phrase force majeure event. |
| third-party indemnification | The search can find any word from this group of words. | The search finds third, party, and indemnification (even if they do not display together). The search first looks for results with all words and then results with some or at least one of the words. Documents with more occurrences display higher in the list of results. |
| affect | The search can find the word and all stemmed versions of the word. | The search finds affect, affected, and affected parties. |

title: Document History Events in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Document History Events in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about each of the logged document history events in Docusign CLM.

Here is a list of logged Document History Actions that will display when document history
is viewed in Docusign CLM:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| - When a document is uploaded - When a document is moved - When a document is previewed - When a document is downloaded - When a document is viewed - When a document is checked out - When a document is checked in - When a description is added, changed, or removed - When a document is renamed - When attributes are added, changed, or removed - When attribute groups are added, changed, or removed - When a document is copied | - When a new document is created - When a document is shared, emailed, or sent - When a document is deleted - When a document is sent for signature - When a document signature request is signed - When a document signature request expires - When a document signature request is rejected - When a document signature request fails - When a document signature request is delegated - When a document checkout is canceled - When a document download justification occurs - When a document bookmark is added or changed | - When a document is renamed due to duplicate handling - When a document Chatter post is submitted - When a document is sent through a workflow process - When a document is sent out for fax - When a document fax submission fails - When a document fax submission succeeds - When a document reminder is scheduled, changed, deleted - When a document reminder is processed - When a document is added to or removed from the Watch List - When a document is sent out for external review - When external review expires, completes, or is canceled - When an external review request is resent | - Document History Filters - Attribute Changes - Document Activity - Document Reminders - Downloads - E-mail Activity - External Review - Fax Activity - Previews - Share Activity - Signatures - Versions - Workflows |

title: Update My Profile in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Update My Profile in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to update your profile in Docusign CLM. You can change your profile image,
name, email address, and contact information.

You can update your profile in Docusign CLM. For
example, you can change your profile image, name, email address, and contact
information.

Note: Integrations that are enabled by your
Docusign CLM administrator might lock some profile options. You see only the options
that are enabled by your Docusign CLM administrator.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the user profile icon in the top right corner, and select
   Manage Profile.

   The My Profile page opens.
3. Update your profile image:
   1. Select Update in the Profile
      Image section.
   2. Browse for a new profile image, and select
      Save.

      You have successfully updated your profile image.
4. Update your display name:
   1. Select Update in the Name
      section.
   2. Edit your name as needed, and select Save.
5. Update your email address:
   1. Select Update in the Email
      Address section.
   2. Select Continue if you see a dialog with
      information.
   3. Update your email, select Send Code, and
      complete the verification process.
   4. Select Save when you have specified your new
      email.
6. Update your contact information:
   1. Select Update in the Contact
      Information section.
   2. Makes updates to the information, and select
      Save.

   You have successfully updated your profile in Docusign CLM. The product
   applies any changes to your profile picture, name, email address, and contact
   information.

title: Annotate a Document With a Pinpoint in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Annotate a Document With a Pinpoint in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to use pinpoints to annotate documents in legacy document
preview.

Note: This documentation covers
legacy document preview experiences for Docusign CLM. As of July 1, 2025,
most customers have been upgraded to Document Review as their default document preview
experience in Docusign CLM. For more information on deprecation of
document preview experiences, see [Docusign Document Preview
Experience Changes FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ssg1729007411846&topicId=twq1729007565221.html&_LANG=enus). For more information on features and enhancements available in Document Review, see [Document Review in Docusign
CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=eyk1666363075054.html&_LANG=enus).

Users can annotate documents in Docusign CLM by using
pinpoints. This feature enables annotation on documents without changing the content of the
document itself.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents from the navigation header.

   The Documents window displays.
3. Select the document to be annotated to open it in Document Preview.
4. Select Pinpoint, and choose a color for your pinpoint from the
   dropdown menu.
5. Choose a place in the document to add your annotation.

   A comment window displays.
6. Enter your comments. Once all comments have been entered, select
   Save.

   You have successfully left a pinpoint annotation on a document. Any users that
   view the document will be able to view the annotation.

title: Save a Search Query for Reuse in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Save a Search Query for Reuse in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to save a search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can create saved
searches to save time and help with recurring search tasks.

title: Save a Search Query for Reuse in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Save a Search Query for Reuse in Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by
default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator disables
the setting.

After performing a search, you can save the search query for later use in Docusign CLM. Saving
searches can be helpful for reusing complex or common search queries. Saving
searches can also save time and help with recurring search tasks.

Note:

- Administrators can share a saved search with all users in the account;
  however, each user who executes the shared search might get different
  results, depending on permissions.
- If you saved searches before modernized search became active for the
  account, you must create those saved searches again.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.vKjs3xxZ1iTnLlB17QhZVW2UVlCSfQNnrHuVkV6GnLU&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. [Execute a search by entering search criteria
   and optional filters](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xto1707493278474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later use.").

   The product displays search results.
4. Select Save on the search results page.

   The Save Search dialog opens.
5. Define the properties for the saved search:
   1. Specify a title for the search.
   2. (Optional for administrators) Select Share this search with
      others to share the saved search with all users in the
      CLM account.
   3. Select Save.

The product saves your search. Saved searches get displayed
in a Saved section when you open a search window. You can select a saved search to
execute it. From the results page for a saved search, you can edit the search; however,
any changes must be saved as a new saved search

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about legacy search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for
documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and
export results.

In Docusign CLM, you can select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNDgsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.nY_Ez_xkm8n4mhuFkZar65ZXzzVfY9uKS33mEKCSTXQ&_LANG=enus) icon and perform a basic search of folders and documents. This icon is located in the
navigation header. You can enter criteria in the search field. The product then performs
a search based on title, description, modified by, attribute value, attribute group
names, and content of folders.

Note:

- On the **Account Settings** page, the Enable Modernized Search
  Experience setting is selected by default. The legacy search
  experience is available only when an administrator disables the setting. We
  recommend using the [modernized search
  experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.").
- In CLM and CLM+, basic search does not search the contents of documents by
  default. CLM administrators can configure basic search to include the content of
  documents. Administrators can enable this setting in Admin > Account Management > Account Settings > Search > Include the contents of documents in basic searches.
- In CLM Essentials, basic search queries search the contents of documents by
  default.

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Note: Advanced search is available in CLM and CLM+ but not in CLM
Essentials.

You can narrow search results using advanced search in Docusign CLM. Docusign recommends using advanced search to limit your search to specific criteria for best
search results. You can apply filtering to search for specific content properties or
attributes with advanced search. Users can access advanced search by selecting
Go to Advanced Search from search ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNDgsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.nY_Ez_xkm8n4mhuFkZar65ZXzzVfY9uKS33mEKCSTXQ&_LANG=enus) in the global navigation
header. For more information about what filters are available in advanced search,
see [Filters for Advanced Search in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=dtv1648752673175.html&_LANG=enus "Look up filters available in advanced search").

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
text: - A keyword search must contain at least four characters to execute.

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: OCR and Text Extraction Create Searchable Content for Uploaded Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
When you upload a document, Docusign CLM uses Optical Character Recognition (OCR) or text
extraction to make your content searchable.

OCR uses technology to identify characters in scanned documents. When you upload a
document as an image file, it is automatically processed using OCR. Results of text
search depend on the quality of the image uploaded. Handwritten text on images is
not processed using OCR.

OCR is used when the following document types are uploaded to CLM:

- .JPG
- .JPEG
- .PNG
- .TIF
- .TIFF

Note: PDF files with less than 150 text
characters, including spaces, are processed with OCR. If your PDF contains 150 text
characters or more, text extraction is used. See the text extraction section below
for more information. If you upload a scanned PDF and your scanner uses OCR, the
text will already be extracted from the PDF and will not be processed using OCR in
CLM.

Text extraction scans and extracts text from PDF and DOCX documents. When you upload
a PDF or DOCX with 150 characters or more, they are automatically processed using
text extraction. Text is not extracted from documents with more than 500 pages. If
you have a document over 500 pages, you can split the document into multiple files
to ensure your text is extracted and searchable

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
text:
- Search in Docusign CLM searches by the prefix of words, not postfix or the
  middle of words.
- By default, a search only queries the most recent version of documents. To query
  document versions, use the Search all document versions filter in advanced
  search.
- The search results page displays up to 100 results by default. Administrators
  can configure the maximum number of search results to display (100 to 1000) in Admin > Account Management > Account Settings > Search Results Display.
- Search results only display content the user performing the search has access
  to.
- Use quotation marks to execute a phrase search.
- Stop words are commonly used words such as articles, pronouns, and prepositions.
  Docusign CLM ignores the following stop words both when indexing entries for
  search and when retrieving them as a result of a search: a, an, and, are, as,
  at, be, but, by, for, if, in, into, is, it, no, not, of, on, or, such, that,
  the, their, then, there, these, they, this, to, was, will, with.

title: Legacy Search in Docusign CLM
text:
- **App:** This search will not execute. A search must contain at
  least four characters to execute successfully.
- **Appl:** All words starting with the letters *a-p-p-l.*
  Example results: **appl**e, **Appl**eton, **appl**es.
- **Apple:** The word *apple* and all stemmed versions of the
  word. Example results: **apple**s, **apple** pie, **apple** banana
  pie.
- **Apple pies:** The words *apple* and *pies,* first
  looking for results with both words and then results with one of the words.
  Example results: **Apple pies, apple, pies**
- **“Apple pies”:** The phrase “apple pies”, and results with stop words such
  as “apple and pies”, but not “apple banana pies”. Example results: **apple pie,
  apple and pie, apple** or **pies.**

title: Incremental Rollout of Docusign CLM Features to Sites
heading_path: Incremental Rollout of Docusign CLM Features to Sites
text:
Read about how Docusign releases product features incrementally to sites. This process provides a way to release product functionality efficiently on a fairly predictable schedule.

Around the world, Docusign hosts the following sites. These sites have datacenters that provide infrastructure to support the Docusign CLM application for a regional area.

- EU11 (Europe site)
- EU21 (Europe site)
- S1.EU (Europe site)
- S1.AU (Australia site)
- NA11 (North America site)
- NA21 (North America site)
- S1.US (North America site)
- S2.US (North America site)
- US11 (FedRAMP site for U.S. federal agencies)
- US12 (FedRAMP site for U.S. federal agencies)
- US13 (FedRAMP and IL4 site for U.S. federal agencies)

Docusign releases most product features incrementally to sites over a period of approximately 10 business days. Docusign typically releases a feature to sites in a specific order. The release to sites can start on the day after Production deployment. This process provides a way to release product functionality efficiently on a fairly predictable schedule. Docusign can also monitor the rollout and make adjustments to optimize the rollout process.

Note:

- Minor updates to existing features or functionality are not released by site. Instead, these items are made available to everybody by a certain date.
- The timing can vary for delivery of features to sites. Times and dates can change, depending on whether Docusign needs to pause a rollout or adjust a rollout.

title: Incremental Rollout of Docusign CLM Features to Sites
heading_path: Incremental Rollout of Docusign CLM Features to Sites
Order of Feature Rollout to Sites
text:
Over approximately 10 business days, CLM product features typically get rolled out incrementally to sites in the following order.

Note:

- This documentation describes the typical rollout schedule and site order; however, some features might follow a different rollout, based on business needs.
- To determine which site your CLM account is associated with, you can [identify the site ID for your account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jnm1716521819984.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify the site (and region) that your Docusign CLM account is associated with. Identifying the site is useful to estimate when your site might receive product features that roll out incrementally.").

1. S1.AU
2. US12
3. US11
4. US13
5. EU21, EU11, and S1.EU
6. NA21 and S2.US
7. NA11 and S1.US

**Example:** If the rollout starts on a Monday, sites could receive the feature as follows:

1. S1.AU could receive the feature on Monday (day 1)
2. US12 could receive the feature on Tuesday (day 2)
3. US11 could receive the feature on Wednesday (day 3)
4. US13 could receive the feature on Thursday (day 4)
5. EU21, EU11, and S1.EU could receive the feature on Friday (day 5)
6. NA21 and S2.US could receive the feature on Monday (day 8)
7. NA11 and S1.US could receive the feature on Wednesday (day 10)

title: Upload a Public Key to Your Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Upload a Public Key to Your Docusign CLM Account
text:
Learn how to upload a public key to your Docusign CLM account for SFTP
authentication.

You can upload a public key to your Docusign CLM account. Docusign CLM uses this public
key for SFTP authentication and transactions.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select My Profile from the user profile menu.
3. Select Privacy & Security  >  Certificates  >  Manage.

   The Certificates window displays.
4. Select Upload Public Key, and navigate to where your
   public key is stored.

   You have successfully uploaded a public key to Docusign CLM for SFTP
   authentication and transactions.

title: Profile Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to manage your profile details in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, your profile contains your user information. You can change your profile
picture, name and signature style, language and region controls, and apps connected to
your Docusign account.

By updating these options, you can customize your Docusign CLM account.

My Profile
:   Select this option to upload or change your profile picture, display name, email
    address, and contact information in Docusign CLM. For more information about
    configuring profile options, see [Update My Profile in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=sgp1659723990769.html&_LANG=enus).

Privacy & Security
:   Select this option to configure logon security, account recovery options, device
    security, and certificates for your Docusign CLM account. For more information
    about configuring privacy and security options, see [Update Your Privacy & Security Settings in
    Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=zsa1659726257051.html&_LANG=enus).

Connected Apps
:   Select this option to view all applications that are connected to your Docusign CLM account. For more information about connecting apps to Docusign, see [Connect Document Sources and Manage Other
    Connected Applications](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=qsm1578456669396.html&_LANG=enus).

Signatures
:   Select this option to add or update your name and signature styles for Docusign CLM. For
    more information about signature styles in Docusign CLM, see [To Create or Edit a
    Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=lrz1578456442739.html&_LANG=enus).

Stamps
:   Select this option to add or update your stamp styles for Docusign CLM. For more
    information about stamps in Docusign CLM, see [Manage Stamps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=dys1578456666697.html&_LANG=enus).

Language & Region
:   Select this option to configure your language and region settings for Docusign CLM. For more
    information about language and region settings, see [Update Language & Region
    Settings in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=zdt1659733232147.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Troubleshooting Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Troubleshooting Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to troubleshoot error messages for document and folder history in Docusign CLM.

You receive the message: "There are no history records matching your current filters. Please
broaden your criteria to see more history."

title: Troubleshooting Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Troubleshooting Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
No History Records for the Document or Folder
text:
This message is displayed when there are either:

- No history records for the document or folder history page you have
  selected
- Filters have been selected that return no results

To address this, you can try any of the following:

1. Check or uncheck all filter boxes available to display all available
   history records.
2. Confirm that there has been activity in the folder or on the document
   in question.
3. Verify with your Docusign CLM administrator that you have permission to
   view history records for that folder or document.
4. Review Folder History Actions to confirm that the history events are
   ones that are being tracked. For more information about Folder History
   Actions, see [Folder History Actions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=hks1576610042319.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about each of the logged folder history events in Docusign CLM.").

title: Download Documents from Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Download Documents from Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to download documents from search results in Docusign CLM. Administrators and
users can download documents for relevant business needs.

title: Download Documents from Search Results in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Download Documents from Search Results in Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected by
default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator disables
the setting.

You can download up to 25 documents from the search results page in Docusign CLM. You can
download documents in their native format. You can also download a document as a PDF
when the document is a PDF, DOC, DOCX, PPT, PPTX, XLS, or XLSX file. If you download
multiple documents, they get downloaded in a ZIP file.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMzksInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.hB4gvcVHYa7HCPJbLSBGOW53JugbkB4PCLnv_7k4ZCg&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. [Execute a search by entering search criteria
   and optional filters](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xto1707493278474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later use.").

   The product displays search results.
4. Select the checkboxes next to target documents, and perform any of the
   following actions:
   - Select Download > Document to download documents in their native format.
   - Select Download > Document as PDFs for PDF, DOC, DOCX, PPT, PPTX, XLS, or XLSX
     documents.

The product downloads the selected documents. If you download
multiples files, the files get downloaded together in a ZIP file. You are finished
downloading your documents.

title: Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Document and Folder History in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about document and folder history in Docusign CLM. Users and administrators can track the status and changes within a document.

In Docusign CLM, document and folder history provides a way to track document activity. Users and administrators can view details and changes in a centralized document repository. You can investigate document and folder history for the following purposes:

- Track document and folder history over a given length of time
- Produce audit trails and compliance reporting
- See document access within a specific scope of time
- See actions that other users have taken on a document

Note: A CLM workflow can use the Update Document Metadata Value step to update attributes for a document. When this workflow activity occurs, the document’s version history shows that the document was modified by “Workflow”. The timestamp of the modification is the workflow step’s execution time.

title: Search for Folders in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Search for Folders in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to search for folders in Docusign CLM. You can use these
steps to add a filter and search for folders.

title: Search for Folders in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Search for Folders in Docusign CLM
text:
The Enable Modernized Search Experience setting is selected
by default in account settings. You cannot perform this task if an administrator
disables the setting.

In Docusign CLM modernized search, you can search for folders. Folder
searches can be useful for exploring the contents of folders. For example, you can
find a vendor’s folder and see which documents are associated with the vendor.

Tip: After searching for folders, you can save the search query
for future reuse.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.KAa767Jmy4t2O5yTIrkwjuB0p89cg4GaScmGFYbjgOU&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. [Execute a search by entering search
   criteria](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xto1707493278474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later use.").

   The product displays search results.
4. Apply a filter to display folders in the results:
   1. Select Change above the search results.
   2. Select one of the following options:

      - Documents and Folders
      - Folders Only

      The product updates the search results.
5. (Optional) [Refine the search by entering
   search criteria and optional filters](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ops1707494327789.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to apply filters to search results in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can apply filtering to refine and narrow the results.").

   The product displays updated search results.
6. Select a folder name in the Name column to open the folder
   location.

The product opens the folder location in a separate browser window. You have completed a search for folders in Docusign CLM.

title: View Folder History in Docusign CLM
heading_path: View Folder History in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to view the history of a folder in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users use
these steps to see changes on a folder across revisions and additions.

You can see the history of a folder in Docusign CLM.
Administrators and users can see any changes that occurred on a folder across revisions and
additions.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Navigate to the folder that you want to view.
4. Select the checkbox for the folder, and select View > History.

   The folder history page opens.
5. (Optional) Perform any of the following actions to manipulate the history data:

   - Sort entries by selecting the column header for any field.
   - Filter activity records by selecting the checkboxes for action types.
6. Select Done to return to the document list.

   You have viewed the history of a selected folder.

title: Add an Attribute Field Filter to Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add an Attribute Field Filter to Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to add an attribute field as a search filter to Docusign CLM Advanced
Search.

title: Add an Attribute Field Filter to Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add an Attribute Field Filter to Advanced Search in Docusign CLM
text:
This task applies to the legacy search experience. By
default, the [modernized search experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus "Read about modernized search in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can search for documents and content. You can apply filtering, save searches, customize search results, and export results.") is
enabled in account settings. We recommend using modernized search.

Note: Advanced search is available in CLM and CLM+ but not in CLM
Essentials.

You can add attribute fields as search filters in Docusign CLM advanced search.
Attributes you add as a search filter are only displayed in your CLM instance.

1. Navigate to advanced search:
   1. Select Search
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.DqiERwZS5KwuZt7tEplZKFacx-Dn-SdHAsjMZE0tp9c&_LANG=enus) from the
      global navigation header.
   2. Select Go to Advanced Search.

   The Search Results page displays.
2. Select Add Attribute Field from the
   Filters tab on the side panel.

   The Select Attribute Fields dialog box
   displays.
3. Select an attribute to add as a search filter.
4. Select Apply.

The attribute field is added to the
Filters tab, and you can search using the attribute.

title: Introduction to Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about Docusign CLM and how it consolidates every step of the contract process in
one secure location.

Docusign CLM is a contract lifecycle management tool that consolidates every step of the
contract process in one secure location. CLM also automates numerous tasks within these
steps, ultimately reducing risk and time to completion.

Docusign CLM Administration
:   Administrators are responsible for account and user management in Docusign CLM.
    Administrators also maintain product integrations and, in many cases, automated
    workflows and reporting. For more information about administrative features in
    Docusign CLM, see [Docusign CLM
    Admin Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=jru1584137309649.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign CLM for Users
:   In Docusign CLM, users manage documents through collaboration and tasks. Users can edit
    documents, initiate electronic signature, and send documents for external
    review. For more information about document management in Docusign CLM, see
    [Document Management in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=kwa1659209252162.html&_LANG=enus "Read about document management in Docusign CLM. Users with access to a document can perform actions to manage the document.").

Document Generation in Docusign CLM
:   Document Generation is the primary tool for generating new documents or uploading existing
    documents to launch new workflows in Docusign CLM. For more information about
    Document Generation, see [Docusign CLM
    Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=gjv1576610127258.html&_LANG=enus).

Workflows in Docusign CLM
:   In Docusign CLM, you can configure workflows to execute repeatable business processes. You
    can assemble and connect workflow steps that help automate manual tasks and
    manage complex processes. Adding automation to your business processes helps
    eliminate unnecessary risk and reduce errors. For more information about
    workflows in Docusign CLM, see [Docusign CLM
    Workflows Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=uxf1651607363522.html&_LANG=enus).

Reporting in Docusign CLM
:   Reporting in Docusign CLM lets you gather and analyze data from documents, workflows, and
    user actions. Reports can also be visualized or presented through charts by
    adding them as widgets in the Docusign CLM Dashboard. For more information about
    reporting in Docusign CLM, see [Reporting in
    Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lye1642705772274&topicId=ylb1584137317114.html&_LANG=enus).

Smart Rules in Docusign CLM
:   Smart rules in Docusign CLM automate actions on a folder or document based on events in that
    folder or with that document. For more information about smart rules, see [Smart Rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qaz1642962729191&topicId=yhx1576610044302.html&_LANG=enus).

Party Management in Docusign CLM
:   Party management in Docusign CLM allows users to view and upload contracts,
    capture data, and manage agreements per party. For more information about party
    management, see [Docusign CLM
    Party Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=mjn1643378694283&topicId=kkx1602086130522.html&_LANG=enus).

Connectors and Integrations for Docusign CLM
:   Docusign CLM connectors allow you to share data between Docusign CLM and external systems.
    Save time through increased efficiency and avoid errors and risks associated
    with manual data entry. For more information about Docusign Connectors and
    Integrations for Docusign CLM, see [Docusign CLM Connectors for External
    Systems](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=qxq1617226904331.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Document Actions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the actions that can be taken on documents in Docusign CLM's document
library. This includes actions that enable moving, uploading, and deleting
documents.

Docusign CLM includes a suite of tools that enable document management. Users can take
advantage of several methods for uploading, moving, copying, and deleting documents.

Document management in Docusign CLM also enables users to download documents, or send and
share documents for review.

title: Use Microsoft 365 for the Web to Edit Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Use Microsoft 365 for the Web to Edit Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to make edits and changes to a document in Docusign CLM using Microsoft 365 for the Web.

Users with licenses of Microsoft 365 for the Web can use it to edit Docusign CLM documents. This enables users to make changes to documents without having to
leave their web browser. Changes to documents are uploaded as new versions of that
document.

Note:

- Salesforce users can also use this integration to make changes to
  documents. For more information about this integration in Salesforce, see [In-Browser Editing With Office
  365](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qob1643667997351&topicId=atn1576609990761.html&_LANG=enus).
- To use this feature, a Docusign CLM Administrator must enable the Docusign CLM Microsoft Office 365 integration. For more information about how to
  enable this integration, see [Enable the Microsoft 365 for the Web Connector in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ykd1651766025252.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the Microsoft Office 365 integration for Docusign CLM. Administrators can use these steps to allow Docusign CLM users to checkout, edit, and co-author documents online with Office 365.").
- This guide covers editing documents from Docusign CLM in Microsoft 365 for the Web. For information on editing documents using the App Center installation of Microsoft 365, see [Open a Document from Docusign CLM in Microsoft 365 (App Center)﻿](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=tsk8f6633b3-3e38-4439-91db-52314298e2b4.html&_LANG=enus).
- You must have an active license of Microsoft Office Online to use this
  feature.
- This feature only works with Microsoft Word documents (DOCX).

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents from the navigation header.

   The folder list displays.
3. Select a document.

   The document displays in Document Explorer.
4. Select Edit  >  Open With Microsoft 365 for the Web.

   The document opens in Microsoft 365 for the Web.
5. Sign in to Microsoft Office Online with your company credentials, if
   prompted.
6. Edit the selected document.
7. Once all changes have been made, select File  >  Save to save your work.

   You have made changes to a Docusign CLM document using Microsoft 365 for the Web. The document will be saved as a new version of the original
   document.

title: Search for Documents and Content in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Search for Documents and Content in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to use a modernized search to find documents and content in Docusign CLM.
Administrators and users can also apply filters or save a search query for later
use.

You can search for documents and content in Docusign CLM. The modernized search interface
provides an efficient, flexible, and personalized search experience.

Note: Modernized search is not available for CLM.CM account types.
This search experience is available only for Docusign CLM accounts. On the
Account Settings page, the Enable Modernized
Search Experience setting is selected by default.

You can also apply filters to search for specific content properties or attributes.
For example, you can filter results to show only documents that were created in the
past seven days.

Tip:

- Users and administrators can save a search query for later use. Administrators can share
  the saved search with all users; however, each user who executes the shared
  search might get different results, depending on permissions.
- To search for cascading attribute values, you can select the values from
  drop-down filters. The value must be an exact match to a value in the
  corresponding input file.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.kxsBM8E2L8ic8ajXtqyTJcRgUUFQgOYOPAf9gG2N2QA&_LANG=enus) icon
   in the navigation header.

   The product opens a search window. The window also shows any recent search queries,
   recent documents, and saved searches.
3. Type at least four characters in the search text box, and press
   Enter.

   Tip: You can use quotation marks to search for
   an exact phrase. For example, specify “ACME MSA FY25”
   to find a contract with that specific name.

   The product displays search results.

   Tip: If you
   have the Location column displayed, this column is an active link in search
   results. You can select the Location link to open a new browser window
   showing the folder location.
4. (Optional) Select All Filters, and [apply filtering to refine your
   result](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=ops1707494327789.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to apply filters to search results in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can apply filtering to refine and narrow the results.")s.

   The product executes the search again and displays updated
   results.
5. (Optional) Select Save to retain this search for future
   use.

You have completed your search in Docusign CLM. If results get
split across pages, you can use the page navigator to view more pages of results. To
return to the product interface, select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/fsk1642969066834/page/images/Arrow-Left.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTgzMjIsInNoZWFmIjoiZnNrMTY0Mjk2OTA2NjgzNCJ9.kxsBM8E2L8ic8ajXtqyTJcRgUUFQgOYOPAf9gG2N2QA&_LANG=enus) icon in the top left corner.

title: Search for Documents and Content in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Search for Documents and Content in Docusign CLM
text:
**Search for a Customer’s Contract to Find a Reusable
Clause**Alex is a contract manager at Fontara. He needs to create a new contract with
existing customer Nesis Biotech. Alex needs to find an existing Nesis Biotech contract
and reuse one of its clauses. Alex filters the search to show only active contracts for
the Nesis Biotech party.He finds the target contract and opens it directly from
the search results page. He finds the product warranty clause and copies the content.
Alex reuses the clause in his draft of the new services agreement contract.

title: Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type
heading_path: Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type
text:
Learn how to identify your account type in Docusign CLM.

When you log on to Docusign CLM, you can identify your
account type. There are two account types:

- CLM.DS
- CLM.CM

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the profile icon.

   Your name and account type display.
3. Identify your account type:

   CLM
   :   Indicates that you are using CLM that authenticates through
       Docusign. Also known as CLM.DS.

   CLM.CM
   :   Indicates that you are using CLM that authenticates through
       SpringCM. Also known as CLM.CM.

   You have successfully identified your CLM account type.

title: Reset Your Password in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Reset Your Password in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to reset your password in Docusign CLM.

Learn how to reset your password in Docusign CLM.

Note:

- This topic covers password reset instructions for both Docusign CLM (CLM.DS) and
  SpringCM (CLM.CM) account types. To see what CLM account type your have, see [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify your account type in Docusign CLM.").
- To learn more about resetting a user password as a CLM administrator, see [Reset User Password](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lyr1583277364866.html&_LANG=enus).

1. To reset your password for Docusign CLM (CLM.DS):
   1. Log on to Docusign CLM (CLM.DS).
   2. Select My Profile from the user profile menu.
   3. Select Privacy & Security.
   4. Select Default Security  >  Change Password.

      The password change screen displays.
   5. Enter a new password that follows these rules:

      - New password must be at least 6 characters.
      - New password cannot contain spaces, or the following characters: < > ,
      - New password must be different from your last 12 passwords.
   6. (Optional) Select Add Question to add questions for password
      recovery.
   7. Select Save.

      You have successfully changed your password for Docusign CLM
      (CLM.DS).
2. To reset your password for SpringCM (CLM.CM):
   1. Log on to SpringCM (CLM.CM)
   2. Select My Preferences from the user profile menu.
   3. Choose Reset Password, and then select Reset
      Password.

      You will receive an email to verify the password reset request.
   4. Once you receive the password reset email, select the link titled Reset
      Password.

      A webpage displays for resetting your SpringCM (CLM.CM)
      password.
   5. Enter and confirm a new password. Once a password has been entered, select
      Confirm.

      You have successfully changed your password for SpringCM
      (CLM.CM).

title: Access Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to log on and log off of Docusign CLM.

To access all of the features and documents in
Docusign CLM, users must log in using their Docusign credentials. Once you have
completed your tasks in Docusign CLM, you should securely log out through your user
profile. This ensures that none of your account information can be accessed by outside
parties.

Note:

- To see what CLM account type you have, see [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to identify your account type in Docusign CLM.").
- To access Docusign CLM from the app switcher, users must have a Docusign CLM
  account. Contact you Docusign CLM administrator if CLM does not display in
  the app switcher.

1. To log on to CLM.DS:
   1. In a web browser, navigate to [https://clm.docusign.com](https://clm.docusign.com/).

      Note: To log on to a Docusign CLM UAT or test
      environment, navigate to [https://clmdemo.docusign.com](https://clmdemo.docusign.com/).
   2. Enter your Docusign CLM email address, and select
      Next.
   3. Enter your Docusign CLM password, and select Log
      In.

      The Docusign CLM home page displays. You have successfully
      accessed your Docusign CLM account.
2. To log on to CLM.CM:
   1. In a web browser, navigate to [https://login.springcm.com](https://login.springcm.com/).

      Note: To log on to a CLM.CM UAT or test
      environment, navigate to [https://loginuat.springcm.com](https://loginuat.springcm.com/).
   2. Enter your CLM.CM credentials, and select Log
      In.

      The CLM.CM home page displays. You have successfully accessed
      your CLM.CM account.

title: Manage Language and Region Settings in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Manage Language and Region Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to manage language and region settings in Docusign CLM. You can update the
sending and signing language for documents. You can also update the regional settings for
time zone, date, address, and more.

Users can update their language and region settings in
Docusign CLM. You can update the sending and signing language for documents. You can
also update the regional settings for time zone, name, date, address, and
more.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the user profile icon in the top right corner, and select
   Manage Profile.

   The My Profile page opens.
3. Select Language & Region.
4. Update the sending and signing language for documents:
   1. Select Update in the
      Language section.
   2. Select the languages for sending and signing documents, and select
      Save.
5. Update the region settings:
   1. Select Update in the Regional
      Settings section.
   2. Update settings and format for region, time zone, date and time, name
      display, intials, and address.
   3. Select Save.

You have successfully updated your language and region
settings in Docusign CLM.

title: Update Your Privacy and Security Settings in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Update Your Privacy and Security Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to update your privacy and security settings in Docusign CLM. These settings
provide control over authentication, passwords,recovery methods, and more.

Users can view and update their settings for privacy
and security in Docusign CLM. This includes account recovery options, passwords, account
verification, security certificates, and login activity.

Note: Integrations that are enabled by your Docusign CLM administrator might lock some
profile options. You see only options that are enabled by your Docusign CLM
administrator.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the user profile icon in the top right corner, and select
   Manage Profile.

   The My Profile page opens.
3. Select Privacy & Security.
4. Manage your privacy and security settings as needed:

   Password
   :   Lets you update your Docusign CLM password and password questions. Select
       Change Password and then update your
       password and password questions, if necessary. You can then save
       your changes.

       Note: For more information about
       resetting your Docusign CLM password, see [Reset Your Password
       in Docusign CLM.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=yax1576795525466.html&_LANG=enus)

   Passwordless Login
   :   Provides a toggle to enable or disable a passwordless login experience. To enable
       passwordless login, you must add a passkey or physical security key.
       If this experience is enabled, you can log in by using a passkey or
       a physical security key (with PIN) instead of a password.

       Note: For more information about login security
       options, see [Set Up Passwordless Login for Your
       Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=dzo1651694930392.html&_LANG=enus) and [Two-Step
       Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=txt1578456659215.html&_LANG=enus).

   New Device Verification
   :   Provides a toggle to enable or disable the requirement to have an
       additional verification step for logins from a new device or
       browser.

   Two-Step Verification
   :   Provides a toggle to enable or disable the requirement to have a verification code,
       security key, or biometrics when logging in with a password.

   Verification Methods
   :   Lets you set up account recovery options through email, phone
       number, external authenticator apps, security keys, and
       biometrics.

   App Passwords
   :   Lets you set up an additional Docusign-generated app password for integrated apps. Select
       Generate App Password, and then add a
       custom name. You can then generate the app password.

   Recent Activity
   :   Lets you view your recent login activity. Select View to view your
       recent activity.

   Certificates
   :   Lets you set up public keys for SFTP interactions. Select Manage
       and then upload a public key.

       Note: For more
       information about uploading a public key to your Docusign CLM
       account, see [Upload a Public Key to Your Docusign CLM Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=uaw1659728754243.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload a public key to your Docusign CLM account for SFTP authentication.").

You have successfully updated security settings for your Docusign CLM account.

title: Configure Your Task Preferences in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure Your Task Preferences in Docusign CLM
text:
You can configure your task preferences in Docusign CLM. You can instruct the product
to return to your inbox after you complete a task in Docusign CLM.

You can configure your task preferences in your Docusign CLM account. You can instruct the
product to return to your inbox after you complete a task in Docusign CLM.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select the user profile icon in the top right corner, and select My
   Preferences.
3. Configure your task preferences:
   1. Select the Return to your inbox after completing a
      task checkbox in the Tasks
      section.
   2. Select Save.

You have configured your task preferences. Selecting the
checkbox instructs the product to return to your inbox after you complete a task in
CLM.

title: Enable Task Completion Screens in Docusign CLM or CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable Task Completion Screens in Docusign CLM or CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable task completion screens in Docusign CLM.

Note: This documentation covers legacy document preview experiences for Docusign CLM. As of July 1, 2025, most customers have been upgraded to Document Review as their default document preview experience in Docusign CLM. For more information on deprecation of document preview experiences, see [Docusign Document Preview Experience Changes FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ssg1729007411846&topicId=twq1729007565221.html&_LANG=enus) For more information on features and enhancements available in Document Review, see [Document Review in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=eyk1666363075054.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign CLM administrators can enable the task completion screens for users. These screens present a more modern layout.

1. Log in to the product as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Select the Enable task completion screens checkbox in the Workflow & Tasks section.
5. Select Save.

You have enabled the task completion screens for your users.

title: Build a Scheduled Rule in Docusign CLM to Automate Sending Documents to the Trash
heading_path: Build a Scheduled Rule in Docusign CLM to Automate Sending Documents to the Trash
text:
Learn how to build a scheduled smart rule in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build this
rule to move files to the trash folder on a schedule.

title: Build a Scheduled Rule in Docusign CLM to Automate Sending Documents to the Trash
heading_path: Build a Scheduled Rule in Docusign CLM to Automate Sending Documents to the Trash
text:
[Build a schedule to trigger a
scheduled rule for trash removal](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=kjv1636412077513.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a schedule in Docusign CLM to help with trash removal. Administrators can use the schedule to trigger a rule that moves canceled contracts to the trash.").

After building a schedule, you can build a rule to move
canceled contracts to the trash on a schedule. This process provides automation to help
you remove unwanted documents and optimize account storage.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select  Smart Rules under Automation Tools.

   The Smart Rules page opens.
4. Select Rule > New Scheduled Rule.

   The Edit Scheduled Rule page opens.
5. Create a scheduled rule:

   1. Complete the following fields to define the rule:

      Name
      :   Specifies a descriptive name for the rule.

      Execute as
      :   Selects one of the following options for executing the
          rule:
          - your\_user\_name
            executes the rule by using your security
            rights.
          - Specific User executes the rule by using the security rights
            of the administrator whose name or email address you
            specify.

            Note: We recommend
            creating a generic CLM administrator to use for
            automation. This way, your automation processes
            are not tied to users who might leave or change
            roles.

      Action
      :   Select Move to Trash from the
          drop-down menu.

      Number of Days Old Without an Update
      :   Specifies a number of days.

      Select Folder
      :   Adds a folder or directory to which the rule applies. You
          must select this link, select the checkbox next to a folder
          or directory, and select the Select
          button.
   2. Select Save.

   A message indicates that the rule was saved successfully. The
   Smart Rules page opens, showing the rule with a
   disabled status.
6. Select the checkbox next to the rule, and select Rule > Enable.

   The status changes to Enabled.

You have finished the process of creating a schedule and scheduled
rule.

Files that match your parameters get automatically moved to the trash folder.
