When the trash folder gets emptied, these files get permanently removed from all
Docusign servers. They no longer add to your account storage limits.

title: Customize Document Types in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create alternate names and remove visibility for document types in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can create alternate names for document types. When an
alternate name is assigned to a document type, the new name is displayed anywhere a
document’s Type is visible.

Users will be able to select the new document type when uploading documents. Customized
document types can also be selected when viewing documents in Document Review.
Documents that already exist in Docusign CLM will display the new
document type.

Note:

- Party management must be enabled to use custom document types. To enable party management,
  see [Enable or Disable Party
  Management in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=mjn1643378694283&topicId=uva1603410757890.html&_LANG=enus).
- Changing a document type’s name will not change the document’s
  classification. For more information about document classes, see [Document Types
  in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=pls1666892322322.html&_LANG=enus).
- If a document type has an alternate name assigned, all existing documents of that type
  display the alternate name.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Document Types under Documents &
   Folders.

   The Document Types page opens.
4. Select Edit.

   The Document Types page displays fields for
   Alternate Name and Visibility for each document type.
5. Choose a document type, and enter an alternate name.
6. Select Save, or select Cancel to
   abandon all changes.

   You have assigned an alternate name to a document type in Docusign
   CLM. Users can select the customized document type when uploading documents or
   making changes in Document Review.

title: Disabling or Enabling Password Logon for SSO Users
heading_path: Disabling or Enabling Password Logon for SSO Users
text:
Read about how Docusign CLM administrators can enable or disable
password logon when SSO is configured.

If single sign-on (SSO) is set up for your organization, administrators can disable (or
enable) password logon for a select SSO user or for all SSO users. See [Docusign Single Sign-On Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=njp1583359138365.html&_LANG=enus) for more information.

In Docusign CLM.CM, Super Administrators or User Administrators can set the account defaults,
while only Super Administrators can disable or enable a specific SSO user’s ability to log on
by using a password. When a User Administrator creates a new user, the user receives the
account password defaults. For CLM.CM account types, see [Single Sign-On
in Docusign CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=mma1584137313140.html&_LANG=enus).

title: View Unassigned Tasks and Assign Them in Docusign CLM
heading_path: View Unassigned Tasks and Assign Them in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to view and make assignments for unassigned tasks in Docusign CLM. Task group members can access an Unassigned view to see
unassigned tasks, claim tasks, and assign tasks.

title: View Unassigned Tasks and Assign Them in Docusign CLM
heading_path: View Unassigned Tasks and Assign Them in Docusign CLM
text:
Select Enable updated task management page
on the Account Settings page.

In Docusign CLM, task group members can access an
Unassigned view on the Tasks page. In
this view, you quickly see how many unassigned tasks exist for your task groups. You
can also claim tasks or assign tasks in the view.

Note:

- If you are not a member of a task group, you do not see the
  Unassigned view.
- If no unassigned tasks exist, you do not see the Unassigned view.
- You cannot manage the assignment of a task that was created by adding a
  comment to a document. For this type of task, you must select the task item
  to manage the comment in the document.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The Tasks page opens.
3. Select Select Task View > Unassigned.

   Tip: The view name is followed by a number, which indicates
   the number of unassigned tasks for the task group.

   The Unassigned view opens.
4. (Optional) Manage tasks as needed:
   1. Select one or more tasks.
   2. Perform any of the following actions:
   - Select Claim to assign a task to yourself.
   - Select Assign, and choose another member of your task group
     to assign a task to.

You are finished viewing and making assignments for unassigned tasks.

title: Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Read about the SFTP self-service for CLM.CM. Super administrators can use SFTP for
moving documents or folders between a local data source and CLM.CM.

Important:

- SFTP for Docusign CLM.CM is self-service.
- Docusign does not provide or recommend SFTP client applications.
- This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. For more information about SFTP for Docusign CLM,
  see [Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=lam1619202914294.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to connect an SFTP client to Docusign CLM for secure uploads and backups."). To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account
  you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
  Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

Secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) enables movement of documents or folders between a local
data source and Docusign CLM. You can use SFTP for uploading and downloading within Docusign CLM, or to move documents across different folders. SFTP is used to migrate documents to and
from Docusign CLM for batches of files between 30MB and 1GB in size. To learn more about
limitations for SFTP in Docusign CLM.CM, see [SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=osg1576609938920.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the limitations of SFTP for Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM.").

Folder structure hierarchy is maintained when using SFTP. When moving parent folders with
child sub-folders, folder structure will be kept intact through the move. SFTP should not be
used to delete files or folders. When deleting documents using SFTP, files will automatically
be disassociated from the origin folder. This is a feature of SFTP clients, not Docusign CLM.CM. If you delete a folder with many files, you will see the folder separately from the
files in the trash folder.

Your username in Docusign CLM.CM must be unique among all accounts when using SFTP. If your
username is used for multiple logins in Docusign CLM.CM, you must either change your username
or use a different email address.

To use SFTP with Docusign CLM.CM, you will need to install an SFTP client. Please consult
with your IT administrator to choose an SFTP client that is compliant with your
organization.

Note: There are known issues with WinSCP's SFTP client, specifically the
“resumable file upload” feature. Using this feature may result in fragmented or duplicate
uploads, or loss of data. Docusign is not responsible for issues that may occur when uploading
files with this feature enabled.

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about each of the account settings available in Docusign
CLM.

Administrators can configure Docusign CLM account settings by selecting
Admin from the navigation header. Docusign CLM
administrators can configure options and settings in each of these sections.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM. To determine
which kind of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
- Document Generation: enables users to generate documents based on
  templates. For more information about Document Generation, see [Document Generation in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=gjv1576610127258.html&_LANG=enus).
- eForms: allows for creation of web-based forms for document and
  data submission.
- Reminder Templates: allows creation of pre-defined document
  reminder criteria, based on document name, recipients, and other categories.
- Schedules: enables setup of scheduled rules for document
  management.
- Smart Rules: allows for setup of automated actions on folders or
  documents based on events that occur in folders or documents. For more information about
  Smart Rules, see [Smart Rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qaz1642962729191&topicId=yhx1576610044302.html&_LANG=enus).
- Workflows: allows for automation of complex processes, including
  document generation and review. For more information about workflows, see the
  documentation for [Docusign CLM Workflows Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=uxf1651607363522.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
- Attributes: metadata tags and groups that can be applied to a
  document, enabling categorization and easier search. For more information about document
  attributes, see [Document Attribute Groups in Docusign
  CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=rxl1576795529933.html&_LANG=enus).
- Attribute Import Mappings: create an XML or CSV spreadsheet map
  for importing documents and their related attributes. For more information about attribute
  import mapping, see [Create an
  Attribute Import Mapping in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=ioi1576795526609.html&_LANG=enus).
- Auto Numbers: used to automatically assign a unique identifier to
  a document at upload or ingestion into Docusign CLM. For more
  information about auto number attributes, see [Auto Number Attributes in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=xbk1576610116403.html&_LANG=enus).
- Document & Folder Settings: used to configure settings for
  user interactions with documents and folders.
- Document Types: used to change name and visibility of document
  types in Docusign CLM. For more information about document types, see
  [Document Classification in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ycb1666892024625.html&_LANG=enus).
- Document Access: enables administrators to grant or revoke user
  group access to specified document types.
- Inbound Email Addresses: enables setup of an inbound email
  connection for emailing documents directly to Docusign CLM.

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
- Users: view and manage all user profiles in Docusign CLM.
- Groups: view and manage all user groups in Docusign CLM.
- Task Groups: view and manage all task groups in Docusign CLM.
- Delegated Permissions: enables creation of custom permission
  profiles for users in Docusign CLM.

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
- Account Settings: general settings for your Docusign CLM account, including language, region settings, and feature
  enablement.
- Branding: customization options for Docusign
  CLM, including logos and colors.
- Deployment: create and configure deployment packages for your
  account.
- Navigation & Actions: customize the navigation header and
  actions menu for Docusign CLM users.
- Security Settings: setup security options, such as
  authentication, timeout length, two-factor authentication, and sharing settings.

title: Account Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
- System Domains: set resource URLs for connecting or
  authenticating with third-party integrated applications.
- Manage Domains: add or remove domains for access to Docusign CLM.
- Connections: enables administrators to add third-party apps to
  Docusign CLM.
- **Docusign Analyzer**: enables administrators to connect their Docusign CLM instance to Docusign Analyzer.

title: Disable Two-Factor Authentication in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to disable two-factor authentication in CLM.CM.

In CLM.CM, super administrators can disable two-factor
authentication for all users.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM.
To determine which kind of CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a super administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Security Settings under Account Management.

   The Security Settings page opens.
4. Select No in the Enable 2-Step
   Verification section.
5. Select Save.

You have successfully disabled two-factor authentication for
CLM.CM.

title: Edit a User in a CLM.CM Account
heading_path: Edit a User in a CLM.CM Account
text:
Learn how to edit a user in a CLM.CM account. For example, administrators can change
the permission profile, password settings, or profile information.

You can edit a user in CLM.CM. For example, you can
change the assigned permission profile, change password settings, or change profile
information.

1. Log in to CLM.CM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Users under User Management.

   The Users page opens.
4. Select the box next to a user, and select Edit.

   The user profile page opens.
5. Make changes as necessary, and select Save.

The product applies the changes to the user.

title: Reassign a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Reassign a Task in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to reassign a task in Docusign CLM.

title: Reassign a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Reassign a Task in Docusign CLM
text:
- Select Enable updated task management page on the
  Account Settings page. The updated page has a more modern
  layout.
- To perform this activity on tasks for other task group members, users must have the
  proper permissions. For more information, see the documentation about [user permissions that administrators can assign to
  task group members](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=rij1684257629881.html&_LANG=enus).

You can reassign tasks in Docusign CLM. Task reassignment helps you balance workloads or
make changes to incorrect assignments.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens.
3. Select a task view.
4. Select the checkbox next to the task, and select Reassign.

   The Choose users dialog box displays.
5. Select a user or task group from the list, and choose
   Select.

   Tip: You can search for users or groups in the search
   field.

The product reassigns the task. The task moves from the original
user's tasks to the new assignee's tasks.

title: Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about delegated permissions in Docusign CLM. Administrators can assign
permissions to users in Docusign CLM (or full subscribers in CLM.CM). Users with delegated
permissions have access to assigned administrative functions.

Administrators can assign limited administrative permissions to CLM users in
Docusign CLM. Users with delegated permissions have access to specific assigned
administrative functions.

Delegated permissions give your organization flexibility when managing work in
CLM. Permissions are grouped into administrative areas based on function. This can help
you assign roles within CLM. For example, you can enable functionality for users on
tasks like customized reports.

Note:

- In Docusign CLM, administrators can assign delegated permissions only to users
  with the CLM User permission profile.
- In CLM.CM, super administrators can assign delegated permissions only to users
  with the Full Subscriber permission profile.
- To determine the type of Docusign CLM account you have, see
  [Identify Your Docusign CLM
  Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create a Group in a CLM.CM Account
heading_path: Create a Group in a CLM.CM Account
text:
Learn how to create a group in a CLM.CM account. Administrators can specify access
through groups to help simplify the security access process.

In CLM.CM, super administrators can create or modify groups. Specifying access through
groups can help simplify security access. This procedure describes how to create a
group.

Note: You cannot add contacts to groups.
Contacts are not allowed in a group.

1. Log in to CLM.CM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Groups under User
   Management.

   The Groups page opens.
4. Select New Group.

   The Create a New Group page opens.
5. Enter a name in the Group Name field.
6. Select Add Group Members.

   The Add Group Members window opens.
7. Select each member:
   1. (Optional) Select filter options to narrow down the roles.
   2. Type the name of a user or group in the Search field, and
      select an entry.

      Note: Selecting a group adds all users from that group to your
      new group.

      The product selects the member for the group.
8. Select Apply.

   The product adds the selected members to the group.
9. Select Save.

   The product creates the group.

title: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Read about the types of administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM.

Docusign CLM administration is divided between two personas:

- Organization administrators
- CLM administrators

title: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Org administrator CLM permission profiles are automatically created during account
provisioning. Org administrators perform the following CLM setup activities:

- Account management

  Examples include creating an account; adding or assigning a CLM Admin user; or enabling the
  CLM application for the CLM administrator.

- User management in a CLM account

  Examples include adding new users; enabling the CLM
  application for users; or adding the CLM User (or CLM Viewer) permission
  profile.
- Group management in a CLM account

  Examples include creating new groups in Docusign Admin;
  adding users to groups; or editing existing groups.

title: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM
text:
The following type of administrator exists in Docusign CLM:

CLM Admin
:   Acts as an administrator of the CLM account. This profile is not the same profile as an
    organization administrator but can be the same user. the account must have
    at least one CLM Admin persona.

title: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Types of Administrators in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
The following types of administrators exist in CLM.CM:

User Administrator
:   Acts as a delegate role for some administrative tasks. User administrators can manage
    users, manage mailing lists, and administer some account-level preferences.
    User administrators *cannot* create or edit security groups. An account
    can have multiple user administrators.

Super Administrator
:   Acts as the highest-level administrator in your account. Super administrators can access
    everything in your Docusign CLM repository, including all users' home
    folders. Use this role for designating a repository administrator. An
    account can have multiple super administrators.

title: Permanently Remove All CLM Content from the Trash Folder
heading_path: Permanently Remove All CLM Content from the Trash Folder
text:
Learn how to delete CLM content from the trash folder. Administrators can permanently
remove content to free storage in the CLM account or serve other needs.

Administrators can delete content from the trash folder in Docusign CLM. This is useful when
you want to free storage in your CLM account.

Important: Data that gets deleted from the
trash folder gets removed from all Docusign datacenters and can never be
restored.

Use this procedure to permanently delete the contents of your trash folder.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select the Trash folder in the folder list in the
   Documents view.

   The Trash page opens, showing all deleted
   content.
3. Select File > Empty Trash.

   A dialog box opens where you must confirm the deletion.
4. Type DELETE in the blank text field, and select
   OK.

   ![Enter the word DELETE](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Enter-the-word-DELETE.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1OTEsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.BaMcQGynTwXq19HvTsodLOjSMmPPopHpO8vTlnIMZFE&_LANG=enus "Enter the word DELETE")

   The dialog box closes. A message indicates that the product successfully
   emptied the trash folder.

You have permanently deleted the contents of the trash folder. All
data from the trash folder is removed from all Docusign data centers. This data can not
be restored.

title: Enable or Disable Unified Task Management in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable or Disable Unified Task Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to enable or disable unified task management in Docusign
CLM. Administrators enable this experience to give users one view to manage tasks across Docusign applications.

CLM administrators can enable or disable unified task management for their CLM accounts. This
task experience gives users one central view to manage tasks across Docusign applications.

When unified task management is active, selecting Tasks in the
navigation header opens the unified task view.

Note: If unified task management is active, the following items are removed
from the account:

- “Set the value for Return to Inbox flag for all users in the account”
  account setting
- “Return to your inbox after completing a task” account preference
- “Set Homepage” checkbox for the “Tasks page” listing on the
  Navigation and Actions page

Use this procedure to enable or disable unified task management for a CLM account.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Perform one of the following actions on the Enable
   unified task management page setting:
   - Select the checkbox to turn on unified task management.
   - Clear the checkbox to turn off unified task management.
5. Select Save.

You are finished managing unified task management for the CLM
account.

title: Create and Enable an eForm in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create and Enable an eForm in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create and enable an eForm in Docusign CLM.

Administrators and Super Administrators can create
eForms that are available to Docusign CLM users.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select eForms under Automation Tools.
4. Select Create to create a new eForm.

   The eForm creation page displays.
5. Provide a name for your eForm in the Name field.
6. Choose attribute fields to use in the eForm:
   1. Select the checkbox for Show Attribute
      Groups.
   2. Select the + next to the attribute group to
      expand it, and select your desired attribute fields.
7. Once all attribute groups have been added, select Generate Sample
   Form.

   HTML for your eForm is displayed in the Form
   HTML: field.
8. (Optional) Select Append Attachment Control to allow
   attachments to be submitted with your eForm.
9. (Optional) Select Append Selected Fields to add
   additional attribute fields.
10. Select Preview to view a sample of the form.
11. Once all options have been selected, select Save.

    The eForm is saved, and the eForms screen displays.
12. Enable the eForm for submission:
    1. Select the checkbox for the eForm you want to enable for
       submission.
    2. Select Allow Submission.

       The eForm is updated with a status of May
       Submit.
13. Choose a folder to store completed eForms:
    1. Select the checkbox for the eForm you want to enable for
       submission.
    2. Select Set Submit Folder.

       The folder selection window displays.
    3. Navigate to your desired folder.
    4. Select the checkbox for your desired submission folder, and then select
       Select.

    The eForms page now shows an assigned submit folder. You have
    successfully created an eForm and enabled it for submission.

title: Add the CLM Admin Permission Profile to an Existing User
heading_path: Add the CLM Admin Permission Profile to an Existing User
text:
Learn how to add a CLM Admin permission profile to an existing user. Administrators add
this profile to give the user administrative rights for Docusign CLM.

You must have at least one CLM administrator in the organization. An administrator can add a
CLM Admin permission profile to an existing user. Adding this profile gives the user
administrative rights for Docusign CLM.

Note: To add this profile, Docusign CLM must be turned on in the
organization.

1. Access Docusign Admin.
2. Locate the user, and open the user's profile.

   The default view is the Memberships
   tab.
3. Select Actions > Edit for the account where you want this user to be an
   administrator.

   The Edit Account Membership page opens. If a CLM
   account is provisioned for the organization, a CLM tile displays in the list of
   applications.
4. Enable administrative permission for CLM:
   1. Move the toggle to Enabled in the CLM
      section.
   2. Select CLM Admin from the Permission
      Profile drop-down list.
5. Select Save.

The product saves your changes. You have given the user
administrative rights for Docusign CLM.

title: Add Custom Clauses for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add Custom Clauses for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to use a custom clause library for document generation in Docusign CLM.
Administrators can add clauses to a document generation form to use preapproved
content.

After an administrator creates a library containing
custom clauses, you can use clauses in document generation forms in Docusign CLM. Adding
clauses to a form lets you use preapproved content during document generation.

Note: To insert a custom clause into a document generation form, you
must have the correct permissions granted by a CLM administrator. For more
information, see [Limited
Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. [Build a document
   generation form](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=sul1576610133098.html&_LANG=enus) or open an existing form.

   The product opens a form for you to work on.
3. Select Add Field > Term.

   The term field displays on the form. The product displays the
   Properties tab for the new field.
4. Specify a name, optional tooltip content, and clause library:
   1. Specify a unique name in the Field Name
      field.
   2. (Optional) Specify a value for Help Text to
      display as a tooltip for the clause in the document generation
      form.
   3. Select a clause library under Which Term?.

      The product populates the field on the form with content from
      the chosen library.
5. Customize the term field on the form by performing any of the following actions
   as needed:

   - Select the option that displays the desired clause if multiple options
     are available.
   - Select the field and drag it to another location on the form.
6. (Optional) Configure the remaining properties for the term field as
   needed:
   1. Select Add Conditions to set display conditions
      for the clause.
   2. Select the Show Option Text checkbox to display
      the text of the clause.
   3. Select the Show Option Notes checkbox to display
      any notes that exist for the clause.
   4. Select the Option Text is Editable checkbox to
      allow users to edit the clause text of a generated document.
7. Select Save.

   You have added a custom clause to a document generation form. You can add more
   clauses to the form as needed.

title: Archive to Agreement Action in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Archive to Agreement Action in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the Archive to Agreement Action in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use this
agreement action to automatically archive a document in CLM when specific conditions are
met.

The “Archive to” agreement action triggers archiving a document to CLM when specific envelope
conditions (e.g., custom fields or sender/recipient combinations) are met. Under Add
Actions, select **Archive to** and your CLM account type.![Agreement Actions in CLM 1](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Agreement-Actions-in-CLM-1.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTkwMTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.PYeRkeM5cVbT7NpLBL42K63erRd9cGqxEpMLlip613k&_LANG=enus "Agreement Actions in CLM 1")

You can customize your “Archive to” action by setting the format and convention for your
files and folders.![Agreement Actions in CLM 2](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Agreement-Actions-in-CLM-2.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTkwMTMsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.PYeRkeM5cVbT7NpLBL42K63erRd9cGqxEpMLlip613k&_LANG=enus "Agreement Actions in CLM 2")

title: XPath in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about how XPath is used in Docusign CLM for Workflows, Smart Rules, and other features.

XPath syntax is used in several places throughout Docusign CLM to access data formatted as XML. Docusign CLM can use XPath syntax in Document Generation templates, for document attributes, Smart Rules, and Workflows.

Note:

- For more information on using XPath syntax in Document Generation templates, see [Document Generation Word Template Merge Tags in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=uno1576795541214.html&_LANG=enus).
- For more information on using XPath syntax for Smart Rules, see [Use Cases and Sample Smart Rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qaz1642962729191&topicId=ttl1576610045092.html&_LANG=enus).
- For more information on using XPath syntax for document attributes, see [Configuration for XML Formatted Manifest Files in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=gov1576795527087.html&_LANG=enus).
- For more information on using XPath syntax in Workflows, see [Workflow Expression Builder in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=kxb1576610102148.html&_LANG=enus) and “Evaluate an XPath” in [Automated Document Management Workflow Steps in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=hpj1576610096401.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Switch to Docusign Admin from Docusign eSignature
heading_path: Switch to Docusign Admin from Docusign eSignature
text:
Learn how to switch from the Docusign eSignature application to Docusign Admin.
Administrators can switch applications to manage CLM users and groups for Docusign CLM
accounts.

title: Switch to Docusign Admin from Docusign eSignature
heading_path: Switch to Docusign Admin from Docusign eSignature
text:
You must perform the steps in the topic [Create an Organization from eSignature Admin](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=rbi1585149742539.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create an organization from eSignature Admin. Creating this organization allows administrators to manage CLM users and groups and manage other CLM settings.").

As an organization administrator, you can set up or manage CLM users and groups for Docusign CLM accounts. To do so, switch from your eSignature application to Docusign Admin.

1. Log in to your Docusign eSignature account.
2. Select the app switcher menu in the global header.

   ![admin-global-header](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/adminglobalheader.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1MTAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.Ke4nJijfoboyj7wg3ZuKaMlhYYzDwsjXhIQYGJkOvaU&_LANG=enus "admin-global-header")
3. Select Admin from the application list.

   The Docusign Admin application opens. Your organization administrator
   credentials log you into all Docusign applications. You can now manage
   groups and users from Docusign Admin.

title: Enable Two-Factor Authentication in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable two-factor authentication in CLM.CM.

In CLM.CM, super administrators can enable two-factor
authentication for all users. Two-factor authentication provides an additional level of
login security for users.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To
determine which kind of CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a super administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Security Settings under Account Management.

   The Security Settings page opens.
4. Select Yes in the Enable 2-Step
   Verification section.

   Note: Optionally, you can select the Allow
   users to login to SpringCM without 2-Step Verification for 30
   days check box to enable a grace period.
5. Select Save.

You have successfully enabled two-factor authentication for
CLM.CM. Users will need to set up an authenticator as part of login.

title: Create a Custom Clause Library with Clauses in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create a Custom Clause Library with Clauses in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create a custom clause library with clauses in Docusign CLM. Administrators
can create the library with clauses to use as pre-approved language during document
creation.

Docusign CLM administrators can create custom clause
libraries that contain clauses. The custom clause library gets created as an SXTERM
file. You can access the library and use clauses in document generation and Microsoft
Office 365.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents panel opens.
3. Select the folder where the clause library will be stored.
4. Select File  >  Create  >  New Term.

   A dialog opens for you to name the clause library.
5. Enter a name for the new library, and select OK.

   A page opens where you can create options that define the clauses for
   the library. On the Options tab, you can see clauses as you
   create them.
6. Define each clause for the library:
   1. Select Add Option.
   2. Specify a clause name in the field at the top to replace the default
      "New Option" text.
   3. Specify the clause content in the Option Text
      field.
   4. (Optional) Specify any notes about the clause in the Option
      Notes field.
   5. Select Save.

      The product saves your clause in the clause
      library.
7. (Optional) Select the Attributes tab to edit attributes
   or assign CLM attribute groups to the clause library.
8. Select Save to save any changes.

   You have successfully created a clause library with
   clauses.

title: Enable the Updated Task Management Page in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable the Updated Task Management Page in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to enable the updated task management page in Docusign CLM.
Administrators can enable this page, which has a modern layout.

Administrators can enable the updated task management
page in Docusign CLM. This page has a modern layout.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Select Enable updated task management page under the
   Workflow & Tasks section, and select
   Save.

The updated task management page is now enabled. Users see the
updated page when they select Tasks in the navigation header in
Docusign CLM.

title: Customize the Global Actions Menu in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize the Global Actions Menu in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to customize the Actions menu in Docusign CLM. Administrators can add custom
actions to the menu to help users save time when executing common tasks.

Administrators and Super Administratrors can add
additional custom actions to the Actions menu in Docusign CLM. These
custom actions can be designed to save your users time when executing common tasks.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Navigation & Actions under Account Management.
4. In the Who is this for? dropdown menu, select Account
   Default.
5. In the What are you customizing? dropdown menu, select
   User Navigation Header.
6. Select Actions Menu.
7. Select New Action.

   The Add New Custom Action window displays.
8. Select options for your new custom action:
   1. Enter a name for your new custom action in the Action Name
      field.
   2. (Optional) Provide a description for your custom action in the
      Description field.
   3. Select a language for your custom action in the Language for this
      action dropdown.
   4. Choose options for what your custom action does from the What should
      this action do? dropdown.
   5. Choose how your custom action displays additional windows and modals from the
      Open this action in: dropdown.
   6. Select an icon for your custom action from the What icon should
      represent this action? dropdown. You can also provide a URL for a custom
      icon by selecting Enter icon image URL.
   7. Select the checkbox for Pass user information with this
      action to log user data when the custom action is selected.
9. Select Save.

   You have successfully created a custom action in Docusign CLM.
   Users will see this action as an available option from the Actions
   menu.

title: Import Bulk Metadata
text:
Read about the Import Bulk Metadata action in Docusign CLM. Administrators use this
action to assign attribute values to up to 500 documents without the need for manual
entry.

Import Bulk Metadata, an action that can be done using a Smart Rule, lets Docusign CLM Administrators assign unique attribute values to up to 500 documents at a time without having a person manually enter document attributes. It is a multi-step process which requires you to create an input data file (typically a CSV), an Attribute Import Mapping, and a Document Rule.

Bulk Metadata Import is used when many unique attributes need to be applied to many documents at the same time. All the files to be updated must be in the same folder for this functionality to work, as This functionality is not meant for applying attributes to files across multiple folders.

Bulk Metadata Import, an action possible through use of a Smart Rule, enables CLM
administrators to assign unique attribute values to up to 500 documents at a time without
manually entering document attributes. It is a multi-step process which requires you to create
an input data file (typically a CSV), an Attribute Import Mapping, and a Document Rule.

Bulk Metadata Import is used when many unique attributes need to be applied to many documents at the same time. All the files to be updated must be in the same folder for this functionality to work, as This functionality is not meant for applying attributes to files across multiple folders.

This method is frequently used during migration of documents from another system to Docusign CLM.

title: Import Bulk Metadata
text:
- Before configuring the CSV, Document Rule, and Mapping, ensure all attributes and
  attributes groups are created and formatted as they will display in the CSV file. Refer to
  the knowledge article Creating and Managing Attribute Groups for more information.
- Upload the documents in bulk into only one folder (i.e., later you will upload the CSV
  file to this same folder). Documents can be uploaded in bulk via any upload method.
  Docusign recommends using SFTP when uploading a large number of documents.

title: Account Security in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about features and settings for account security in Docusign CLM.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM. To find out which version of
Docusign CLM you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM
Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

Administrators can set a number of options for security in Docusign CLM
that apply to all users. Administrators can set control options for session timeouts, sharing,
and prevention of clickjack exploits.

Administrators can access further account security options in Docusign Admin. Options are
available for single-sign on (SSO) configuration, password security, and others. For more
information about Docusign Admin, see [Introduction to Docusign Admin](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=oum1583359129731.html&_LANG=enus).

The following security options are available to administrators in Docusign CLM:

Session
:   Determines how long a user can be logged on and idle before being logged out of Docusign CLM. You set a value for the Idle Session
    Timeout field.

Clickjack Protection
:   Prevents Docusign CLM from being run in a frame, unless that frame is
    from a safelisted domain or IP. Administrators can set up domains that can run Docusign CLM in a frame.

Sharing
:   Controls whether users can publicly share documents and folders with individuals who are
    not members of your Docusign CLM organization.

title: Set an Account Homepage in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Set an Account Homepage in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to set a customized account homepage for users in Docusign
CLM.

Administrators and super administrators can change
the account homepage for users in Docusign CLM. Users will see the account
home page when they log in. By default, the account homepage is the
Documents page.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Navigation & Actions under Account Management.

   The Navigation & Actions page opens.
4. Select the Who is this for? drop-down menu, and select
   Account Default.
5. Select the What are you customizing? drop-down menu, and select
   User Navigation Header.
6. Hover your cursor over a page row in the Pages tab, and select the
   Set Homepage check box.

   The product now labels this item as the homepage.
7. Select Save.

You have successfully set a default account homepage. Users will
see the page when they log in or select the Docusign logo in the navigation
header.

title: Support Access in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about how to provide temporary access to Docusign Support.

Docusign CLM administrators can provide temporary access to Docusign Support to troubleshoot
issues. The administrator can control what the Docusign Support user can access. The
administrator can also provide elevated administrative permissions if necessary. All the
Support user's actions get recorded in document and folder history.

For more information on enabling Support access in Docusign CLM, see
[How to Grant Access to Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Docusign-CLM-How-to-Grant-Access-to-Support?language=en_US).

Note:

- Do not attempt to delete the Support user. The user is a standard
  product feature that is available when needed. Docusign does not impose a fee
  for providing this user.
- For CLM.CM accounts, only super administrators can provide access to Docusign Support. To determine your account type, see the documentation about [identifying the CLM account
  type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create an Explicit Schedule in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create an Explicit Schedule in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create an explicit schedule in Docusign CLM.
Administrators can use schedules for explicit document-management events.

Docusign CLM administrators can use
schedules for explicit document-management events. An administrator can create an explicit
schedule and run smart rules on an explicit date and time.

Note: For more
information about smart rules, see the documentation about [Smart Rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=yhx1576610044302.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Schedules under Automation Tools.

   The Schedules page opens.
4. Select Schedule > New.

   A Create Schedule page opens.
5. Define your explicit schedule:
   1. Specify a name for the schedule.

      A descriptive name helps you choose correctly when using a schedule selection
      drop-down.
   2. (Optional) Specify a description for the schedule.
   3. Enter a date in the first date field under Effective Between
      (the field on the left) in the following format

      mm/dd/yyyy

      Note: If you specify a date in the second date field (the field
      on the right), the date gets removed. This date is unnecessary because you are not
      configuring a recurring schedule.
   4. Specify a time value in the Start Time field in the
      following format:

      hh:mm:ss AM|PM

      Note: You will need to adjust the time so that it is entered in
      Central Standard Time. This time will not automatically adjust for your time zone or
      daylight saving time).
6. Select Save.

You have successfully created an explicit schedule in Docusign CLM.

title: Bulk AI Data Review in Docusign AI Extension for CLM
heading_path: Bulk AI Data Review in Docusign AI Extension for CLM
text:
Read about bulk AI data review in Docusign AI Extension for CLM, and how administrators
can validate AI-suggested attributes in bulk across multiple documents.

Note:
Docusign AI Extension for CLM is currently available only for new CLM
customers. Contact your account representative with questions or to purchase the
Docusign AI Extension for CLM add-on.

Docusign CLM can use artificial intelligence (AI) to extract key terms, or
attributes, from documents. This allows you to understand the agreement data in your
legacy or third-party agreements, and provides visibility to key stakeholders into
critical agreement information. This process also helps ensure that agreements are
discoverable and reportable.

Using bulk AI data review in Docusign AI Extension for CLM, you can now
validate attributes suggested by AI in bulk across multiple documents. This helps to
speed up onboarding and contract migration processes with bulk ingestion, analysis, and
extractions of key legal points from legacy agreements.

You must be an Administrator in CLM.DS to access the Bulk Documents and Data
Management experience in Docusign AI Extension for CLM.

title: Use Manual Entry to Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Use Manual Entry to Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text: Learn how to use manual entry to set up two-step verification in CLM.CM.

title: Use Manual Entry to Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Use Manual Entry to Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text:
- [Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in
  CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=oou1585347035068.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable two-step verification for your user profile in CLM.CM.").
- Download the Google Authenticator app to your device ([Android](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447?co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid&hl=en) or [iOS](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447?co=GENIE.Platform%3DiOS&hl=en)).

When you set up two-step verification, you can use manual entry instead of scanning a QR
code.

1. Specify your credentials on the login page for your CLM.CM account, and select
   Log In.

   The product prompts you to begin setting up two-step
   verification.
2. Select your device type (Android or
   iOS), and select Next.

   The product displays a page with a QR code (barcode).
3. Select **Use manual entry instead**.

   The product displays a page that shows a setup key.
4. Specify the setup key in the Google Authenticator app:
   1. Open the Google Authenticator app, and select **Enter a setup
      key**.
   2. Enter your email address in the Account
      field.
   3. Enter the setup key in the Key field.
   4. Ensure that Time based is selected for the
      drop-down.
   5. Select Add.
5. Select Next on the CLM.CM page that displays the setup
   key.

   The product prompts you to verify a code from the Google Authenticator
   app.
6. Enter the six-digit code from the app into the Verification
   Code field on the CLM.CM page, and select **Verify**.

The product logs you on. Each time you log on afterward, you must
get a verification code from the Google Authenticator app.

title: Set a Default Monetary Currency for Your Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Set a Default Monetary Currency for Your Docusign CLM Account
text:
Learn how to set a default monetary currency for a CLM account. Administrators can use
these steps to configure the account setting and support their international use cases
around the world.

title: Set a Default Monetary Currency for Your Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Set a Default Monetary Currency for Your Docusign CLM Account
text:
Party management must be enabled. For
information about enablement, see the documentation about [enabling or disabling party management in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=mjn1643378694283&topicId=uva1603410757890.html&_LANG=enus).

With party management enabled, an administrator can set a default monetary currency for a CLM
account. Making this change allows administrators to support international use cases
around the world.

Important: Changing the default currency (USD) changes only the display of the
currency in your account. The change does not update the actual
contract value. Converting currencies must be done manually.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Admin panel displays.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.
4. Select one of the following values from the Default
   Currency drop-down list in the Localization section:

   - AUD (Australian dollar)
   - BRL (Brazilian real)
   - CAD (Canadian dollar)
   - EUR (European Union euro)
   - GBP (British pound sterling)
   - JPY (Japanese yen)
   - MXN (Mexican peso)
   - USD (United States dollar) (Default)
5. Select Save.

   You have set the default currency for your CLM account. The specified
   currency now shows up in the following areas of the product:
   - Document previews
   - Party 360 summary page under Parties
   - Agreement summary page under Parties
   - Upload forms for party documents

title: Widgets for Docusign CLM Dashboards
text:
Look up the functions of widgets in Docusign CLM dashboards.

You can use widgets to organize information for your business purposes. The following table describes widgets that can be configured on Docusign CLM dashboards:

| Widget | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Folder | Displays the contents of any folder that the user has access to. This widget allows you to search folders and documents. You can also execute document actions. You can configure the widget to show specific folders or all folders in the account. |
| Folder watchlist | Displays document tasks in a specified folder that are assigned to users as part of a workflow. To use this widget, folders must be added to the watchlist through a workflow step. Note: For more information, see the Add Documents to Watched Documents List step description in [Automated Document Management Workflow Steps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=hpj1576610096401.html&_LANG=enus). |
| My watchlist | Displays document tasks that are assigned to users as part of a workflow. To use this widget, documents must be added to the watchlist through a workflow step. Only documents that have active tasks get displayed in the widget. After the task gets completed, the document no longer displays in the widget. Note: For more information, see the Add Documents to Watched Documents List step description in [Automated Document Management Workflow Steps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=hpj1576610096401.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Recent documents | Displays the 10 most recently accessed, viewed, or modified documents by the current user. You can also perform document actions on any of the listed documents. |
| Report | Displays a selected report. This widget allows some reports to be exported in CSV or XLSX format. Note: For more information about the widget, see [Visualize Document Reports on the Dashboard in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lye1642705772274&topicId=yfw1603415758563.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Saved Search | (Available in [modernized search](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=jbg1707491822735.html&_LANG=enus)) Displays the results from a saved search. The person who adds the widget selects the saved search to display. From an existing widget, you can perform the following activities:  - Select a document from the results to open the document preview in a new tab - Select Open to access the full page of search results for the saved search   Note: You can edit the widget to change to a different saved search. After saving your changes, you must refresh the browser page. |
| Search | Enables selection of a saved search and displays search results on the dashboard. Note: For more information on search in Docusign CLM, see [Search in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=aug1576609932447.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Workflow inbox | Displays workflow tasks that are assigned to the current user. This widget can be configured to show tasks from specific workflows. |

title: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM
heading_path: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to use SFTP to connect to Docusign CLM.

title: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM
heading_path: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM
text:
[Upload Public Key for SFTP Authentication in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=yva1619203531519.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload your public key for SFTP authentication in Docusign CLM.")

Administrators can connect to Docusign CLM using an
SFTP client.

Important:

- Docusign does not provide keypairs for SFTP authentication. If you need
  assistance in creating or managing keypairs for your SFTP configuration,
  please contact your account team regarding our Professional Services
  offerings.
- This topic applies to Docusign CLM. For more information about SFTP for Docusign CLM.CM,
  see [Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bbl1576609938274.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the SFTP self-service for CLM.CM. Super administrators can use SFTP for moving documents or folders between a local data source and CLM.CM."). To determine which kind of
  Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM
  Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Navigate to Integrations  >  System Domains.
4. Copy the URL in the SFTP: field.

   Note: The SFTP URL is specific to your Docusign CLM
   account.
5. In your SFTP client, set up a new connection.
6. Set SFTP as the protocol to use.
7. Enter the Host Name as your SFTP URL from step 4.
8. Enter the Port Number as 22.
9. Associate your Private Key with the connection.

   You have successfully connected to your Docusign CLM account using SFTP. You may now
   view, upload, and download files.

   Note:
   - If any folder within the account meets or exceeds 10,000 objects,
     the folder will not be accessible through SFTP. Please contact
     Customer Support for further assistance.
   - Documents cannot be placed in the root directory. Navigate to a
     desired folder to upload or download documents.

title: Writeback to Salesforce in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Writeback to Salesforce in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about Writeback in Docusign CLM. Administrators use Writeback to ensure Salesforce fields and objects stay in sync with Word documents that get generated by CLM and use Salesforce data.

Note: This documentation covers legacy document preview experiences for Docusign CLM. As of July 1, 2025, most customers have been upgraded to Document Review as their default document preview experience in Docusign CLM. For more information on deprecation of document preview experiences, see [Docusign Document Preview Experience Changes FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ssg1729007411846&topicId=twq1729007565221.html&_LANG=enus). For more information on features and enhancements available in Document Review, see [Document Review in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=eyk1666363075054.html&_LANG=enus).

Writeback helps ensure that Salesforce data fields and objects stay in sync with Word documents that get generated by Docusign CLM and use Salesforce data. For example, a document can change during a workflow or during negotiation. In these situations, Writeback alerts users when Salesforce and the document have fields that do not match. Users can then update Salesforce.

Note: To use Writeback, you must meet the following requirements:

- You must have Salesforce integrated with Docusign CLM. For information about setting up an integration with Salesforce, see  [Docusign CLM for Salesforce Administrator Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=evq1576795556979.html&_LANG=enus).
- You must configure document generation forms in Docusign CLM. For information about these forms, see [Document Generation Forms in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=rsb1576795539656.html&_LANG=enus).

Writeback uses the Data Reconciliation workflow step to simplify the data reconciliation process between CLM and Salesforce. Using this step provides a task in the CLM interface that allows users to resolve mismatches and update Salesforce.

title: Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text: Learn how to set up two-step verification as a CLM.CM user.

title: Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Set Up Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text:
- [Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in
  CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=oou1585347035068.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable two-step verification for your user profile in CLM.CM.").
- Download the Google Authenticator app to your device ([Android](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447?co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid&hl=en) or [iOS](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447?co=GENIE.Platform%3DiOS&hl=en)).

After you enable two-step verification or an administrator enables it, you must set up
verification for your user profile.

1. Specify your credentials on the login page for your CLM.CM account, and select
   Log In.

   The product prompts you to begin setting up two-step
   verification.
2. Select your device type (Android or
   iOS), and select Next.

   The product displays a page with a QR code (barcode).
3. Scan the QR code:
   1. Select **Scan a QR code** in your Google Authenticator app.
   2. Follow the instructions in the app to scan the code.

      The app displays a code.
4. Select **Next** on the CLM.CM page that displays the QR code.
5. Enter the six-digit verification code from the app, and select
   **Verify**.

The product logs you on. Each time you log on afterward, you must
get a verification code from the Google Authenticator app.

title: Enable Interactive Preview for Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable Interactive Preview for Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Enable interactive preview in Docusign CLM to allow for commenting.

Note: This documentation covers legacy document preview experiences for Docusign CLM. As of July 1, 2025, most customers have been upgraded to Document Review as their default document preview experience in Docusign CLM. For more information on deprecation of document preview experiences, see [Docusign Document Preview Experience Changes FAQ](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ssg1729007411846&topicId=twq1729007565221.html&_LANG=enus). For more information on features and enhancements available in Document Review, see [Document Review in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=eyk1666363075054.html&_LANG=enus).

To enable collaboration through commenting on documents in Docusign CLM, an administrator must enable interactive preview.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator, and select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM Admin page opens.
2. In the left-side panel, under Documents & Folders, select Document & Folder Settings.

   The Document & Folder Settings window opens in the right-side panel.
3. Under the drop-down for Default Document Viewing Format, select Interactive Preview.
4. Select Save.

   Interactive preview is now set as the default document preview format.

title: Enable Interactive Preview for Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable Interactive Preview for Documents in Docusign CLM
text: **What to do next:**  [Enable the Updated Task Management Page in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=dtn1607108809778.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the updated task management page in Docusign CLM. Administrators can enable this page, which has a modern layout.")

title: Claim a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Claim a Task in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to claim a task in Docusign CLM.

title: Claim a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Claim a Task in Docusign CLM
text:
- Select Enable updated task management page on the
  Account Settings page. The updated page has a more
  modern layout.
- To perform this activity on tasks for other task group members, users must have
  the proper permissions. For more information, see the documentation about [user permissions that administrators can
  assign to task group members](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=rij1684257629881.html&_LANG=enus).

In Docusign CLM, you can claim tasks. This process helps you manage
workloads and manage business processes.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens.
3. Select a task view.
4. Select the checkbox next to the task that you want to claim.
5. Select Claim.

   A message confirms that you claimed the task.

You have claimed the task.

title: User Profile Properties in CLM.CM
text:
Look up the user profile properties for CLM.CM. Administrators can set these properties
when creating or managing users.

The following user profile properties are available in CLM.CM. Administrators can set these
properties when creating or managing users.

| Profile Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Login Name | Specifies a login name. This property is required for users that must log on to Docusign CLM. A login name must be between 2 and 100 characters and must be unique to CLM.CM. After a user's login name gets set, it cannot be changed. To change a login name, you must delete the user and re-create the user. You can search for users in the CLM address book by login name. |
| Permission Profile | Assigns a profile that has a defined set of capabilities. The assigned profile determines what actions a user can perform. Note: For more information, see the documentation about [permission profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=wji1582562825539.html&_LANG=enus "Read about permission profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM. Administrators can assign a profile with defined capabilities to determine what actions a user can perform."). |
| Delegated Permission | Assigns delegated permissions to a user in the Full Subscriber role. Users who get assigned a permission will have access to any areas you indicate; however, the users still respect the folder and document security that is set for full subscribers. Note: For more information about delegated permissions, see the [Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about delegated permissions in Docusign CLM. Administrators can assign permissions to users in Docusign CLM (or full subscribers in CLM.CM). Users with delegated permissions have access to assigned administrative functions.") topic. |
| User Persona | Assigns a persona that changes the look and feel of the CLM.CM interface based on a user's job function. |
| Managed by | Specifies another CLM.CM user who can manage workflow tasks that are assigned to this user. The user that is listed in the Managed by field can see workflow tasks that are assigned to this user in the workflow inbox. The assignee can also respond on behalf of the user when the user is unavailable to take action. |
| Group Memberships | Optionally adds the user to a security group. You can create the user without adding the user to a group. You can edit the user later to add the group. You can also edit the group and add the user to the group. |
| Enable User Date/Disable User Date | Defines a start and end date for a user to be active in your account. For example, you want to provide access to a contractor for a temporary project. You can specify the start and end dates of the project. The contractor can log on to your CLM.CM account only for the duration of the project. |

title: Account Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about the various features available to Docusign CLM administrators for managing
their account. Administrators have multiple options for setting up login security, device
management, and user interface.

As an administrator in Docusign CLM, you have a wide array of controls and options for setting
up Docusign CLM. Part of getting your new environment ready includes options for
security, user interface and experience, and user permissions.

These guides are intended to lead you through these options, and to help make decisions
on how these features are used.

Note: Several of these topics are specific to either Docusign CLM or
Docusign CLM.CM, as indicated by their title. To determine which product you have, see
[Identify Your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Add a Widget to Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Add a Widget to Your Dashboard in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to add widgets to your dashboard in Docusign CLM. You can use widgets to
organize information for your business purposes.

In Docusign CLM, you can use widgets to create
visibility on document information and reports. Widgets help you organize information for your
business purposes.

Note: To learn more about widgets and their functions
in Docusign CLM, see [Widgets for Docusign CLM Dashboards](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=fgc1642618158282.html&_LANG=enus "Look up the functions of widgets in Docusign CLM dashboards.").

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Dashboard in the navigation header.

   The dashboard page opens.
3. Select Add > Widget.

   The Add Widget menu opens at the bottom of the
   screen.
4. Select a widget, and drag it onto the dashboard.

   The product might display a dialog box for you to configure widget properties.
   If not, the product adds the widget to the dashboard.
5. Configure the widget properties (if prompted), and select
   Save.

You have added a widget to your dashboard in Docusign CLM.

title: Create Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to use delegated permissions to assign administrative permissions.
Administrators can use these steps to create delegated permissions and enable them for Docusign CLM users.

Administrators can assign limited administrative
permissions to CLM users. Users with delegated permissions have access to specific assigned
administrative functions. Delegated permissions give your organization flexibility when
managing work in CLM.

Note:

- In Docusign CLM, administrators can assign delegated permissions only to users with
  the CLM User permission profile.
- In CLM.CM, super administrators can assign delegated permissions only to CLM users
  with the Full Subscriber permission profile.
- To see what CLM account type you have, see the topic [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
2. Select Delegated Permissions under User
   Management.

   The Delegated Permissions page opens.
3. Select New Delegated Permission.

   The Add Delegated Permission page opens.
4. Enter a name for the new delegated permission.
5. Select the boxes next to the administrative areas that you want to grant.
6. Select Save.

The delegated permission is now available from the
Delegated Permissions page.

title: Create Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
text:
**What to do next:**

- [Assign Delegated Permissions to a User in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=puh1576610087652.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use delegated permissions to assign administrative permissions. Administrators can use these steps to create delegated permissions and enable them for Docusign CLM users.")

title: Unassign a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Unassign a Task in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to unassign a task in Docusign CLM.

title: Unassign a Task in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Unassign a Task in Docusign CLM
text:
- Select Enable updated task management page on the
  Account Settings page. The updated page has a more modern
  layout.
- To perform this activity on tasks for other task group members, users must have the
  proper permissions. For more information, see the documentation about [user permissions that administrators can assign to
  task group members](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=rij1684257629881.html&_LANG=enus).

You can unassign tasks. This process helps you manage
workloads and manage business processes.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens.
3. Select a task view.
4. Select the checkbox next to the task that you want to unassign.
5. Select Unassign.

   A prompt opens, requesting you to confirm the action.
6. Select Unassign.

You have unassigned the task.

title: Configure Writeback in Docusign CLM Workflows using the Data Reconciliation Workflow Step
heading_path: Configure Writeback in Docusign CLM Workflows using the Data Reconciliation Workflow Step
text:
Learn how to configure Writeback in Docusign CLM Workflows.

The data reconciliation workflow step allows you to use Writeback at any point in
your CLM workflow to reconcile the data fields in your document with the values in
the Salesforce record. Docusign recommends using Writeback in CLM workflows at the
end of the negotiation process once a document has been signed. For information
about setting up workflows in CLM, see [Workflow Management in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=kuq1584137317950.html&_LANG=enus).

Note: The data reconciliation workflow step requires a Microsoft
Word document in parameters and an assignee.

In Tracked Names, specify the tracked content TrackNames that are provided
to you in the Document Generation form configuration. To locate the TrackNames:

1. Navigate to Admin > Automation Tools > Document Generation > Forms.
2. Open your Document Generation form.
3. Select a form field. If the form field is linked to Salesforce, open the
   Merge Tags tab. To see if a form field is linked to
   Salesforce, review the Link to Salesforce Field checkbox
   located under Field Value.
4. In the Merge Tags tab, copy the field name that displays
   within quotation marks after TrackName=.
5. Paste the field name into the Tracked Name field in the
   data reconciliation workflow step. Use a pipe delimiter to separate multiple
   field names. Do not add spaces. The name must be an exact match without the
   quotation marks.

   ![CLM Writeback Data reconciliation workflow step](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-Writeback-Data-reconciliation-workflow-step.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg5NzIsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.w2itAhKSfkPREz72-Nbd0mW2VIPNZ9Sglgm3LkaH7JI&_LANG=enus "CLM Writeback Data reconciliation workflow step")

   The data reconciliation workflow step will output writeback fields
   and results as an .xml file. This output includes a summary of which fields
   had mismatches, which fields in the document are linked to Salesforce, and
   whether the user wrote new values to Salesforce or ignored the mismatched
   fields.

title: Transfer a Component Package to a Destination CLM Account
heading_path: Transfer a Component Package to a Destination CLM Account
text: Learn how to transfer and unpack a component package in Docusign CLM.

title: Transfer a Component Package to a Destination CLM Account
heading_path: Transfer a Component Package to a Destination CLM Account
text:
[Create a Component Package in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=pgv1576609934376.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to package Docusign CLM account components for transfer to another CLM account. Folders and attribute groups are examples of components that an adminisrator can package.")

Administrators can transfer a component package to a destination CLM account. With this
process, you can move account components between environments. Examples are as follows:

- You can move components from a UAT environment to a Production environment.
- You can move components from one account to another account within the same environment
  (such as Production).

You can use any transfer method. This procedure performs the transfer by using the Docusign CLM user interface. You can also use the Secure File Transfer
Protocol (SFTP) to perform the transfer.

Note:

- To access the package, you need at least View and Edit privileges on the .spackage
  file. To install the package, you must be a CLM administrator.
- The package cannot be edited in the destination account. If the package
  requires corrections, you must create a new package in the source account.

To transfer the package, perform the following steps:

1. Download the component package that you want to transfer:
   1. Log in to the source CLM environment where you [created the package](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=pgv1576609934376.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to package Docusign CLM account components for transfer to another CLM account. Folders and attribute groups are examples of components that an adminisrator can package.").
   2. Browse to the location of the .spackage file, and download the file locally.
2. Log in to the destination Docusign CLM account where you want to
   install the package.
3. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
4. Browse to an upload location.
5. Upload the .spackage file:
   1. Select File > Upload.
   2. Browse to where you downloaded the file from the source environment.
   3. Select the file, and select Open.

      The product uploads the file to your desired location.
6. Double-click the file name and wait for a page to open where you can install the
   package.

   The product unpacks the file’s data. After several seconds, a deployment page
   opens. You are ready to install the package.
7. Select Install.

   A green banner message indicates that the package was submitted for
   installation. You will receive an email that indicates whether installation was successful
   or errors occurred.

If installation is successful, you can access any transferred
components in the destination CLM account. If you make changes to transferred components, you
can install the package again to restore the original package. You can install the package as
many times as needed.

If you need assistance with resolving errors, contact Docusign Support.

title: Reconcile Data Using Writeback in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Reconcile Data Using Writeback in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to reconcile data using Writeback in Docusign CLM.

When the Data Reconciliation workflow step runs after a workflow is triggered in CLM or
Salesforce, the step will generate a task for the user if mismatches are found between
the tracked content value in the document generation field of your Word document and the
