corresponding Salesforce value.![CLM writeback reconcile task1](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-writeback-reconcile-task1.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.jZxvBJQKeLwNBleoK156tBAT8Mn96RXZ-e8ImCngrCs&_LANG=enus "CLM writeback reconcile task1")

For each field assigned in the data reconciliation task, the user can open the field to
review both the value in Salesforce as well as the value in the document. From this
view, the user can choose to update the Salesforce value from the CLM UI, or ignore the
mismatch and make no changes to the Salesforce value.![CLM writeback reconcile task2](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-writeback-reconcile-task2.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg5NzUsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.jZxvBJQKeLwNBleoK156tBAT8Mn96RXZ-e8ImCngrCs&_LANG=enus "CLM writeback reconcile task2")

In order to complete the Data Reconciliation step, the user must review and select an
outcome for each field in the reconciliation task.

title: Template Mapping in Docusign CLM Document Generation
heading_path: Template Mapping in Docusign CLM Document Generation
text:
Learn how templates are created for document generation in Docusign CLM.

Part of setting up your implementation of Docusign CLM is the creation of document generation
templates.

Document generation templates are created in a two-step process. First, a finalized
document is marked up with comments corresponding to fields and field types for document
generation. Next, the markups are cataloged in a spreadsheet that connects the markups
to field types in Docusign CLM. Each field type can be conditionally formatted for user
input, and conditional inclusion or exclusion in a generated document.

Note: These features apply to all versions of Docusign CLM. For
Docusign CLM Essentials, Docusign Professional Services or your implementing partner
will assist in the review and availability of fields.

title: Docusign CLM Administration Overview
text:
Read about the role of administrators in Docusign CLM. Administrators are responsible
for account management, user management, and other areas in CLM.

Docusign CLM is a secure document-management platform. You can use CLM to generate documents,
collaborate with stakeholders, and automate business processes.

Docusign CLM administrators are responsible for account management and user management.
Administrators can also maintain the following product areas:

- Product integrations (for example, Salesforce)
- Automated workflows
- Reporting

title: Create a Public Share Link on a Folder in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create a Public Share Link on a Folder in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create a public share link for a folder in Docusign CLM. Administrators can
use a public link to share documents with non-CLM users.

Creating a public share link on a folder in Docusign CLM enables documents and resources
to be shared with non-CLM users.

Note: You must be a Docusign CLM
Administrator or have delegated permissions to create public share links. For more
information about delegated permissions, see [Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in
Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The folder list displays.
3. Select the dropdown menu for the folder you want to share, and choose Send  >  Share Link.
4. Choose Make a Public Link, and select your
   options:
   1. Select the checkbox for Allow downloads to
      enable downloads of documents.
   2. Select the checkbox for Allow PDF downloads to
      enable downloads of PDF document.
   3. Select the date picker to choose an expiry date for the public
      link.
5. Share your public link:
   1. Copy the link URL to enable copy-paste sharing.
   2. Select Send to send the public link in an email
      through Docusign CLM.

   You have created a public link for a folder in Docusign CLM.

title: Docusign CLM Process Flow Diagram
text:
Read about process flow diagrams in Docusign CLM and how users can use them for mapping
out an overview of important components and processes before CLM implementation.

All agreements that an organization creates and reviews go through a process in which it
is prepared, acted upon, signed, and managed. As a result, before implementing Docusign CLM, you must create a diagram to map out the process.

Your CLM team helps you refine your existing process flow diagram or create a new one
during the discovery phase. Your team also helps improve your current and future
processes.

If your process flow diagram is not well-defined before the discovery phase, it is best
to outline a framework by answering the following questions:

- Who are the actors involved in the document review process, and what are their
  roles?
- How do contracts enter the process flow, and how does the central actor
  respond?
- What actions does the system, meaning CLM, need to perform?
- Is there a strategy in place to deal with setbacks?
- Are there any special requirements for approval?

First, create your process flow diagram by defining the actors and their roles. Actors
can be clients or end-users, legal or finance departments, attorneys, or internal users.
The CLM system is also an actor because you must decide the actions it performs. For
example, is CLM routing documents conditionally or sending emails or notifications to
alert activity?

Next, consider how a newly prepared contract enters the process flow and how the central
actor responds. For example, central actors commonly route documents to different review
parties within an organization.

Then, plan exception paths for when rejections occur. For example, if a client refuses to
sign a document due to an error, an appropriate cancellation path is necessary to route
the document back to the actor responsible for making corrections.

Lastly, decide if there are any special requirements involved in the process. For
example, a $500k contract might need special approval from the SVP.

![CLM Process Flow Diagram](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-Process-Flow-Diagram.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTkwMTgsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.jGPZJjUYJLoTDdv4Pqryr5mDInsJr3gAlBdfwXHJP-s&_LANG=enus "CLM Process Flow Diagram")

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
Read about each of the account settings available in CLM.CM.

Super administrators can configure CLM.CM account settings by selecting
Admin from the navigation header. CLM.CM super administrators can
configure options and settings in each of these sections.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To determine which kind of CLM account
you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- Document Generation: enables users to generate documents and
  initiate review processes. Access Configurations, Forms, Form Field Libraries, and
  Settings here.
- eForms: allows for creation of web-based forms for document and
  data submission.
- Reminder Templates: allows creation of pre-defined document
  reminder criteria, based on document name, recipients, and other categories.
- Schedules: enables setup of scheduled rules for document
  management.
- Smart Rules: allows for setup of automated actions on folders or
  documents based on events that occur in folders or documents..
- Workflows: allows for automation of complex processes, including
  document generation and review.

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- Attributes: metadata tags and groups that can be applied to a
  document, enabling categorization and easier search.
- Attribute Import Mappings: create an XML or CSV spreadsheet map
  for importing documents and their related attributes.
- Auto Numbers: used to automatically assign a unique identifier to
  a document at upload or ingestion into CLM.CM.
- Document & Folder Settings: used to configure settings for
  user interactions with documents and folders.
- Document Types: used to change name and visibility of document
  types in Docusign CLM. For more information about document types, see
  [Document Classification in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ycb1666892024625.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the different types of document types in Docusign CLM. Administrators can enable custom document types, and make changes to the visibility of document types.").
- Inbound Email Addresses: enables setup of an inbound email
  connection for emailing documents directly to CLM.CM.
- Inbound Fax Lines: enables setup of an inbound fax line for
  faxing documents directly to CLM.CM.

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- Users: view all user profiles, and add, create, import, and
  export users.
- Groups: view all security groups, and add, create, edit, and
  delete groups.
- Task Groups: view and manage all task groups.
- Delegated Permissions: create custom permission profiles to
  assign to users.

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- Account Settings: general settings for your CLM.CM account,
  including language, region settings, and feature enablement.
- Branding: customization options for CLM.CM, including logos and
  colors.
- Deployment: create and configure deployment packages for your
  account.
- Device Management: manage access for user devices accessing your
  CLM.CM account.
- Navigation & Actions: customize the navigation header and
  actions menu for CLM.CM users.
- Reseller Accounts: create and remove trial accounts for
  additional Super Administrators.
- Security Settings: set up security options, such as Salesforce
  authentication, timeout length, two-factor authentication, and sharing settings.

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- System Domains: set URLs for accessing your CLM.CM account.
- Electronic Signature: set up electronic signature
  integrations.
- API Settings: set up API mappings and domain configurations.
- Connections: connect third-party applications to CLM.CM.
- **Docusign Analyzer**: connect CLM.CM to Docusign
  Insight.
- SAML SSO: configure identity and service provider information for
  single sign-on (SSO).

title: Account Settings in CLM.CM
text:
- Parties: create, edit, delete, and bulk upload parties for
  organizing documents.

title: Build a Schedule in Docusign CLM to Trigger a Rule for Trashing Documents
heading_path: Build a Schedule in Docusign CLM to Trigger a Rule for Trashing Documents
text:
Learn how to build a schedule in Docusign CLM to help with trash removal. Administrators
can use the schedule to trigger a rule that moves canceled contracts to the
trash.

To build a scheduled rule, you must first build a schedule in Docusign CLM. Your schedule sets
the dates and times that your rule will run. You can schedule the rule to run once
or run hourly, daily, weekly, or monthly.

Use this procedure to create a schedule that can instruct a rule to move canceled contracts to
the trash.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select  Schedules under Automation Tools.

   The Schedules page opens.
4. Select Schedule > New.

   The Create Schedule page opens.
5. Create a schedule:

   1. Complete the following fields to define the schedule:

      Name
      :   Specifies a descriptive name for the schedule.

      Description
      :   Specifies text that explains the purpose of the rule.

      Effective Between
      :   Specifies a start and end date for the rule.

      Start Time
      :   Specifies the exact time for the rule to begin
          operating.
      :   **Example:** 6:00pm

      Recurrence
      :   Specifies how often the schedule runs:
          - None specifies that the
            schedule runs once and does not repeat.
          - Hourly sets the hours and
            days of the week that the schedule runs.
          - Weekly sets the days that the
            schedule runs each week.
          - Monthly sets the days of the
            month that the schedule runs.
   2. Select Save.

   A message indicates that the schedule was saved. The
   Schedules page opens, showing the new
   schedule.

You have built the schedule. Your schedule can now be used in a
scheduled rule that moves files to the trash folder.

title: Build a Schedule in Docusign CLM to Trigger a Rule for Trashing Documents
heading_path: Build a Schedule in Docusign CLM to Trigger a Rule for Trashing Documents
text:
[Create your scheduled
rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ajj1636262082417.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a scheduled smart rule in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build this rule to move files to the trash folder on a schedule.").

title: Enable or Disable Edits to Submitted eForms in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable or Disable Edits to Submitted eForms in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to enable or disable edits on submitted eForms in Docusign
CLM.

Administrators and super administrators control
whether submitted eForms can be updated in Docusign CLM.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select eForms under Automation Tools.

   The Forms page opens.
4. Select the check box for the eForm that you want to change.
5. Perform one of the following actions:

   - Select Access > Allow Updates to enable editing of submitted eForms.
   - Select Access > Disable Updates to disable editing of submitted eForms.

You have successfully enabled or disabled updates for submitted
eForms.

title: Set Access Rights on a Folder in Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Set Access Rights on a Folder in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to set access rights on a folder in Docusign CLM.CM.

Super Administrators can set granular access rights
on folders in Docusign CLM.CM. By default, documents and subfolders inherit the security
settings established at the parent folder. When viewing the security settings of subfolders or
documents, you will see that most are noted as inherited.

Note: This
topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account you have,
see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as a Super Administrator
2. Select Documents from the navigation header.

   The Documents screen displays.
3. Navigate to the folder for which you want to define access, and select the checkbox for
   that folder.
4. In the Manage menu, select
   Security.

   The Folder Security screen displays.
5. Set the desired access rights for each role.
6. (Optional) Add additional users or groups for access by entering them in the search
   field, or selecting them from the Address Book.
7. (Optional) Set the desired access rights for each user added to the list.
8. Once all changes have been made, select Save.

   You have successfully assigned access rights to a folder in Docusign CLM.CM. All
   documents and sub-folders within the selected folder will inherit the security settings
   from the top level.

title: Create a Recurring Schedule in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create a Recurring Schedule in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create a recurring schedule in Docusign CLM.
Administrators can use schedules for recurring document-management events.

Administrators can create a recurring schedule in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use this type of schedule for recurring
document-management events.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Schedules under Automation Tools.

   The Schedules page opens.
4. Select Schedule > New.

   The Create Schedule page opens.
5. Define your recurring schedule:
   1. Specify a descriptive name for the schedule.
   2. Specify a description for the schedule.
   3. Specify a date range in the Effective Between fields in the
      MM/DD/YYYY format.

      The schedule will be valid during these dates.
   4. Specify a value in the Start Time field in the format HH:MM
      ?M (where ?M is AM or PM).

      Note: Adjust the time so that it is entered in Central Standard
      Time. The time does not automatically adjust for your time zone or daylight saving
      time.
   5. Select Recurrence (to expand the section), and select one of
      the following options to set up the recurrence:

      Hourly
      :   Runs a smart rule more than one time a day on particular days of the week.
          Provide the interval in hours (for example, every 2 hours). Select the days of
          the week when the rule will run. The initial time is taken from the
          Start Time field.

      Weekly
      :   Runs a smart rule at the configured time on particular days of the week.
          Select the days of the week. The time is taken from the Start
          Time field.

      Monthly
      :   Runs a smart rule at the configured time on particular days of the month. You
          specify specific days of the month or relative days of the month. If you select
          the Days of Month option, specify one or more numbers
          representing the DD value in a typical MM/DD/YYYY format. Examples are 01 and
          15. If you select the Day of Week option, select the
          relative option (for example, First), and select the day of the
          week. The time is taken from the Start Time field.
   6. Select Save.

The product saves your recurring schedule.

title: Show or Hide Task Management Columns in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Show or Hide Task Management Columns in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to show or hide columns in the task management page of Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can control columns to create a view that
meets their specific needs.

title: Show or Hide Task Management Columns in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Show or Hide Task Management Columns in Docusign CLM
text:
You must have Enable updated task management
page selected on the Account Settings page.

Administrators and users can show or hide columns on their task management page in Docusign CLM. Customizing the columns helps you create a view that meets
your specific needs.

Note: Administrators cannot control which columns get displayed for all
users. Individual users control how their page's columns are set up.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens.
3. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg2MTksInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.5JPL79vlw6PH2_imMfJcpt-T6bwk2dS5rqqnpN1iWek&_LANG=enus) icon.

   The Customize columns dialog box opens. Currently visible
   columns have a switch that is blue and right-aligned. Currently hidden columns have a
   switch that is white and left-aligned.
4. Show or hide columns as needed:

   - Show a column by selecting the switch to change it to blue and right-aligned.
   - Hide a column by selecting the switch to change it to white and left-aligned.
5. (Optional) Select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Drag-and-Move.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg2MTksInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.5JPL79vlw6PH2_imMfJcpt-T6bwk2dS5rqqnpN1iWek&_LANG=enus) next to a
   column to drag the column and rearrange the column order.
6. Select Apply to save your layout.

The task management page now shows the layout that you
applied.

title: Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM
text:
Read about how to connect an SFTP client to Docusign CLM for secure uploads and
backups.

Important:

- Docusign does not provide keypairs for SFTP authentication. If you need assistance in
  creating or managing keypairs for your SFTP configuration, please contact your
  account team regarding our Professional Services offerings.
- Docusign does not provide or recommend SFTP client applications.
- This topic applies to Docusign CLM. For more information about SFTP for Docusign CLM.CM, see
  [Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bbl1576609938274.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the SFTP self-service for CLM.CM. Super administrators can use SFTP for moving documents or folders between a local data source and CLM.CM."). To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your
  Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

Secure file transfer protocol (SFTP) enables movement of documents or folders between a local
data source and Docusign CLM. You can use SFTP for uploading and downloading within
Docusign CLM, or to move documents across different folders. SFTP is used to migrate
documents to and from Docusign CLM for batches of files between 30MB and 1GB in size. To
learn more about limitations for SFTP in Docusign CLM, see [SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=osg1576609938920.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the limitations of SFTP for Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM.").

Folder structure hierarchy is maintained when using SFTP. When moving parent folders with child
sub-folders, folder structure will be kept intact through the move. SFTP should not be
used to delete files or folders. When deleting documents using SFTP, files will
automatically be disassociated from the origin folder. This is a feature of SFTP
clients, not Docusign CLM. If you delete a folder with many files, you will see the
folder separately from the files in the trash folder.

Docusign CLM supports connection to our SFTP servers through Public Key Authentication. This
offers an additional level of security by storing a public key in Docusign and a
corresponding private key on your computer.

To use SFTP with Docusign CLM, you will need to install an SFTP client. Please consult with
your IT administrator to choose an SFTP client that is compliant with your
organization.

Note: There are known issues with WinSCP's SFTP client, specifically
the “resumable file upload” feature. Using this feature may result in fragmented or
duplicate uploads, or loss of data. Docusign CLM is not responsible for issues that may
occur when uploading files with this feature enabled.

title: IP Address Ranges and Domains for Docusign CLM
heading_path: IP Address Ranges and Domains for Docusign CLM
text:
Read about the IP address ranges and domains used by Docusign CLM.

Docusign CLM has a number of secure IP address ranges, domains, and security protocols to
ensure the safety of your data.

[Visit the Docusign Trust Center](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm) to learn more about the IP addresses and
domains used by Docusign CLM.

title: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to use an SFTP client to connect to CLM.CM. Super administrators use SFTP for
moving documents or folders between a local data source and CLM.CM.

Super Administrators can connect to Docusign CLM.CM
using an SFTP client.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. For
more information on using SFTP to connect to Docusign CLM, see [Use SFTP to Connect to Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=fgy1619203740592.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use SFTP to connect to Docusign CLM."). To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account you have,
see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.CM as a Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation panel.

   The Docusign CLM.CM admin panel displays.
3. Navigate to Integrations  >  System Domains.
4. Copy the URL in the SFTP: field.
5. In your SFTP client, set up a new connection.
6. Set SFTP as the protocol to use.
7. Enter the Host Name as your SFTP URL copied from step 4.
8. Enter the Port Number as 22.
9. Enter your Docusign CLM.CM username and password when prompted for your
   credentials.

   You have successfully connected to your Docusign CLM.CM account using SFTP. You
   may now view, upload, and download files.

   Note:
   - If any folder within the account meets or exceeds 10,000 objects, the folder will
     not be accessible through SFTP. Please contact Customer Support for further
     assistance.
   - Documents cannot be placed in the root directory. Navigate to a desired folder to
     upload or download documents.

title: Define Language Settings for a Docusign CLM Account
heading_path: Define Language Settings for a Docusign CLM Account
text:
Learn how to define language settings for a Docusign CLM account. Administrators use
these steps to customize the product to meet the language needs of users.

On the Account Settings page in
Docusign CLM, an administrator can choose language settings for the account. These
settings help you customize the product to meet the language needs of users. You can
control which languages are enabled in the account. You can also assign the default
language for the account.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Select the Languages Enabled in this Account drop-down,
   and select the checkboxes for the languages that you want to enable.

   Note: If a language gets disabled, users with that
   language selected get assigned the default account language.
5. Select the Default Account Language drop-down, and
   select the default account language for users.
6. Select Save.

The product saves your settings. You finished setting the
language settings for the Docusign CLM user interface.

title: Document Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about the tools for document management available to administrators in Docusign CLM. Administrators can manage document attributes, eForms, and uploads.

Administrators in Docusign CLM have several tools and features available to help manage
documents. Administrators can use these tools to manage document uploads and document
attributes in Docusign CLM.

Administrators can also customize the user experience as part of document management.
Menus and actions can be tailored to reduce confusion and increase efficiency for
document workflows.

title: Two-Step Verification in CLM.CM
text:
Read about two-step verification in CLM.CM. Administrators and users can manage two-step
verification to add more control over security for the account.

In CLM.CM accounts, administrators and users can manage two-step user verification. Using this
verification can give your organization more control over security for the account.

Administrators can enable and disable two-step verification for the entire account. If an
administrator has not enabled two-step verification at the account level, users can
manage the verification for their profiles.

Note: For a Docusign CLM account, the Docusign administrator can enable and
disable two-factor authentication for the entire organization. For more information,
see [Two-Step Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=txt1578456659215.html&_LANG=enus) and [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Start Workflow Agreement Action in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Start Workflow Agreement Action in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the Start Workflow Agreement Action in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use
this agreement action to pass envelope metadata to CLM as custom XML data from
eSignature.

When you select Start Workflow as part of your Agreement Rule configuration, your
Docusign eSignature admin account passes envelope metadata to CLM as custom XML data. In
Add Actions, select **Start Workflow**, your Docusign CLM account type, and the
desired workflow.

![Agreement Actions in CLM 3](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Agreement-Actions-in-CLM-3.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTkwMTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.YZu81YBBBMHVgWZhc7XbD26xwKsEGXIyC8RxIA_U4SU&_LANG=enus "Agreement Actions in CLM 3")

When setting up your workflow, you can use the Evaluate an XPath workflow step in CLM
Workflow Designer to grab specific metadata from the XML to update document attributes
and complete other post-signature tasks.

In the following workflow example, the custom XML data contains two text fields, Employee
ID and Department, that are pulled into CLM from the envelope data delivered by
Agreement Actions.![Agreement Actions in CLM 4](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Agreement-Actions-in-CLM-4.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTkwMTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.YZu81YBBBMHVgWZhc7XbD26xwKsEGXIyC8RxIA_U4SU&_LANG=enus "Agreement Actions in CLM 4")

To grab the specific meta-data in this example, the workflow uses a series of
*Evaluate an XPath* steps to look for the relevant data in the XML. Listed
below are the XPaths to some of the common fields in eSignature Documents:

- **Recipient Name:**

  */Params/envelope/recipients/signers/item/name/text()*
- **Envelope Custom
  Field**

  */Params/DocuSignEnvelopeInformation/EnvelopeStatus/CustomFields*
- **Document Custom
  Field:**

  */Params/envelope/recipients/signers/item/tabs*
- **Template Name:**

  */Params/envelope/templates*
- **Document
  Name:**

  */Params/envelope/envelopeDocuments/item[order/text()=1]/name/text()*

  For
  information on Workflow Designer, see [Docusign CLM Workflows Overview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=uxf1651607363522.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Choose a Delivery Method for Notifications in CLM.CM
heading_path: Choose a Delivery Method for Notifications in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to configure a notification delivery method to receive activity alerts in
CLM.CM. Enabling notifications allows users to receive alerts by real-time emails or a
single digest.

Users can choose a notification delivery method to
receive alerts about activity in CLM.CM. Notifications can be received as real-time
emails or a single digest.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To
determine which kind of CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM.
2. Select the Profile icon ![Profile icon for CLM.CM](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Person.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3MTIsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.LmhFW0qXyYLDYaME9dpGPJ3vaZIYOLWtPCmsiEwfXr8&_LANG=enus "Profile icon for CLM.CM"), and select My Preferences.

   The My Preferences page opens.
3. Select Notification Preferences.
4. Select the check boxes to configure your notification preferences.
5. Select Save.

   You have successfully set up notification preferences for your account
   in CLM.CM.

title: Schedules in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about schedules in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use schedules for recurring
and explicit document management events in Docusign CLM.

Schedules define specific or recurring dates and times used by other Docusign CLM features,
such as rules or reports. There are two kinds of schedules in Docusign CLM:

- Explicit schedules allow for specified dates and times.
- Recurring schedules allow you to specify a day and time relative to the hour,
  week, or month.

Administrators should use schedules to run smart rules on an explicit date and time, or as a
recurring event. The scheduled smart rule can then execute a variety of actions according to
the selected schedule, including:

- Sending an email
- Starting a workflow
- Sending a custom report
- Moving one or more documents to the trash

Note: For more information about smart rules, see the documentation about
[smart rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qaz1642962729191&topicId=yhx1576610044302.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Convert an eForm from Public to Private in CLM.CM
heading_path: Convert an eForm from Public to Private in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to convert a public eForm into a private eForm in CLM.CM.

In CLM.CM, all eForms are private by default. If you
have public eForms, super administrators can convert those eForms from public to private.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To determine which kind
of CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a super administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select eForms under Automation Tools.
4. Select the checkbox for the eForm that you want to modify.
5. Select Access > Make Private.

   You have converted a public eForm into a private eForm.

title: Account Security in CLM.CM
text:
Read about features and settings for account security in CLM.CM.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To find out which version of CLM you
have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM
Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

In CLM.CM, super administrators can set options for security. Options are available for IP
address-based access, password strength, session timeouts, and more.

Super administrators can also set sharing options, enable two-factor authentication, and enable
protections from exploits such as clickjacking.

Security settings can be applied on a per-user or user group basis, allowing for more
granular control.

The following security options are available to super administrators in CLM.CM:

Session Timeout
:   Determines how long a user can be logged in and idle before being logged out of
    CLM.CM.

IP Address Restriction
:   Sets a list of IP network addresses that are allowed to access CLM.CM. For more
    information about IP restrictions, see [Set IP Access Restrictions in CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=uyo1684439213435.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to restrict access to CLM.CM by IP address.").

Passwords
:   Prpvides the following password control:

    - Controls whether to allow password login for SSO users
    - Controls the settings for password strength, expiration, and reuse

2-Step Verification
:   Sets options for setting up two-factor authentication as part of user login. For
    more information about setting up this verification, see [Two-Factor Authentication in Docusign CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=rnu1684267366708.html&_LANG=enus "Read about two-factor authentication for login in Docusign CLM.CM. Super Administrators can enable two-factor authentication in Docusign CLM.CM as an extra layer of login security.").

Clickjack Protection
:   Prevents CLM.CM from being run in a frame, unless that frame is from a safelisted domain.
    Super administrators can set up a list of domains that can run CLM.CM in a
    frame.

Salesforce Authentication
:   Enables users to access CLM.CM by using their Salesforce credentials.

Log In Agreement
:   (When enabled) Prompts users to accept a configured agreement before proceeding to use
    CLM.CM. Super administrators can also set options for login restrictions.

Sharing
:   Controls whether users can publicly share documents and folders with individuals who are not
    members of your CLM.CM organization. You can also view all public documents and
    folders.

title: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
text:
Look up operating system requirements and browser support for Docusign CLM.

The following sections describe the operating system requirements and supported browsers
for Docusign CLM.

Note:

- While access to Docusign CLM is possible through mobile browsers,
  there is no dedicated Docusign CLM app for Android or iOS. Depending
  on the settings selected by your Docusign CLM Administrator, some
  Document Preview and Docusign CLM features may not function as
  intended in mobile browsers.
- Any inbound or outbound connection with Docusign CLM must use the TLS 1.2 encryption
  protocol. This requirement impacts all methods of communicating with the Docusign CLM
  platform.

title: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
PC Requirements (Docusign CLM and Desktop Applications)
text:
PC requirements are as follows:

- Microsoft Windows 10 or newer
- Microsoft .NET Framework 4.0 for Microsoft Windows
- Visual C++ Runtime Libraries for Microsoft Windows

title: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
Mac Operating System Requirements (Docusign CLM and Desktop Applications)
text:
Mac operating system requirements are as follows:

- MacOS 10.14 (Mojave) or newer

title: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Operating System Requirements and Browser Support for Docusign CLM
text:
Docusign recommends using the latest version of the following supported browsers:

- Google Chrome
- Mozilla Firefox
- Apple Safari
- Microsoft Edge

title: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to select and markup a finished document for document generation template
creation.

title: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
text:
Here are some important
considerations to make when selecting a document for template mapping:

- The document selected to become a template should be a finalized contract,
  agreement, or previously completed template.
- The document should have easily identifiable areas for markup. This enables you
  and your CLM implementation expert to create a template map from those
  fields.

Each template for document generation is created from a
mapped document. Mapping a document identifies the parts of a document that will use
merged data to change selected fields within contracts. 

Once you have selected a document
for mapping, the CLM team will guide you through the process of
markup.

1. Open the document for mapping.
2. In the document, highlight a section of the document that will become a
   field.
3. Right-click the highlighted section, and select New
   Comment.

   A comment field displays in the right-side margin of the document.
4. In the comment field, provide a reference or doc number, a field name, and a
   brief description of the field. Here are a few examples.

   - For a field that will contain a contractor's name: **“01 - Contractor -
     Contractor Name”**
   - For a field that will be linked to a Salesforce object: **“02 - Product
     Type - Salesforce - Quote.ProductLineItems.ProductType\_\_c”**
   - For a section that will be conditionally hidden or shown: **“03 - Gov
     Clause - Show Government Furnished Clause if ‘Government Furnished?’
     Drop-Down = True”**

   Note: If a field is re-used,
   it should still be associated with the initial number used in the
   markup.
5. Repeat this process for any other fields that will need to be mapped for
   templating throughout the document. Save the document with a name indicating
   that it has been marked up for templating.

title: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM
text: - [Make a Template Map Spreadsheet for Document Generation in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ogg1631728002348.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a template map spreadsheet for a document generation template.")

title: Manage Devices for Users in CLM.CM
heading_path: Manage Devices for Users in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to manage and revoke access for devices in CLM.CM.

CLM.CM applications can be used to download documents
to a device. Super administrators can manage access for these devices in CLM.CM.

Super
administrators can see the devices that users have used to access CLM.CM. The
administrators can also see the activities performed. Where necessary, super
administrators can revoke access by application for a user's devices.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To find out which version of CLM
you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Device Management under Account Management.

   The Device Management page opens. This page is
   where you manage devices for users.
4. Choose the user whose devices you want to manage:
   1. Specify a user name or email address in the Select a
      User field, and select the user from the dropdown
      results.
   2. Select Select.

   The product displays a list of the selected user's devices.
5. Select your options:

   - To revoke access for this user device, select
     Revoke.
   - To see the activity performed on this user device, select See
     Activity.
   - To revoke access for all devices for the user, select Revoke
     All.

   Note:
   - Revoke requests are processed silently. Revoking access automatically deletes all local
     content and logs the user out of the application.
   - If a user tries to reauthorize after access is revoked, the user gets notified that
     access is revoked for that device.

   You are finished managing user devices in CLM.CM.

title: Access a Public eForm in Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Access a Public eForm in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to access public eForms in Docusign CLM.CM.

In Docusign CLM.CM, public eForms are published forms
that are available to the general public. Recipients can access, complete, and submit the
eForm through the internet.

Note:

- This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account
  you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
  Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).
- New eForms can no longer be made public. Public eForms that have already been
  published will continue to work without interruption.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.CM as a Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM admin panel displays.
3. Navigate to Automation Tools  >  eForms.
4. In the Access menu, select Show Public
   Links.

   The public links screen displays.
5. Copy the URL, and paste it into a web browser to access your eForm.
6. Complete all parts of the eForm, including the reCAPTCHA at the end of the form.
7. Select Save to submit the eForm.

   You have successfully accessed and completed a public eForm.

title: Update Users in Bulk in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Update Users in Bulk in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to update Docusign CLM users in bulk. Administrators can perform a bulk update on users by using a CSV file.

Administrators can upload a CSV with CLM-specific user fields to update CLM users in bulk. Fields that you can update include Persona, ManagedBy, DelegatedPermissions, and MailingLists.

Note: This article applies to Docusign CLM. You cannot use this bulk update feature to add new users.

The product uses the CSV's Email field to locate users to update. If a user email cannot be found, the product skips that row in the CSV.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Users under User Management.
4. Select Bulk update.

   A Bulk User Update dialog box displays.
5. Select Export CSV.

   A CSV template file downloads to your computer.
6. Complete user details in the CSV, and upload the updated CSV file.
7. Select Submit in the Bulk User Update dialog box.

The CSV file is uploaded. You receive an email when users get updated. The email contains details of any changes.

title: Dashboards in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about dashboards and dashboard widgets in Docusign CLM. Administrators and users
can use dashboards to consolidate and organize information.

In Docusign CLM, administrators and users can use dashboards to consolidate and organize
information.

Administrators can create shared dashboards to control the visibility of folders, reports,
and document-linked tasks. Shared dashboards can also be applied globally for an entire
Docusign CLM account.

Users can use personal dashboards and widgets for visibility on document information and
reports.

title: Unique Usernames
text:
Read about usernames in Docusign CLM. Administrators can create a user and set the
username, which can be associated only with a single Docusign CLM account.

On August 25, 2017, Docusign CLM introduced a change that affects customers who are in
multiple Docusign CLM accounts. The change to how usernames are managed provides better
security and helps ensure that the product can provide more enterprise capabilities.

Each username can now only be associated with a single Docusign CLM account. Usernames must
be unique across an entire Docusign CLM environment. Users in multiple accounts need to log on
to each account separately.

title: Unique Usernames
text:
- Each user will have a unique user name.
- Each user is tied to one and only one Docusign CLM Account.
- The username is a string but does not have to look like an email address.
- Usernames can only begin with an alpha or numeric character.
- The email address must currently be unique in each account. This means only one user can be tied to an email address in the same account.
- Usernames are unique to the Docusign CLM environment.
- Each username must be between 3 and 100 characters.
- Only Super Administrators or User Administrators can create users and set the usernames. End users can not set or choose the username.
- Once the username is set, it cannot be changed.

title: Unique Usernames
text: When you create a new user in Docusign CLM, you will need to also include the username in addition to the email address. The username can be the email address provided that the username/email address is unique. The username must be unique in the entire Docusign CLM environment. If the username is not, an error will be returned and you will need to enter a new username.

title: Unique Usernames
heading_path: How to create a user via the REST API?
text:
When you create a new user via the REST API, then you can pass both the username and the email address. If the username is not provided then the email address will be used as the user name.

If the username is not unique, then an error is returned.

title: Unique Usernames
heading_path: How to create a user via SOAP?
text:
When you create a new user via the SOAP API, then the email address will always be set as the user name. Their is no provision in the SOAP API to pass both the username and the email address.

If the email address is not unique, then an error is returned.

If the user is created, then the username and the email address are the same.

title: Unique Usernames
heading_path: How to create a user via Bulk Import(CSV) ?
text:
When you create a new user in Docusign CLM via Bulk Import, a new column (called Username) has been added.

If you do not add the column to the csv and do not provide a username, then the import uses the email address as the username.

If the username has been added, then the import process uses the username as the username and the email address as the email address.

If the email address or the username is not unique, then an error is returned.

title: Unique Usernames
heading_path: What happened to my username if I'm part of multiple accounts?
text:
If a user is only in one Docusign CLM account, then the email address will be automatically converted to the username.

If a user is in multiple Docusign CLM accounts, then in the default account, the username will be the current email address of the user. All other accounts will include the Account ID for each user. Additionally, an email will be sent to each user that is in multiple accounts with their new username.

Lets take John Smith as an example. John Smith is in 3 different accounts in the Docusign CLM production environment. Here is the account list:

- Acme Production, Account ID 12345 (default)
- Acme Sandbox, Account ID 9899
- Acme Development, Account ID 7674

After the conversion has completed, John will have three unique user names, one for each Docusign CLM Account.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/uniquelogintable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NjEsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.CbX7MrGnhYYFTxLKsg7D-WPDQTBRNH1TvhuidSCxZnw&_LANG=enus)

title: Make a Template Map Spreadsheet for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Make a Template Map Spreadsheet for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to create a template map spreadsheet for a document generation template.

title: Make a Template Map Spreadsheet for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Make a Template Map Spreadsheet for Document Generation in Docusign CLM
text:
[Markup a Document for Document Generation Template Creation in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ngu1631724356690.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select and markup a finished document for document generation template creation.")

Once the markup process is complete, the proposed fields will need to be listed in a template map spreadsheet. This enables your Docusign CLM implementation expert to assist in setting up the document generation template and form.

Note: These features apply to all versions of Docusign CLM. For Docusign CLM Essentials, Docusign Professional Services or your implementing partner will assist in the review and availability of fields.

1. Create a new spreadsheet with the following column headers:

   - Reference or Doc Number
   - Field Name
   - Read Only/Edit
   - Required/Optional
   - Instructional Text (for the user completing the form)
   - Field Type
   - Field Validation Info
   - SFDC Object (for Salesforce integrations with Docusign CLM)
   - SFDC Field Name (for Salesforce integrations with Docusign CLM)
   - SFDC API Name (for Salesforce integrations with Docusign CLM)
   - Additional Notes/Information
2. Enter the information from your marked up document under the appropriate column in the spreadsheet.
3. When finished, save the spreadsheet with a similar name to your marked up document.

A document generation template and document generation form can be created from the mapping template spreadsheet and mapped document.

title: Perform the Tasks of Other Task Group Members in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Perform the Tasks of Other Task Group Members in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to access and perform the tasks of other task group members in Docusign CLM.

In Docusign CLM, an administrator or user can perform a task that is
assigned to another task group member. This process creates more flexibility when
managing tasks and handling business needs.

For example, assume that an approval task is assigned to Jason in the Legal Users
task group. Jason is on vacation. Another user in the group can access the task
directly and complete it.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens.
3. Select a task view.
4. Select the task name.

   The product accesses the task.
5. View and complete the task as needed.

   A message confirms that you completed the task.

You are finished working with the task of another task group
member.

title: Change the Actions Menu Name in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Change the Actions Menu Name in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to change the Actions menu name in Docusign CLM.

Administrators and super administrators can change
the name of the Actions menu in Docusign
CLM.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Navigation & Actions under Account Management.

   The Navigation & Actions page opens.
4. Select the Actions Menu tab.
5. Specify a name in the Actions Menu Name field, and select
   Save.

You have successfully changed the name of the
Actions menu in Docusign CLM.

title: Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable two-step verification for your user profile in CLM.CM.

If an administrator has not enabled two-factor authentication at the account level, you can
enable two-step verification for your user profile. This action does not impact
other users in your account.

1. Log in to your CLM.CM account.
2. Select ![profile icon](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Utilitymenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1NjYsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.Xuh3KQVX2_qmR71X2uSj9-vfCuWZz4JVR1AbIidfrVM&_LANG=enus "profile icon") in the top right corner.
3. Select **My Preferences**.
4. Select **2-Step Verification** in the left pane.

   The 2-Step Verification page opens.
5. Select **On** to enable two-step verification, and select
   Save.

The product saves your setting and logs you out.

title: Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text:
The next time you log in, [set up two-step verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=fnt1585347689093.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up two-step verification as a CLM.CM user.") for your
situation.

title: Custom Clause Libraries in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Custom Clause Libraries in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about clause libraries for Docusign CLM. Administrators can create clause
libraries for use in document generation.

In Docusign CLM, a clause library contains pre-approved language to use during document
creation. An administrator can create a custom clause library. Docusign CLM users can
then access the library in several ways. For example, you can use clause libraries to
create document generation forms in Docusign CLM.

Note: Users can also access CLM clause libraries in Microsoft Word. For more
information on using your Docusign CLM Clause Library in Microsoft Word, see [Docusign CLM Clause Library Add-In for Microsoft
Office](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=alb1642965087302&topicId=ceq1670951972419.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Disable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
heading_path: Disable Two-Step Verification for Your User Profile in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to disable two-step verification for your user profile in CLM.CM.

If you enabled two-step verification for your user profile, you can disable the two-step
verification. This action does not impact other users in your account.

**Note**: Removing your account from your Google Authenticator app does not
turn off two-step verification for your CLM.CM account.

1. Log in to your CLM.CM account.
2. Select ![utility-menu](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Utilitymenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.NcN1WiyTIW53eaghYW9lmAG-K0yFkke2muLQnVAYnvU&_LANG=enus "utility-menu") in the top right corner.
3. Select **My Preferences**.
4. Select 2-Step Verification in the left pane.

   The 2-Step Verification page opens.
5. Select **Off** to disable two-step verification, and select
   Save.

   A dialog box displays, requesting additional information to confirm the
   action.
6. Enter your account password (and a verification code from the Google
   Authenticator app, if necessary), and select
   Continue.

The product disables two-step verification for your user
profile.

title: SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the limitations of SFTP for Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM.

Note: This topic applies to both Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM.

Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM have the following limitations for files, folder structures,
and operations:

title: SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Microsoft Office Naming Convention/Folder Structure Limitations
text:
Microsoft Office limits the number of characters used in folder path and file names when
transferring documents. Documents and folder structures with long file names may create
difficulty when uploading to Docusign CLM. To avoid this issue, reduce the number of
folders, and shorten document names where possible. Character length limits are as follows
for the following Microsoft Office applications:

- Microsoft Word: 254 characters, including file name extension.
- Microsoft PowerPoint: 258 characters, including file name extension.
- Microsoft Access: 258 characters, including file name extension.
- Microsoft Outlook: 258 characters, including file name extension.
- Microsoft Excel: 218 characters, including file name extension.

title: SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM
text:
While SFTP is a secure file transfer protocol, there may be potential isses with firewall
rules. Firewalls may need to be reconfigured to enable SFTP transfers. Please consult with
your IT administrators for configuration.

title: SFTP Limitations in Docusign CLM
text:
Your SFTP client may display date and time stamps that differ from the actual Docusign CLM
system date and time. This is a known issue that is unique to the SFTP client being used. To
confirm the actual modification dates and times on documents or folders, please view the
details in Docusign CLM.

title: Define a Group's Security Access to a Folder
heading_path: Define a Group's Security Access to a Folder
text:
Learn how to give a group access to a folder. Certain administrators or users with Set
Access rights can set up the group's security access to the folder.

title: Define a Group's Security Access to a Folder
heading_path: Define a Group's Security Access to a Folder
text:
To manage security on a folder, you must be authorized as
follows:

- (CLM.CM) Super administrator or user who is granted Set Access rights to the
  folder.
- (Docusign CLM) a CLM administrator

You can give a group access to folders and documents within those folders. You select the
level of security access for the group.

1. Select Documents in the navigation header.
2. Navigate to the folder for which you want to define access.
3. Select the box next to the folder.
4. Select Manage > Security.

   The Folder Security page opens.
5. Type the group name in the text field at the bottom, and select the name from the
   results drop-down list.

   Tip: You can also select the name from the address
   book.

   The product adds the name to the table of user roles and assigned security
   settings.
6. Select a security level from the Security drop-down list for the group.

   Members of this group will have this security level access.
7. Select Save.

You are finished setting up the folder access for the
group.

title: Component Packaging in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about component packaging in Docusign CLM. Component packaging can be used in
Docusign CLM to move some account settings to a new account, making setup easier.

Docusign CLM account components can be packaged for transfer to other Docusign Accounts.
Packaging is often used to move configurations from one account to another. Administrators can
use Component Packaging to move components between UAT and Production environments.

Component packaging is meant to transfer components rather than a whole Docusign CLM account.
Packaging is not meant to migrate a mass amount of content, such as documents, from one
account to another.

title: Manage Tasks for Managed Users in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Manage Tasks for Managed Users in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to manage tasks for managed users in Docusign CLM.

title: Manage Tasks for Managed Users in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Manage Tasks for Managed Users in Docusign CLM
text:
Select Enable updated task management
page on the Account Settings page. The updated page has a
more modern layout.

You can claim, unassign, and reassign tasks for users who have your login set as their
profile's Managed By attribute.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The task management page opens. Any tasks for managed users are available in the
   Managed Tasks view.
3. Select the Managed Tasks view.

   The product displays the task view.
4. Select the checkbox for one or more tasks, select any of the following actions, and follow any prompts to complete the
   activity:

   Claim
   :   Removes the task from the user and assigns the task to you.

   Reassign
   :   Opens a selection window where you can assign a new owner.

   Unassign
   :   Removes the task assignment from the user.

You have completed the task management activity for a managed
user.

title: List of Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: List of Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM
text:
Look up delegated permission values and definitions. Administrators can assign limited
administrative permissions through delegated permissions in CLM.

The following delegated permissions can be assigned to users by CLM administrators.

| Permission | Administrative Area | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Document Generation | Automation Tools | Allows a user to create, edit, and delete in the document generation tool. |
| Object Mapping (not available for CLM.CM) | Automation Tools | Allows configuration and mapping of artificial intelligence (AI) tools. |
| Workflow & Automation | Automation Tools | Allows a user to create, edit, and delete the following components:  - Workflows - Smart rules - Schedules - Forms - Reminder templates   Note: The workflow engine has unrestricted access to the document repository; however,users with this permission still respect existing folder security for the CLM User permission profile (Docusign CLM) or Full Subscriber permission profile (CLM.CM). Any membership in groups also continues to be respected. |
| UI Preferences | Account Management | Allows a user to change account branding, navigation, and actions. |
| Security | Account Management | Allows a user to change account-level security settings. These settings include two-step verification, logon restrictions, SAML SSO, and password strengths. |
| Attributes & Auto Numbers | Documents & Folders | Allows a user to configure document and folder attribute fields. These fields include Attribute Groups, Auto Numbers, and Attribute Import Mappings. |
| Inbound Folder Content | Documents & Folders | Allows a user to manage inbound folder content from fax and email. This content includes inbound email addresses and inbound fax lines. |
| Create, Edit and Delete Any Report | Reporting | Allows a user to create, edit, and delete reports within the account. |
| Integrations | Integrations | Allows a user to administer settings for third-party integration configurations, including Salesforce, electronic signature, and REST API. |
| Users | User Management | Allows a user to create, edit, and delete users and task groups in the address book. |

title: Create a Component Package in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create a Component Package in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to package Docusign CLM account components for transfer to
another CLM account. Folders and attribute groups are examples of components that an
adminisrator can package.

Docusign CLM account components can
be packaged and transferred to other CLM accounts. Folders and attribute groups are examples
of components that an adminisrator can package.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Deployment under Account Management.
4. Provide a name for your package in the Name field.
5. (Optional) Provide a description for your package in the
   Description text box.
6. Choose your options for the package by selecting the checkboxes:

   - Auto Install Package
   - Overwrite existing objects when installing package
   - Include account settings
   - Include account branding preferences
   - Include account-level customization of navigation tabs, menus and
     toolbars
7. Select the component tabs, and select components to be transferred in the
   package.

   Note:
   - Documents and security settings can be included with folders. To do so, select the
     checkboxes for Include documents and Include group
     security on the Folders tab.
   - On the Forms tab, you can select a checkbox to include form
     documents from a selected eForm’s submit folders. If no submit folder is selected,
     the destination account’s root folder becomes the submit folder for the transferred
     form. The properties of each eForm will be displayed in the tab.
8. Select Choose Storage Location to select a location for your
   component package.
9. Select Save.

   The product saves a file with a .spackage extension and places the file in the
   storage location. You have created a component package for transferring data to another
   CLM account.

title: Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn more about each of the document types in Docusign CLM.

Docusign CLM has three broad categories for document types: standalone documents, amendments,
and supplements. Knowing these categories and the document types in each category
can help administrators determine alternate names and visibility.

title: Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
These are the primary documents in an agreement. They can be related to other
standalone documents, or be modified by agreements. By default, these document types
are classified as standalone documents:

- Master Service Agreement
- Memorandum of Understanding
- Terms and Conditions
- Letter of Intent
- Quote
- Purchase Order
- Proposal
- Order Form
- Statement of Work
- License
- Service Level Agreement
- Distribution
- Engagement Letter
- Fee
- Lease
- Retainer
- Nondisclosure Agreement
- Services Agreement
- Other

title: Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
These documents serve to modify another document in an agreement within the same
party, and are not standalone documents. By default, these document types are
classified as amendments:

- Addendum
- Amendment
- Change Order

title: Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
These documents give additional information or context to an agreement. Typically,
they are viewed and acknowledged, but not signed. By default, these document types
are classified as supplements:

- Appendix
- Exhibit
- Attachment
- Supplemental Document

title: Enable User Account Notifications in Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable User Account Notifications in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable notifications to receive alerts about user account activity.
Administrators can receive notifications about alerts by email.

Administrators can enable notifications to receive
alerts about user account activity. Administrators receive alerts when users enable, disable,
or change their Permission Profile.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.CM as a Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM.CM admin panel displays.
3. Navigate to Account Management  >  Account Settings  >  Notifications.
4. Select your options for the notifications you want to receive.
5. Once all options have been chosen, select Save.

   You have successfully setup notifications for user activity in Docusign CLM.CM.

title: Assign Delegated Permissions to a User in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Assign Delegated Permissions to a User in Docusign CLM
text: Learn how to use delegated permissions to assign administrative permissions. Administrators can use these steps to create delegated permissions and enable them for Docusign CLM users.

title: Assign Delegated Permissions to a User in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Assign Delegated Permissions to a User in Docusign CLM
text:
[Create Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=hyn1576610087430.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use delegated permissions to assign administrative permissions. Administrators can use these steps to create delegated permissions and enable them for Docusign CLM users.")

Administrators can assign delegated permissions to users. These permissions give users limited administrative abilities in Docusign CLM. Delegated permissions give your organization flexibility when managing work in CLM.

Note:

- In Docusign CLM, administrators can assign delegated permissions only to users with the CLM User permission profile.
- In CLM.CM, super administrators can assign delegated permissions only to CLM users with the Full Subscriber permission profile.
- To see what CLM account type you have, see the topic [Identify Your CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&bundleId=dsa1643667928464&_LANG=enus).

1. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
2. Select Users under User Management.

   The Users page opens.
3. Select a user to assign delegated permissions to.
4. Select a permission from the Delegated Permission drop-down list.
5. Select Save.

The delegated permission is now assigned to the user.

title: Assign a CLM User to a Persona
heading_path: Assign a CLM User to a Persona
text:
Learn how to assign a CLM user to a persona. Administrators can use personas to help
simplify a user's experience and actions while working in the application.

title: Assign a CLM User to a Persona
heading_path: Assign a CLM User to a Persona
text:
Create a group and name it after the role that you want. For
example, you can name it *Accounts Payable Processor*.

CLM administrators use personas to create a unique display in the interface for menu,
toolbar, and navigation options. Personas can help simplify a user's experience and
actions while working in the application. In a user's profile, you can assign a
single persona.

1. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
2. Select Users under User
   Management.

   The Users page opens.
3. Select the user to assign the persona to.

   The product displays the user's details.
4. Type the name of the group in the User Persona
   field.

You have assigned a CLM user to a persona. You can now create a
persona view for the Documents page in CLM.

title: Disable an eForm in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Disable an eForm in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to disable eForms in Docusign CLM that that are obsolete or
unneeded.

Administrators and Super Administrators can disable
eForms that that are obsolete or unneeded.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an administrator or Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM admin panel displays.
3. Navigate to Automation Tools  >  eForms.
4. Select the checkbox for the eForm you want to disable.
5. In the Access menu, select Disable
   Submission.

   You have successfully disabled the selected eForm.

title: Security Access Levels in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Security Access Levels in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about each of the options for security access available in Docusign CLM.
Administrators and Super Administrators can set security on a per-user or per-user group
basis.

Note: This topic applies to both Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM.

In Docusign CLM and Docusign CLM.CM, administrators have several options for tailoring
security permissions to their needs. Administrators can use granular options to set security
on a per-user, per-user group, or per-folder basis.

Unless otherwise specified, documents and sub-folders inherit the security settings
established at the parent folder level. When viewing the security settings of sub-folders or
documents, you will see that most security settings are noted as inherited. Administrators can
select the checkbox for View Permissions for All Users to see the users
who have access to a specific folder or document. Viewing permissions this way will return the
first 1,000 results. By default, administrators and Super Administrators will have access to
all documents and folders.

When applying security, you can set the following access rights for each user or user
group:

- No Access: prohibits users from accessing folders or documents.
- View: permits users to view folders and documents through Document
  Review or download.
- View & Create: permits users to view a folder and upload and
  revise the content of a document. Users with these access rights cannot change document
  attributes.
- View & Edit: permits users to view, revise, share, and upload
  new content. Users with these access rights cannot delete the folders, documents, or grant
  access to folders or documents. This is the default access setting for all content in Docusign CLM.
- View, Edit, & Delete: permits users to view, revise, share,
  upload new content, delete documents, and delete folders. Users with these access rights
  cannot grant access to other folders.
- View, Edit, Delete, & Set Access: permits users to view, edit,
  share, upload, grant or revoke access to folders and documents.

Folder security can be applied based on a permission profile, applied to an individual user,
or applied for a user group:

- Permission Profiles: allows you to grant or revoke access based on
  the user's permission profile. The core permission profiles can have security access
  delegations of user and viewer. Administrators have access to all documents and folders in
  the account by default.
- Users: users that need explicit access granted or revoked can be
  added to a user group that has the desired security settings.
- Groups: groups that need explicit access granted or revoked can be
  added to a user group that has the desired security settings.

Note: Explicitly granted permissions in Docusign CLM win
over group permissions in the event that a group inherited permission excludes or includes
permissions. If a user was granted or revoked access to a particular folder or sub-folder, the
explicitly granted permission will override group access.

title: Manage Support User Permissions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Manage Support User Permissions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to manage Docusign Support user access
permissions.

As a CLM administrator, you can manage permissions for
the Docusign Support user.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Users under User Management.
4. Select the user named Docusign Support.
5. Make your changes to User Persona, Group
   Memberships, and Managed By.
6. Select Save.

   You have changed permissions for the Docusign Support
   user.

title: Task Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about task management options available to administrators, managers, and users in
Docusign CLM.

The task management page is the central inbox for user tasks in Docusign
CLM. Users access their tasks by selecting Tasks in the navigation
header.

The following task management activities are available in Docusign CLM:

- [Enable the Updated Task Management Page in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=dtn1607108809778.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Enable Task Completion Screens in Docusign CLM or CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=abc1607108732190.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Show or Hide Task Management Columns in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=laj1576610075029.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Reassign a Task in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bpn1576610076260.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reassign a task in Docusign CLM.")
- [Unassign a Task in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=iyt1576610076022.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to unassign a task in Docusign CLM.")
- [Claim a Task in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=fwe1736958719989.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to claim a task in Docusign CLM.")
- [Manage Tasks for Managed Users in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ozk1576610075763.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to manage tasks for managed users in Docusign CLM.")
- [Perform the Tasks of Other Task Group Members in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=olp1736970700987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access and perform the tasks of other task group members in Docusign CLM.")
