- [View Unassigned Tasks and Assign Them in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ayh1727214998188.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view and make assignments for unassigned tasks in Docusign CLM. Task group members can access an Unassigned view to see unassigned tasks, claim tasks, and assign tasks.")
- [Enable or Disable Unified Task Management in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=cqr1750686934502.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=vqr1746802622033.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to access the unified task view from Docusign CLM. Administrators and users can use this central view to manage tasks across Docusign applications.")

title: Functions of Email Addresses in DocuSign CLM
heading_path: Functions of Email Addresses in DocuSign CLM
text:
Read about the usage, functions, and limitations of email addresses in DocuSign
CLM.

In DocuSign CLM, a user’s email address is a unique identifier that connects the user to a
database. Users can be added to groups and assigned tasks in CLM by using their email
addresses. Every user must have a unique email address.

Changing a user’s credentials affects DocuSign CLM functions as follows:

- Folder permissions for users should ideally be connected to groups in DocuSign CLM. To manage
  folder access, users can be added or removed from groups. If permissions are
  connected to an email address, changes to that email address interrupt folder
  access.
- Email address changes affect the assignment of workflow steps.

  Tip: We recommend assigning workflow steps to groups or task groups
  instead of users.
- If a task gets assigned to a user whose email address has changed, the task might become
  unusable.

  Tip: Assign tasks to groups when
  possible.
- If users are managed through Salesforce, the email address of a Salesforce user and CLM user
  should match. If the email address is mismatched, the CLM File It iframe might
  become unusable. The user might also lose access to advanced features like document
  generation. Administrators can use User Sync for CLM.CM or user management for
  DocuSign CLM.
  - **User Sync for CLM.CM:** If User Sync is enabled, Salesforce users are
    synced with CLM every 15 minutes. In cases of email mismatch, the old user
    list gets deactivated and replaced by a new user list.

    Note: Users that
    get created by User Sync do not have a username assigned. These users
    cannot log on to CLM.CM from login.springcm.com.
  - **User Management for DocuSign CLM:** DocuSign CLM allows two users with
    the same email address for DocuSign applications (eSignature). This allows
    Salesforce users to see and view the DocuSign CLM File It widget. Users must
    be created and enabled through the User Management page of the DocuSign Apps Launcher (DAL). This page is
    available through the DocuSign Setup page in the DAL.
    If a user changes his or her email address in Salesforce, a new CLM user
    must be created. The old CLM user must be deactivated. The new CLM user must
    be assigned manually to groups and task groups.
- In DocuSign CLM, users can get provisioned by SSO at the organizational administrator level.
  When this happens, administrators should use their SSO provisioning procedure to
  change a user’s email address.
- In CLM.CM, users can get provisioned through SSO. In this situation, changing a user’s email
  address creates a new user. The old user account and license remain active.

title: Enable Notifications for a Folder or Document in Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable Notifications for a Folder or Document in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable notifications for a folder or document. Users can enable
notifications to receive alerts about folder or document activity.

Users can enable notifications for a folder or
document in Docusign CLM.CM. Enabling notifications allows users to receive alerts about
folder or document activity.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM.
To determine which kind of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.CM.
2. Select Documents from in the navigation header.

   The Documents screen displays, listing all
   documents.
3. Select the checkbox for the folder or document you want to receive notifications
   for.

   Note: Selecting a folder or document
   that already has notifications enabled disables notifications.
4. Select Manage  >  Enable Notification.

   You have successfully enabled notifications for a document or folder in Docusign CLM.CM. A modal displays indicating the change in notifications for the folder or
   document.

title: Group Management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Group Management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Read about group management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM. Administrators can create and
modify groups of CLM users. Specifying access through groups can help simplify security
access.

A group is a collection of CLM users. Often, these users have similar document access needs.
A group has defined access rights to folders. Administrators can create and modify groups.
Specifying access through groups (instead of user role or individuals) simplifies the security
access process.

Note: A group contains licensed user accounts and cannot have
contacts.

Docusign recommends using groups for managing document and folder access in your CLM account.
A group often contains users who serve department needs or project teams. For example, the
group might have users for Human Resources or Sales.

The way that administrators manage groups depends on the CLM account type:

- For Docusign CLM account types, you manage groups from Docusign Admin. For more
  information about managing groups from Docusign Admin, see [Groups](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fza1583359190504.html&_LANG=enus).
- For CLM.CM account types, you manage groups on the Groups page in the
  Admin section of the CLM application.

Note: To see what CLM account type you have, see the topic [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Create an Organization from eSignature Admin
heading_path: Create an Organization from eSignature Admin
text:
Learn how to create an organization from eSignature Admin. Creating this organization
allows administrators to manage CLM users and groups and manage other CLM
settings.

title: Create an Organization from eSignature Admin
heading_path: Create an Organization from eSignature Admin
text:
You must be a Docusign eSignature account administrator with
the *All Administration Capabilities* permissions. The account that you use to
create an organization must be enabled with Docusign Admin.

To create a dedicated CLM
account in Docusign Admin, you must create an organization. Creating this
organization gives you the ability to create and manage CLM users and groups. You
can also manage other CLM settings.

1. Log in to Docusign eSignature.
2. Select **Admin** in the header.
3. Select **Get Started** to launch the organization setup process.

   ![eSignature Admin - Create an Org](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/eSignature-Admin--Create-an-Org.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1MDYsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.5wZ9ZAACACjgtokDQ_OjKaAUfkZZf-yAGTqfjIF0SiI&_LANG=enus "eSignature Admin - Create an Org")

   Note: New Docusign customers might not see
   the Get Started widget during the first log on to eSignature Admin. If not,
   log off and log back in to refresh the server. Existing Docusign customers
   might not see the Getting Started widget. If not, clear your cache and then
   log back in to eSignature.
4. Enter a name in the Organization Name field.
5. (Optional) Enter a description for the organization.
6. Select Next.
7. Select the accounts that you want to add under this organization.

   Note: The account that you
   used to create the organization is automatically linked. Select any
   additional accounts that you want to manage centrally through your
   organization. The list shows all accounts for which you are an account
   administrator with All Administration Capabilities permissions. You can link
   additional accounts at any time.
8. Select Create.

The product creates the organization and links your selected
accounts.

title: Customize Document Actions in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Document Actions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to customize the Document Actions menu in Docusign CLM.

Administrators and Super Administrators can customize the Document Actions menu in
Docusign CLM. Options for document actions can be added or removed based on user
profile. Administrators can also create custom actions for document management,
providing users with shortcuts to common operations.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an administrator or Super Administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM administrator panel displays.
3. Navigate to Account Management  >  Navigation and Actions.
4. Select a user persona from the Who is this for? dropdown
   menu.

   Note: The Account Default option
   will set Document Actions for all Docusign CLM users.
5. In the dropdown menu for What are you customizing?,
   select Document Management Actions.
6. In the section for Default Actions, select the document
   actions you want to enable or disable. You can search for actions by entering
   them in the search field.
7. (Optional) Select Custom Actions to create a custom
   action for the Document Actions menu.
   1. Enter a name for your Document Actions submenu in the
      Toolbar Submenu Name field.
   2. Select New Action.

      The Add New Custom Action screen
      displays.
   3. Enter a name for your custom action in the Action
      Name field.
   4. (Optional) Enter a description for your custom action.
   5. Select the display language for your custom action in the
      Language for this action dropdown menu.
   6. Select an action from the What should this action
      do? dropdown menu.
   7. Choose how your action should display from the Open this
      action in: dropdown menu.
   8. (Optional) Select the checkbox for Pass user information
      with this action to record user data for the information
      transaction for this action.
   9. Once all required and optional fields have been selected and entered,
      select Save.
   10. Select the toggle to enable or disable your custom action.

       Note: Custom actions can be reordered by dragging
       and dropping them in the desired order.
8. Once all options have been selected, select Save.

   You have successfully customized the Document Actions menu in Docusign CLM. Users
   will see the changes the next time they log on to Docusign CLM.

title: eForms in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about eForms in Docusign CLM. eForms are custom HTML forms that administrators
can build into Docusign CLM. These forms provide a way to replace paper processes with web-based
forms.

Note: You can no longer make new eForms public. Published public eForms
will continue to work without interruption.

eForms are custom HTML forms that administrators can build into Docusign CLM. These forms
provide a way to replace paper processes with web-based forms. You can store any submitted
eForm in a folder, or include the eForm in an advanced workflow step. You can include fields
from your attribute groups in an eForm. Selecting attributes automatically generates the HTML
code for your eForm.

Administrators and Super Administrators can determine whether eForms are private or public.
Private eForms are available only to Docusign CLM users. Private eForms are typically used
within a company between users who have acces to Docusign CLM.

Public eForms are available to anyone with internet access. Public eForms are mostly used
between a company and its customers and vendors. Public eForms include a URL that can be
opened in a web browser. A Docusign CLM license is not required to complete a public
eForm.

title: Remove Public Share Links on a Folder in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Remove Public Share Links on a Folder in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to disable public share links on a folder that you previously shared in Docusign CLM.

Docusign CLM administrators can
create public share links to allow non-CLM users to view documents and content on your
account. If a public share link is no longer needed, a Docusign CLM
administrator can remove it.

Note: You must be a Docusign CLM administrator or have delegated permissions to create
public share links. For more information about delegated permissions, see [Limited Administrative Permissions with
Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The folder list displays.
3. Select the drop-down menu for the folder, and select Send  >  Share Link.

   A window opens that gives you access to any public links.
4. Select View Active Links.

   Another window opens to display any public links that exist.
5. Remove the public share link:
   1. Select the X icon for the public link that you
      want to remove
   2. Select Continue to disable public access to the
      link.

      The product removes the link.

You have removed a public share link for a folder in Docusign CLM.

title: Two-Factor Authentication in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Read about two-factor authentication for login in Docusign CLM.CM. Super Administrators
can enable two-factor authentication in Docusign CLM.CM as an extra layer of login
security.

Super Administrators can setup and manage two-factor authentication for user accounts in
Docusign CLM.CM. This gives your organization more control over the level of login
security in Docusign CLM.CM. Administrators can enable and disable two-factor
authentication for the entire account.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind
of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your
Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Docusign CLM Trash Folder Management
text:
Read about the trash folder. To manage this folder, administrators can remove all trash,
set retention policies, and create schedules and smart rules.

When CLM users delete files and folders, these items get sent to the trash folder. The trash
folder contains all CLM content that has been deleted but not permanently removed.

The following list describes important information about the trash folder:

- Users can delete only CLM files and folders that they have View, Edit & Delete access
  to.
- Items in the trash do not display in search results.
- Deleted content moves to a trash folder that is named for the deletion date (in
  YYYYMMDD format).
- Deleted folders maintain their original structure. Parent folders and subfolders keep the
  same grouping.
- Users with View, Edit & Delete access can view and restore files and folders that are in
  the trash.

  Note: Items moved to the Trash folder will retain
  the security settings from the original folder they were in.
- The trash folder can be purged to permanently remove all deleted content.

  Important: Data that
  gets purged from the trash folder is removed from all Docusign datacenters. This
  purged data can never be restored.

Use the following procedures to manage your CLM trash folder:

- [Permanently Remove All CLM Content from the Trash Folder](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=coe1636261979689.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to delete CLM content from the trash folder. Administrators can permanently remove content to free storage in the CLM account or serve other needs.")
- [Set a Trash Retention Limit for Your CLM Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=trq1636262029119.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set a trash-retention policy to manage your deleted CLM content.")
- [Build a Document Rule to Set Your CLM Trash Retention Policy](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=spg1636262147265.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a document rule to move files to the trash folder in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build the rule to efficiently eliminate unwanted documents from the account.")
- [Building a Schedule and Rule to Trash Unwanted Documents in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=vyg1636477906006.html&_LANG=enus "Read about building a schedule and rule to move canceled contracts to the trash in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build these items to help ensure removal of unwanted contracts.")

title: Create a Docusign CLM Attribute Import Mapping
heading_path: Create a Docusign CLM Attribute Import Mapping
text:
Learn how to create an attribute import mapping in Docusign CLM.
Administrators create this mapping as part of assigning attribute values to multiple documents
without the need for manual entry.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can create
an attribute import mapping. You create this mapping as part of assigning
attribute values to multiple documents without the need for manual entry.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Attribute Import Mappings under Documents &
   Folders.

   The Attribute Import Mappings page opens.
4. Select New Mapping.
5. Enter a name in the New Mapping field.
6. Open the Type drop-down menu, and select
   CSV.
7. Type `2` in the Data Starts at Row
   field.
8. Select Mapping > Create Mapping Element.
9. Assign a column number, the filename, and appropriate attribute fields (formatted as
   Attribute Group.Attribute Field) for each element.

   Note: Assign the Filename drop-down option to column 1.
10. Repeat steps 7 and 8 for the remaining columns.

    The numbers assigned to each column must match the order of the corresponding columns
    in the custom CSV.
11. Select Save.

You have finished creating an attribute import mapping.

title: Create a custom CSV data file
heading_path: Create a custom CSV data file
text:
Learn how to create a custom CSV data file. Administrators create this file as part of
assigning attribute values to multiple documents without the need for manual entry.

This file will contain the attribute information associated with each filename organized
under columns formatted with ‘group.field’ as the header. All attribute values entered into
the cells of the CSV file MUST contain a valid value for the specified attribute, including
drop-down field values. Enter dates in a "mm/dd/yyyy" format. List the filenames in column
one. Column one must have no header.

For example, a CSV file for a bulk import of resumes contains information in a certain
format. The document names in a folder is listed on the left-hand side. The attribute
group.attribute field is listed in the column headers and the attribute values are completed
in the other CSV value fields.

title: Allow Users to Manage Contacts in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Allow Users to Manage Contacts in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to give users access to contacts in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use these
steps to allow users to create and edit contacts.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can manage contacts. By default, contact management is
disabled for users. To allow users to create and edit contacts, an administrator
must enable contacts. Follow these steps to give users access to manage
contacts.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Navigation & Actions under Account
   Management.

   The Navigation & Actions page opens.
4. Select User Navigation Header in the What are
   you customizing field.
5. Enable the toggle for Contacts.
6. Select Save.

You have enabled contact management for your CLM users. Users can
now see the Contacts tab and create and edit contacts.

title: User Management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: User Management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Read about user management in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM. Administrators can manage users
to control how the users interact with the product. The management methods depend on the CLM
account type.

A user is a person who logs in to access CLM documents. A user requires a Docusign CLM
license in your account. Based on their permission profile, a user can potentially perform any
of the following activities:

- View, add, edit, and share documents
- Administer the account

Administrators can manage users to control how the users interact with the product. The way
that administrators manage users depends on the CLM account type:

- For Docusign CLM account types, you manage users from Docusign Admin.
- For CLM.CM account types, you manage users on the Users page in the
  Admin section of the CLM application.

Note: To see what CLM account type you have, see the topic [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Enable External Review Notifications in CLM.CM
heading_path: Enable External Review Notifications in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to enable notifications in CLM.CM to receive alerts about external review
activity. Administrators can receive notifications about alerts by email.

Administrators can enable notifications in CLM.CM to
receive alerts about external review activity. Administrators receive alerts when a
reviewer, CC recipient, or sender completes or cancels an external review.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To determine which kind of CLM
account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a super administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Navigate to the Notifications section, and select the
   check boxes for your preferences in the External Review
   section.
5. Select Save.

   You have successfully set up notifications for external reviews in
   CLM.CM.

title: User Personas in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: User Personas in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Read about user personas in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM. Administrators can use personas to
help simplify a user's experience and actions while working in the application.

Personas are role-based user settings. CLM administrators use personas to create a
unique display in the interface for menu, toolbar, and navigation options. Personas are
created from groups and are based on the group name. In a user's profile, you can assign
a single persona.

Personas can help simplify a user's experience and actions while working in the application.
Different buttons and menu items get displayed, based on the user's persona or job
function.

For example, a training manager sees different buttons in the user interface than an HR
manager. This can help make end-user training happen in minutes or hours rather than
days or weeks.

title: Build a Document Rule to Set Your CLM Trash Retention Policy
heading_path: Build a Document Rule to Set Your CLM Trash Retention Policy
text:
Learn how to build a document rule to move files to the trash folder in Docusign CLM.
Administrators can build the rule to efficiently eliminate unwanted documents from the
account.

Administrators can use smart rules to build logic and automate actions in
Docusign CLM. One type of smart rule is a document rule. Document rules automate
actions on documents when events happen.

You can build a document rule to move files to the trash folder. This rule can help you
eliminate unwanted documents from your account.

Use this procedure to build a rule that moves files to the trash when a document attribute
value changes to Canceled.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Smart Rules under Automation Tools.

   The Smart Rules page opens.
4. Select Rule > New Document Rule.

   The Edit Document Rule page opens.
5. Complete the following fields to define a name, event, and rule executor for
   the rule:

   Name
   :   Specify a descriptive name for the rule.

   Event
   :   Specify Document Indexed.

   Execute as
   :   Select one of the following options for executing the rule:
       - Initiator executes the rule by using
         the security rights of the user who takes action.
       - your\_user\_name
          executes
         the rule by using your security rights.
       - Specific User executes the rule by using the security rights of
         the administrator whose name or email address you
         specify.

         Note: We recommend
         creating a generic CLM administrator to use for
         automation. This way, your automation processes are not
         tied to users who might leave or change
         roles.

   After completing the fields, your rule has a name, an assigned event,
   and an assigned executor.
6. Define filters for the rule:
   1. Select the Filter drop-down menu, and select
      In Folder.
   2. Select the Select Folder link, select the folder
      where the rule will run, and select the Select
      button.

      The product adds the filter to the bottom of the Filters
      section.
   3. Select the Filter drop-down menu, select
      Has Attribute Value, select an attribute
      group and attribute field, and type Canceled in
      the Attribute Value field.
   4. Select Add Filter.

      The product adds the filter to the bottom of the Filters
      section. The following example shows filters added to the rule
      definition.

   ![Add Filters](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Add-Filters.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg1OTYsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.yx2fiSFjDPygQDKsTgGM3CYvSDj8c696IneV7ATDc4g&_LANG=enus "Add Filters")
7. Create an action to move the document to the trash:
   1. Select the Action drop-down menu, and select
      Move Document.
   2. Enter /your\_clm\_account\_name/Trash/
      in the Or Enter Path field.

      /DocuSign/Trash/
   3. Select the Add Action link.

      The product adds the action to the bottom of the Actions section.
   4. Select Save.

      The product saves the rule and displays the defined rule on the
      Smart Rules page. The rule shows a
      Disabled status.
8. Enable the new document rule:

   1. Select the checkbox next to the new rule.
   2. Select Rule > Enable.

   The rule's status changes to Enabled.

You have completed the process. When document attribute values
change to Canceled, the documents automatically move to the trash
folder. When the trash folder gets emptied, these files get permanently removed from all
Docusign servers. The removed files no longer add to your account storage
limits.

title: Language Support and Settings in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Language Support and Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about language support and settings in Docusign CLM. Administrators can configure
language settings to support a localized interface that uses a preferred
language.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can configure language settings. These settings provide support
for creating a localized interface that uses a preferred language. The Docusign CLM user
interface supports the following languages:

- Dutch
- English
- French
- German
- Japanese
- Portuguese (Brazil)
- Spanish (Latin America and Spain)

Important: In Docusign CLM, you can store documents and
attribute values that are written in non-supported languages. However, you do so at your
own risk, and you must use UTF-8 encoding for the text. For document generation,
customer-generated content that uses a non-supported language might cause problems.
Examples of customer-generated content are form instructions or content in a template.

Docusign CLM supports the most widely used languages. If you have an urgent need for language
support, you can contact Docusign Support to submit a request. However, we cannot
guarantee approval of the request.

title: Edit an eForm in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Edit an eForm in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to modify an eForm in Docusign CLM.

Administrators and super administrators can modify
the HTML code of an eForm. Edits to an eForm can include the following activities:

- Modify code within the Form HTML field.
- Use an HTML editor to modify eForm code, then paste the content into the Form
  HTML field.
- Write HTML code, then paste it into the Form HTML field without
  using any attributes.

Important: Choosing new attribute fields and selecting
Generate Sample Form or Generate Fields Only
will delete code in the Form HTML field.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select eForms under Automation Tools.

   The Forms page opens.
4. Select the check box for the eForm that you want to change, and select
   Edit.

   The Edit Form page opens for the selected
   eForm.
5. Make your changes:
   1. (Optional) Select the Show Attribute Groups check box to see
      attribute groups.
   2. Modify or replace existing HTML code by inserting new code or selecting different
      attribute fields.
6. Select Preview to verify the changes to your eForm.
7. Perform one of the following actions to save the eForm:

   - Select Save to overwrite the current eForm.
   - Select Save As to save your work as a new eForm.

   You have successfully edited an eForm.

title: Set a Trash Retention Limit for Your CLM Account
heading_path: Set a Trash Retention Limit for Your CLM Account
text:
Learn how to set a trash-retention policy to manage your deleted CLM
content.

When you delete CLM content, your trash folder retains the content for 90 days by default. You
can change the limit to retain trash anywhere between 0 and 365 days.

Important: When content has been in the trash for the
specified number of days, the content gets permanently deleted. This content is
removed from all Docusign servers and can never be recovered.

Use this procedure to change the default number of days that trash is retained.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Type a value in the Trash Retention Days field:

   - 90 is the default setting. Files get permanently deleted after 90 days.
   - 0 is the most aggressive setting. All files get permanently deleted at
     the end of each day.
   - 365 is the least aggressive setting. All files get permanently deleted
     after one year.
   - You can enter any number between 0 and 365.
5. Select Save at the bottom of the page.

   A message indicates that your changes were successfully
   saved.

You have completed the process.

title: Agreement Actions in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about Agreement Actions in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use Agreement Actions
to automate post-signature tasks.

Docusign eSignature administrators can use Agreement Actions to automate post-signature tasks
for a completed envelope. When Agreement Actions is linked to Docusign CLM, you can
create agreement rules and trigger the following actions:

- Archive completed agreement documents to Docusign CLM
- Start a Docusign CLM workflow

Note: To use Agreement Actions with CLM, you must connect CLM to your
Docusign eSignature Admin account. For more information, see [Automate Common Post-Signature Tasks with Agreement
Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=hzy1594673444380.html&_LANG=enus).

Agreement Actions supports the following actions for CLM:

- Archive to
- Start Workflow

Note: For more information about agreement rules, actions, and
conditions, see [Create and Manage
Agreement Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=btt1594673504460.html&_LANG=enus).

Use cases for Agreement Actions in CLM workflows are as follows:

- Setting attributes for a completed envelope or agreement document
- Kicking off post-signature processes downstream

These use cases are typically configured together as an overall workflow solution.

title: Manage the Docusign CLM Account Setting for Completing External Reviews By Email
heading_path: Manage the Docusign CLM Account Setting for Completing External Reviews By Email
text:
Learn how to enable or disable the Docusign CLM account setting for completing external reviews by email. Administrators use these steps to control whether reviewers can use this method to complete external reviews.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can enable an account setting that lets reviewers complete external reviews through email. Allowing email-based completion gives reviewers flexibility and can speed up business negotiations. This setting is *not* enabled by default.

Note: Only PDF and DOCX documents can be submitted for external review through email. Any other file types must be uploaded directly to Docusign CLM.

After receiving an email with one or more attached documents, the reviewer can download and revise documents as needed. The reviewer replies to the email with revised documents and includes any relevant information in the body of the email.

Docusign CLM ingests the revised document as a version of the original document to complete the review. Use this procedure to enable or disable the account setting that allows completion of external reviews through email.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Perform one of the following actions on the Enable External Review Completion by Email setting:
   - Select the checkbox to enable the setting.
   - Clear the checkbox to disable the setting.
5. Select Save.

You are finished managing the CLM account setting for completing external reviews by email.

title: User Management in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about user management in Docusign CLM.

A user is a person who logs in to access Docusign CLM documents, tasks, reports, and so on. A
user requires a license in your CLM account. Administrators create and manage users and
groups in Docusign Admin.

Note: In Docusign CLM, administrators can perform a bulk update for
multiple users at one time. You can perform this update from the
Users page by uploading a CSV file.

In Docusign Admin, user management includes the following activities:

- Add users
- Edit users (including group membership and permission profile)
- Perform bulk actions (such as adding or updating users in bulk)
- Add or edit groups

title: Control How to Send Multi-Recipient Emails in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Control How to Send Multi-Recipient Emails in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to control how Docusign CLM sends emails that have multiple
recipients. Administrators use these steps to choose whether to send the email to each
recipient individually or send one email to all recipients.

In Docusign CLM, an administrator can control how to send emails that have
multiple recipients. The administrator can enable either of the following behaviors:

- Send the email to each recipient individually.
- Send one email to all recipients.

By default, the product sends a separate email to each recipient.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Account Settings under Account Management.

   The Account Settings page opens.
4. Select one of the following options under "Handling of Email with Multiple
   Recipients":

   Send single recipient emails
   :   Handles an email with multiple recipients by sending a separate
       email to each recipient. Each person receives an email that is
       addressed only to that person.

   Send multi recipient emails
   :   Handles an email with multiple recipients by sending one single email to all recipients.
       Each person receives an email that is addressed to all recipients.
       If a recipient uses his or her email client to send a “Reply all”,
       everyone receives that email.

       Note: An email
       cannot have more than 50 recipients.
5. Select Save.

   The product saves your changes.

You have defined a behavior for how to send multi-recipient
emails in Docusign CLM.

title: Create a Shared Dashboard in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create a Shared Dashboard in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create a shared dashboard in Docusign CLM. Administrators can create shared dashboards to control the visibility of folders, reports, and document-linked tasks.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can create a shared dashboard to assign to users or groups. Administrators can use shared dashboards to control the visibility of folders, reports, and document-linked tasks. For example, the administrator can share the dashboard only with certain users.

Note: Giving users access to a shared dashboard does not give them access to anything that they do not already have access to. Additionally, the users cannot modify the dashboard or add widgets

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Dashboard in the navigation header.

   The dashboard page opens.
3. Select Add > Dashboard.

   The Add New Dashboard dialog box opens.
4. Define the dashboard properties:
   1. Type a name in the Name field, and select Personal Dashboard under Visibility.
   2. (Optional) Select the Set this dashboard as my personal default checkbox.
   3. Select Save to create the dashboard.

      The product creates the dashboard.
5. Select Add > Widget to [add each widget](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=htp1576795529743.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add widgets to your dashboard in Docusign CLM. You can use widgets to organize information for your business purposes.") to set up your dashboard for use.

   After setting up all widgets, you can share the dashboard with your audience.
6. Convert the dashboard to a shared dashboard:
   1. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg4MDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.gYBdUPX3UAy8ZFfqBW_C26fJGA_TdRyuI3nyS71HNBI&_LANG=enus) icon next to your dashboard's name.

      The Edit Dashboard dialog box opens.
   2. Select Shared Dashboard under Visibility.

      The Who can see this dashboard field displays.
   3. Perform *one* of the following actions:

      - Type text in the field to choose users or groups, or select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Person.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg4MDcsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.gYBdUPX3UAy8ZFfqBW_C26fJGA_TdRyuI3nyS71HNBI&_LANG=enus)to open a chooser window and make your selections.
      - Select the Share with entire account checkbox.
   4. Select Save.

The product converts the dashboard to a shared dashboard. A message is now visible in the dashboard to indicate who the dashboard is shared with. You are finished creating a shared dashboard.

title: Customize Navigation and Actions Settings in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize Navigation and Actions Settings in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to customize the user interface for navigation and customize the Actions menu
in Docusign CLM.

Administrators and super administrators can customize
the navigation header and Actions menu in Docusign CLM. Navigation and
action items can be hidden or shown, based on selected options.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Navigation & Actions under Account Management.

   The Navigation & Actions page opens.
4. Select the Who is this for? drop-down menu, and choose a group
   or account to apply your changes to.

   Note: By default, navigation changes are applied to all users on
   your account.
5. Select the What are you customizing? drop-down menu, and choose
   the navigation options that you want to customize.
6. Select the toggles to customize the visiblity of navigation links and actions.
7. Select Save.

You have successfully customized navigation and actions for a
group in Docusign CLM. The next time users log in, they will see the
changes.

title: Split a PDF in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Split a PDF in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to create multiple PDFs using Split in Docusign CLM.

The Split feature enables you to divide one PDF into
multiple PDFs without altering the original.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Documents from the Navigation Header.

   The Documents list displays.
3. Select a PDF.

   The selected PDF displays in Document Review.
4. Select the Pages menu, and choose
   Split.
5. Enter the desired page numbers into the Pages field. If you need
   to add more pages, select Add More.

   Note: You can enter page numbers as
   individual pages or ranges of pages in the Pages fields. For example, entering "3-5, 7,
   1" in one Pages field will copy pages 3, 4, 5, 7, and 1 in that order into the new
   PDF.
6. Once all pages have been chosen, select Save.

   You have successfully split a PDF in Docusign CLM. Select the
   generated links to view and download the new PDF files.

title: Set IP Access Restrictions in CLM.CM
heading_path: Set IP Access Restrictions in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to restrict access to CLM.CM by IP address.

Super administrators can restrict or allow access to
the CLM.CM environment by IP address.

Note: This topic applies to
CLM.CM. To determine which kind of CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to CLM.CM as a super administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Security Settings under Account Management.

   The Security Settings page opens.
4. Select your option in the Network section:

   - To set up IP addresses that are allowed to access CLM.CM, select
     Allow.
   - To set up IP addresses that are denied access to CLM.CM, select
     Deny.
5. Enter the IP addresses you want to allow or deny in the IP
   Addresses field.

   Note: Multiple IP addresses must be separated by a comma.
   Regular expressions (RegEx) can be used to allow or restrict a range of
   addresses.
6. Select Save.

You have successfully set up access to CLM.CM based on IP
address.

title: Create a CLM Administrator for your Organization
heading_path: Create a CLM Administrator for your Organization
text:
Learn how to add a CLM administrator to the organization. Administrators can use these
steps to add a user as a CLM administrator.

Organization administrators can access Docusign CLM by default; however, you must add at least
one CLM administrator to the organization. Use this procedure to add a user as a CLM
administrator.

Note: You can also assign administrative rights to an existing user. To do
so, [add the CLM Admin permission profile to
the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=das1581520893906.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add a CLM Admin permission profile to an existing user. Administrators add this profile to give the user administrative rights for Docusign CLM.").

1. Access Docusign Admin.
2. Select Users.

   The Users page opens.
3. Select Add User.
4. Complete the User Profile page, and select
   Next: Accounts at the bottom.

   The Add Account Membership page opens.
5. Select an account from the Account drop-down list.

   A list of applications displays.
6. Select a level of access from the Permission Profile
   drop-down list in the eSignature section.
7. Enable administrative permission for CLM:
   1. Move the toggle to Enabled in the CLM
      section.
   2. Select CLM Admin from the Permission
      Profile drop-down list.
8. Select Next to complete the rest of the process, review
   the user profile, and save the user.

You are finished. The CLM administrator can access the CLM
account.

title: Customize a Toolbar in Docusign CLM.CM
heading_path: Customize a Toolbar in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to customize a toolbar in Docusign CLM

Super Administrators can customize the toolbar in
Docusign CLM.CM. Toolbar items can be added or removed as needed.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind
of Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log on to Docusign CLM.CM as a Super Administrator.
2. Select Documents from the navigation header.

   The documents list displays.
3. Select View  >  Show Toolbar.

   The Docusign CLM.CM toolbar displays.
4. Select View  >  Customize Toolbar.

   The toolbar customization window displays.
5. Choose your toolbar customization options:

   - Enter the email address or name for which the toolbar customizations display in the
     Appears for: field.
   - Select any menu name and choose a menu item to convert it to a toolbar item.

     Note: Menu items converted to toolbar items will
     display at the end of the toolbar.
   - Move icons in the toolbar by selecting an icon, and then selecting
     << Move Left or >> Move
     Right.
   - Remove icons from the toolbar by selecting an icon, and then selecting
     Delete.
   - Add separator bars between icons by selecting an icon, and then
     selecting the checkboxes for Separator Before or
     Separator After.
   - Revert all changes made by selecting Revert to
     Default.
6. Once all changes have been made, select Save.

   You have successfully customized the toolbar for the selected user in
   Docusign CLM.CM.

title: First Steps for an Org Administrator in Docusign CLM
heading_path: First Steps for an Org Administrator in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about the first steps that an organization administrator can take after Docusign CLM account provisioning. Administrators can use this process to set up Docusign CLM.

Docusign Admin supports Docusign CLM accounts and administrative components for your
agreement processes. As an organization administrator, your CLM permission profile was
included in account provisioning.

You can use the following process to set up Docusign CLM.

Note: The credentials that you use for Docusign Admin and eSignature are
also your Docusign CLM credentials.

1. Log on to Docusign eSignature with Docusign org administrator credentials.
2. Open the Docusign Admin Dashboard﻿. For information about how to access the Docusign Admin
   Dashboard, see [Open the Docusign Admin
   Dashboard](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=hao1583359136204.html&_LANG=enus).
3. Log on to Docusign CLM and create a CLM administrator for your organization. For information
   about how to create a CLM administrator for your organization, see [Create a CLM Administrator for your Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=viv1581375160992.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add a CLM administrator to the organization. Administrators can use these steps to add a user as a CLM administrator.").

title: Customizing Menus and Toolbars in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customizing Menus and Toolbars in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about customizing menus and toolbars in Docusign CLM. In Docusign CLM,
administrators can customize menus and toolbars to simplify a user’s experience.

In Docusign CLM, administrators and Super Administrators can customize menu actions for
users. Document Actions options can be enabled or disabled to streamline the user
experience.

Administrators and Super Administrators can also create custom actions. Customizations can
help simplify the user experience by creating shortcuts to common actions. Menu options can be
created to execute actions in workflows, convert documents, and other document management
options.

title: Global Notifications in Docusign CLM.CM
text:
Read about how to configure alerts in Docusign CLM.CM for users. Users can receive
alerts about folder and document activity, and choose a notification delivery method. Super
Administrators can enable additional notifications to receive alerts about user account and
external review activity.

Note: This topic applies to Docusign CLM.CM. To determine which kind of
Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM
Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

Docusign CLM.CM users can receive alerts about folder and document activity, and choose a
notification delivery method:

- Email notification: Users can receive notifications by
  real-time emails or a single digest.
- Notification counter: Notifications will display as a
  number on the page header.

Docusign CLM.CM Super Administrators can enable notifications to receive alerts about user
accounts and external reviews:

- User account notifications: Administrators can receive alerts when
  users enable, disable, or change their Permission Profile.
- External review notifications: Administrators can receive
  alerts when a reviewer, CC recipient, or sender completes or cancels an external
  review.

title: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
heading_path: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to access the unified task view from Docusign CLM.
Administrators and users can use this central view to manage tasks across Docusign applications.

title: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
heading_path: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
text:
Unified task management must be enabled. New CLM customers receive
this feature by default. Existing CLM customers enable this feature by selecting
Enable unified task management page on the Account
Settings page.

In Docusign CLM, you can access a unified task view to manage tasks across
your applications (such as Docusign CLM and eSignature). Use the
central view to review important context, prioritize work, and complete tasks
efficiently.

Important:

- Unified task management is not available for CLM accounts in FedRAMP sites,
  CLM accounts in IL4 sites, or CLM.CM accounts. Unified task management is
  also not available for CLM Essentials accounts.
- CLM customers who opt in to unified task management should not
  assign the Tasks page as the CLM account homepage.
  Making this assignment can cause navigation problems when switching apps. By
  default, the Documents page is the homepage.

From the unified task view, you can perform any of the following activities:

- View detailed context about tasks
- Apply filters to refine your task view
- Customize columns in the task view
- Manage tasks for CLM managed users

Use this procedure to access the Docusign unified task view from Docusign CLM.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. Select Tasks in the navigation header.

   The Tasks page opens. This page provides a unified
   task view.

From the unified view, you can review tasks and view task
details. If applicable, you can also take action directly on a task or complete a task
management action (such as reassignment).

title: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
heading_path: Access the Docusign Unified Task View from Docusign CLM
text:
Begin managing tasks by reviewing the documentation about
[Docusign unified task
management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ikc1746801059132&topicId=bhd1746801493533.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Public Links for Sharing Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Public Links for Sharing Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how public links can be used to share documents and resources. Administrators can
use public links to allow non-CLM users to view and download documents.

In Docusign CLM, public links can be used to share documents and content with non-CLM users.
Public links allow non-CLM users to view documents and download them. Links can be set
to expire on a given date or removed manually by an administrator.

title: Building a Schedule and Rule to Trash Unwanted Documents in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Building a Schedule and Rule to Trash Unwanted Documents in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about building a schedule and rule to move canceled contracts to the trash in
Docusign CLM. Administrators can build these items to help ensure removal of unwanted
contracts.

In Docusign CLM, a smart rule lets you build "if-then" logic to automate actions
in your account. A scheduled rule is a smart rule that takes action on
files at specified dates and times. A schedule dictates the times and dates
that a scheduled rule runs.

Administrators can build a schedule and scheduled rule to move canceled contracts to the trash.
This process removes unwanted contracts. Your CLM account storage also gets reduced when
the files get emptied from the trash folder.

To build a schedule and rule that automate the deletion of unwanted files, complete the
following tasks:

[Build a Schedule in Docusign CLM to Trigger a Rule for Trashing Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=kjv1636412077513.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a schedule in Docusign CLM to help with trash removal. Administrators can use the schedule to trigger a rule that moves canceled contracts to the trash.")

[Build a Scheduled Rule in Docusign CLM to Automate Sending Documents to the Trash](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=ajj1636262082417.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a scheduled smart rule in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build this rule to move files to the trash folder on a schedule.")

title: Enable Support Access in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Enable Support Access in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to provide temporary access to a Docusign Support
user.

As a Docusign CLM administrator, you can provide a Docusign Support user temporary access to your account. The first time you grant access to
Support, a user called Docusign Support gets created.

Note: Before enabling support access, contact Docusign Support for assistance. Docusign
Support will let you know if access to your account is necessary.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select the Help Icon
   ![Help Icon Button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Help-Icon-Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.BMv0d360ON1b5pxnbJfLhvIajeL45IvtsMGrUBgTVq4&_LANG=enus "Help Icon Button"), and choose Grant Support Access.

   The Grant Support Access dialog box
   opens.
3. Choose the amount of time you want to grant access.
4. (Optional) Clear the Use previous Docusign Support’s
   permissions check box if you do not want to reuse previous
   permissions.

   Note: This option is enabled by default. When enabled,
   permissions that were previously configured for the Docusign Support user remain.
5. Review the terms, and select the checkbox for By checking the
   checkbox, you agree to the following terms.
6. Select Grant Access.

   The Support Access Granted dialog box opens, identifying when
   Support access will end.
7. Select Close.

   You have successfully enabled Support Access in Docusign CLM. You
   should inform Docusign Support that you have enabled Grant
   Support Access.

title: Log in to CLM.CM with Two-Step Verification
heading_path: Log in to CLM.CM with Two-Step Verification
text: Learn how to log in to CLM.CM by using two-step verification.

title: Log in to CLM.CM with Two-Step Verification
heading_path: Log in to CLM.CM with Two-Step Verification
text:
You must [set up two-step
verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=fnt1585347689093.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set up two-step verification as a CLM.CM user.").

With two-step verification enabled, you must verify a code through your Google Authenticator
app when you log in to CLM.CM.

1. Specify your credentials on the login page for your CLM.CM account, and select
   Log In.

   The product prompts you to enter a six-digit verification code from your Google
   Authenticator app.
2. Find the code in the Google Authenticator app, enter the code on the
   Two-step verification page in CLM.CM, and select
   Log In.

The product logs you on. Each time you log on, you must get a
verification code from the Google Authenticator app.

title: Create a Docusign CLM Document Rule
heading_path: Create a Docusign CLM Document Rule
text:
Learn how to create a document rule in Docusign CLM. Administrators can build document
rules to automate actions on documents when events happen.

1. Select Admin in the navigation header.
2. Select Smart Rules under Automation Tools.
3. Select Rule > New Document Rule.
4. Enter a short description in the Name field.

   An example is "AP Invoice Migration".
5. Open the Event drop-down menu, and select Document
   Entered Folder.
6. Select an option under Execute as to control who executes the
   rule.
7. Add a name filter:
   1. Open the Filter drop-down menu, and select Has
      Name Like.
   2. Enter `*.csv` in the Name field in the
      Filters section.

      Asterisks (\*) function as wildcards throughout CLM.
   3. Select Add Filter to add the name filter.
8. Add a folder filter:
   1. Open the Filter drop-down menu, and select In
      Folder.
   2. Select Select Folder, choose the folder that contains the
      documents, and select the Select button.
9. Add an action:
   1. Open the Action drop-down menu, and select Bulk
      Attribute Import.
   2. Choose a value from the Attribute Import Mapping drop-down
      list.
   3. Select Add Action.
10. Select Save to save the rule.
11. Enable the rule:
    1. Select the check box to the left of the document rule.
    2. Select Rule > Enable.

       The rule's status changes to Enabled.

title: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
Read about permission profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM. Administrators can assign a
profile with defined capabilities to determine what actions a user can perform.

Each Docusign CLM user needs an assigned permission profile. Each profile has a defined set of
capabilities that determines what actions a user can perform. Administrators assign
permission profiles when creating users in Docusign CLM.

When setting security on documents and folders, access can also be determined by a user’s
permission profile. For more information about defining folder access based on user
profiles, see [Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about delegated permissions in Docusign CLM. Administrators can assign permissions to users in Docusign CLM (or full subscribers in CLM.CM). Users with delegated permissions have access to assigned administrative functions.").

Your CLM account type determines which permission profiles are available and how you
assign them.

Note: To see what CLM account type you have, see [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account
Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
In Docusign CLM, profiles are assigned in Docusign Admin at the account level. The
following profiles are available for assignment:

CLM Admin
:   Assigns administrative rights for Docusign CLM. This profile has access to
    everything in your Docusign CLM repository.
:   An account must have at least one CLM Admin profile. This profile is not the
    same profile as an organization administrator but can be the same user.

CLM User
:   Assigns basic user rights for Docusign CLM. This profile is the most common
    permission profile. CLM users can perform the following activities:

    - Access folders, documents, and tasks
    - View, edit and upload documents
    - Run reports

      Note: Users cannot create or edit
      reports by default.
    - Change their own passwords

CLM Viewer
:   Assigns guest rights for Docusign CLM. This profile provides a minimal user
    role with limited access to Docusign CLM.
:   CLM viewers can perform the following activities:

    - Upload or view documents in Docusign CLM.
    - Use search
    - complete eForms
    - Change their own passwords in user preferences
:   CLM Viewers cannot perform the following activities:

    - Move, edit or share documents
    - Interact with tasks
    - View reports, create reports, or access contacts

title: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
heading_path: Permission Profiles in Docusign CLM and CLM.CM
text:
In CLM.CM, profiles are assigned in the CLM application. The following profiles are
available for assignment:

Guest
:   Is the basic user role. Guest users can execute searches and complete eForms. Guests can
    also create (upload), view, and download documents. Guests can change their
    passwords in the Preferences tab. Guests *cannot*
    perform the following activities:

    - Participate in workflows
    - View or create reports
    - Access the address book
    - Create or edit eForms
    - Move, edit, or share documents

Limited Subscriber
:   Is a user role similar to Guest. Limited subscribers can execute searches and complete
    eForms. Limited subscribers can change their passwords in the
    Preferences tab. Limited subscribers *cannot*
    perform the following activities:

    - View or create reports
    - Create contacts
    - Edit or create eForms

Full Subscriber
:   Is the most common user role. Full subscribers have full access to view, edit, and upload
    documents. Full subscribers can execute reports and participate in
    workflows. Full subscribers cannot edit reports and have some expanded
    account settings in Preferences.

User Administrator
:   Is a delegate role for some administrative tasks. User administrators can manage users,
    create and edit mailing lists, and administer some account-level
    preferences. User administrators cannot create or edit security groups. An
    account can have multiple user administrators.

Super Administrator
:   Is the highest-level administrator in your account. Super administrators can access
    everything in your Docusign CLM repository, including all users' home
    folders. Use this role for designating a repository administrator. An
    Account can have multiple super administrators.

title: Docusign CLM Service Limits
text:
Read about service limits in Docusign CLM. These limits enable CLM to operate safely and efficiently.

Service limits enable Docusign CLM to operate safely and efficiently. Administrators should be aware of these limits for account management, planning, and other purposes.

The following table describes the service limits that the product enforces:

Table 1.  Docusign CLM Service Limits

title: Docusign CLM Service Limits
text:
hould be aware of these limits for account management, planning, and other purposes.

The following table describes the service limits that the product enforces:

Table 1.  Docusign CLM Service Limits

| Product Area | Restricted Item | Limit |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AI-Assisted Review add-in for Microsoft Word | Number of rules in a playbook | Exceeding 50 rules might slow down processing time when running the playbook |
| AI-Assisted Review add-in for Microsoft Word | Number of characters in a playbook rule | 1000 characters |
| Document administration | Number of Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) concurrent connections | 20 connections |
| Document comparison | File size | 128 MB |
| Document preview | Page limit for document preview (all preview formats) | 250 pages Note: Documents over 250 pages are downloadable and available for actions in CLM. However, these documents are not viewable in the preview window. |
| Document attributes | Attribute values | 400 characters |
| Document attributes | Attribute name in an attribute group | 50 characters |
| Document attributes | Attribute group name | 50 characters |
| Document attributes | Rows in a CSV that you use to populate values for attributes | Exceeding 10,000 rows can degrade product performance. |
| Document and folder management | Special characters in folder and document names | Prohibited special characters are as follows:  - / - \ - : - \* - ? - “ - < - > - |   Note: The characters # and & are not prohibited but are automatically replaced in External Object Storage (EOS) folders |
| Document and folder management | Folder name | 100 characters Note: Folder names cannot begin or end with a space. These names also cannot end with a period. |
| Document and folder management | Folder description | 255 characters |
| Document and folder management | Document name | 255 characters Note: Document names cannot begin or end with a space. These names also cannot end with a period. |
| Document and folder management | Number of document versions | 1000 versions |
| Document and folder management | Number of direct child objects (documents and subfolders) that can exist within a parent folder | 10,000 objects |
| Document and folder management | File size in document preview | 1 MB for TXT files  256 MB for all other files |
| Document and folder management | Inbound emails for an account | 500 emails per hour |
| Document and folder management | Combined file size when you send one or more documents for external review manually (non-workflow) | 25 MB (all files combined) |
| Document generation | Size for a document that gets created or uploaded through a document generation configuration | 200 MB  Exceeding this size can result in errors. Tip: We recommend limiting the size to 50 MB or less. Generations take longer for documents larger than 50 MB. Generation time can also depend on the number of merges. |
| Document generation | Format of a “Link to CSV Document” dropdown field | Specifying a CSV file that exceeds 10 KB changes the field from a picklist to a type-ahead list. |
| Document generation | File size of the CSV file for a “Link to CSV Document” dropdown field | 1 MB or less (recommended size)  When a CSV gets loaded into a document generation form, the CSV gets loaded into the user's browser.  Larger files can cause delays in loading the form and searching in the dropdown field.  Docusign cannot guarantee support for files that exceed 25 MB. |
| Document generation | Size of the document generation form (based on the number of fields added) | 256 MB  Exceeding this size prevents the form from being saved. Tip: The form size is not checked until you attempt to save. To avoid losing your work, we strongly recommend creating smaller, focused forms as a standard practice. |
| Document uploads | Uploaded document size | 1.5 GB |
| Document uploads | Number of uploaded documents submitted at one time (through the File > Upload menu action or through drag and drop) | 20 documents |
| Electronic signature requests from CLM to eSignature | Size of each document or envelope that CLM sends to eSignature | - 25 MB - 100 MB when "chunked upload" is enabled for the account   Note: Chunked upload permits uploading a PDF file to eSignature in separate pieces. To request enablement of chunked upload, contact Docusign Support. |
| Electronic signature requests from CLM to eSignature | Number of pages in each document or envelope that CLM sends to eSignature | See the [file size limitations for envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=akk1578456465691.html&_LANG=enus). |
| Electronic signature requests from CLM to eSignature | eSignature API | See the [eSignature API rules and resource limits](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/esign-rest-api/esign101/rules-and-limits/). |
| Party management | Total number of parties | Reaching or exceeding 20,000 parties causes performance issues when you select parties or search for parties. |
| Reporting | Report size when exporting to CSV | 5,000 record entries Note: A report that exceeds 5,000 records must be saved to a folder location in Docusign CLM. The report can then be exported as a CSV or XLSX file and saved locally. |
| Search | Amount of content that CLM can extract and index from each document | 1 MB of content |
| Search | Number of search results | 100 results by default for legacy search Note: Customers can request an increase (up to 1000 results).   1000 results by default for modernized search |
| Search | Size limit for a file to be indexed for search | 268 MB  Any file larger than 268 MB cannot be indexed and will not show up in searches. |
| Workflows | Data size for the output from a workflow expression evaluation | 1 MB |
| Workflows | Expression execution limit for a workflow instance Important:  - In UAT environments, this limit is effective for new workflow revisions that are created on or after August 12, 2025. - In Production environments, this limit is effective for new workflow revisions that are created on or after September 2, 2025. | 300 expressions during a 15-minute time period  When the limit is reached, the product pauses the workflow instance. The product also sends an email to users who are defined in the “Advanced Workflow Error Notification User” account setting. If no action is taken on the paused workflow before the scheduled purge day, the product aborts and purges the workflow. |
| Workflows | Size for each individual variable for the following accounts:  - Accounts with workflows that execute in a user acceptance testing (UAT) environment - Newly created accounts with workflows that execute in a Production environment | 5 MB |
| Workflows | Total size of all variables in a single workflow for the following accounts:  - Accounts with workflows that execute in a UAT environment - Newly created accounts with workflows that execute in a Production environment | 25 MB |
| Workflows | CSV file size for the Create Variable from CSV workflow step | 1 MB |
| Workflows | Number of workflows that can be started within one hour per account | 100 workflows (UAT environments)  1,000 workflows (Production environments) |
| Workflows | Workflow executions Note: A workflow execution is counted whenever a new workflow is initiated or a workflow resumes from an asynchronous step (such as sending for signature). | 500 executions every 15 minutes per account (UAT environments)  12,000 executions every 15 minutes per account (Production environments) |
| Workflows | Number of executed steps that you can view for a workflow in the user interface | 1,000 steps |
| Workflows | Activities per workflow instance Note: A workflow activity is counted whenever a workflow step is executed. | 100,000 activities per instance for existing workflow revisions (versions) that were published before April 28, 2022 (UAT) and May 13, 2022 (PROD)  10,000 activities per instance for new workflow revisions (versions) that were published after April 28, 2022 (UAT) and May 13, 2022 (PROD) |
| Workflows | Number of activities per workflow that can be executed consecutively  Number of activities per workflow that can be executed synchronously without a human activity step or asynchronous activity step | 5,000 activities |
| Workflows | Number of days that a workflow can remain under “Executing” or “Paused” status Note: When the limit is reached, the product aborts the workflow revision. After the abort, Docusign CLM schedules the workflow for purging, according to the [purge schedule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=gqw1655998280555.html&_LANG=enus). | 365 days (Production environments)  90 days (UAT environments) Note: If a workflow gets paused by the system due to reaching any service limit, purging begins 30 days after the pause. |
| Workflows | Purge schedule for workflows under “Complete” or “Failed” status | The value for the Days after Workflow Completes and Days after Workflow Fails account settings must be between 7 and 30 days. |
| Workflows | Workflow names | 100 alphanumeric characters |
| Workflows | Workflow variable names | 250 alphanumeric characters |
| Workflows | Number of recipients in the Email Document workflow step | 50 recipients |
| Workflows | Attachment size in the Email Document workflow step | 20.48 MB total for all attachments |
| Workflows | Size of the combined document that the Combine Documents workflow step creates | 200 MB |
| Workflows | Number of files that the Combine Documents workflow step can combine | 250 files |

title: Create a Persona View for the Documents Page in CLM
heading_path: Create a Persona View for the Documents Page in CLM
text:
Learn how to create a persona view for the Documents page in CLM. Administrators can use
personas to help simplify a user's experience and actions while working in the
application.

CLM administrators use personas to create a unique display in the interface for menu, toolbar,
and navigation options. Personas can help simplify a user's experience and actions
while working in the application. Use this procedure to create a persona view for
the Documents page in CLM.

1. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   A Documents landing page opens.
2. Select the View menu name, and select one of the
   following options:

   - Customize Toolbar (CLM.CM only)
   - Customize Menu

   You create a menu item that displays for the persona. Or you can customize a toolbar
   in CLM.CM and assign it to appear for a persona.
3. Make your changes, and select Save.

Any user who gets assigned the persona now sees the
customized display in CLM.

title: Hide Document Types in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Hide Document Types in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to hide document types in Docusign CLM. Hiding a document type prevents that
type from being selected when uploading or editing documents in Docusign CLM.

Docusign CLM Administrators can hide document types in
their accounts. If you hide a document type, you cannot apply that type to existing or
uploaded documents; however, existing documents that have that type applied continue to
show the same type. If necessary, you can change the type in those documents.

Note:

- Party Management must be enabled to use custom document types. For more
  information about enabling Party Management, see [Enable Party Management in
  Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=mjn1643378694283&topicId=uva1603410757890.html&_LANG=enus).
- This feature is currently not available to CLM.CM customers.
- Changing a document type’s name will not change the document’s
  classification. For more information about document classes, see [Document Types in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=pls1666892322322.html&_LANG=enus).
- If a document type has a new name assigned, all existing documents of that
  type will display the new document name.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin from the navigation header.

   The Docusign CLM Admin page displays.
3. Select Documents & Folders  >  Document Types.

   The Document Types page displays.
4. Select Edit.

   The Document types page now allows you to set up the
   alternate name and visibility for each document type.
5. Select the Visible toggle for each document type you
   want to hide.

   The Visibility status for the document type changes to
   Hidden.
6. Select Save.

   You have successfully hidden one or more document types in Docusign CLM.
   Existing Docusign CLM documents of that type will retain the hidden type until
   they are altered or saved. Uploaded and new documents will not be able to use
   the hidden document type.

title: Document Classification in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about the different types of document types in Docusign CLM. Administrators can
enable custom document types, and make changes to the visibility of document
types.

In Docusign CLM, document types are classified into three broad categories: standalone
documents, amendments, and supplements. Each of these categories encompass several types
of agreements. In Docusign CLM, administrators can create alternate names for document
types. This enables administrators to fit document categories to their specific use
cases.

title: Configure Document Generation Forms to use Writeback in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Configure Document Generation Forms to use Writeback in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to configure Document Generation Forms for use with Writeback to Salesforce in
Docusign CLM.

To use Writeback, you must configure Document Generation Forms in Docusign CLM. For
more information about Document Generation Forms in Docusign CLM, see [Document Generation Forms in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=rsb1576795539656.html&_LANG=enus).

Writeback only works for merge fields within the Document Generation form that are
linked to Salesforce fields. To determine if a merge field is linked to Salesforce,
select the field name within the form and open the Properties
tab. Under Field Value, locate the Link to Salesforce
field checkbox. If this box is checked, the field is linked to
Salesforce.

![CLM Writeback Linked to SF](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-Writeback-Linked-to-SF.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg5NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9._4ah3fWg1KebSFptg8Q6X8GDF2n-843NZtFBh3wm1es&_LANG=enus "CLM Writeback Linked to SF")

For all of the document fields that you want to review using Writeback, ensure that the
merge tags for the linked merge fields are pasted in your Word template. Merge fields
