that are linked to Salesforce contain TrackName in the merge tag.![CLM Writeback merge tag](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/CLM-Writeback-merge-tag.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg5NzAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9._4ah3fWg1KebSFptg8Q6X8GDF2n-843NZtFBh3wm1es&_LANG=enus "CLM Writeback merge tag")

TrackName in the merge tag indicates that this is tracked content in CLM, and
there is content control in your Word Document. You can configure settings for your
tracked content by editing the merge tag properties.

Properties for tracked content include:

- Title="Company Name" (Title gives your content control a title that is
  exposed in the Word UI)
- Hidden="true" (Hidden hides the content control)
- Deletable="true" (Deletable allows the content control to be deleted or
  not)
- Editable="true" (Editable locks editing)

For more information about word termplate merge tags, see [Document Generation Word Template Merge Tags](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=uno1576795541214.html&_LANG=enus).

Writeback supports the following Document Generation fields and the
corresponding linked Salesforce objects.

- Currency
- Date
- Dropdown
- Text Box
- Text Field
- Number

title: Create a User in a CLM.CM Account
heading_path: Create a User in a CLM.CM Account
text:
Learn how to create a user in a CLM.CM Account. Administrators can create a user who
can then view or work on documents in your account.

Administrators can create a user when somebody needs access to CLM.CM. The user can view or
work on documents in your account. This person can be a team member, client, vendor, or
other individual.

Assign a permission profile that matches the level of action that the user needs to take.
For example, if a client only needs to view documents, assign a Guest permission profile. If
your internal team member needs to add documents and edit the documents, assign a Full
Subscriber permission profile.

Note: When setting security on documents and folders, you can define
access based on user roles. For more information about delegated administrative permissions,
see the [Limited Administrative Permissions with Delegated Permissions in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bra1576610051531.html&_LANG=enus "Read about delegated permissions in Docusign CLM. Administrators can assign permissions to users in Docusign CLM (or full subscribers in CLM.CM). Users with delegated permissions have access to assigned administrative functions.") topic.

This procedure describes how to create a user who is not an API-only user.

1. Log in to CLM.CM.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Users under User Management.

   The Users page opens.
4. Select New User.

   The Create New User page opens.
5. Complete the Account Details section:
   1. Enter a name in the Login Name field.

      Login names must be unique.
   2. Select the user's role from the Permission Profile drop-down
      list.
   3. (Optional) Select a permission from the Delegated Permission
      drop-down list.

      A list displays only when you have saved permissions in your account.
   4. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/personabutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus)icon under
      User Persona, and assign the user to any necessary security
      groups.
   5. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/People.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus) icon under
      Managed By, and assign the user to a
      manager/organization.
   6. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/groupsbutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus) icon under
      Group Membership, and assign the user to any distribution
      groups.
6. Set up the dates for enabling and disabling the user:
   1. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/calendarpicker2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus) icon under Enable User Date to choose
      the user's start date.
   2. Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/calendarpicker2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus) icon under Disable User Date to choose
      the user's expiration (end) date.

title: Create a User in a CLM.CM Account
heading_path: Create a User in a CLM.CM Account
text:
pb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg3NDAsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.JCMl4bDi3m2XjvOkzLI65_nwWlcucrl6QC-UC3RKEno&_LANG=enus) icon under Disable User Date to choose
      the user's expiration (end) date.

      If you do not want the user to expire, leave this field blank.
7. Select the Password never expires box to let users keep the same
   password.

   Note: The password expiration default is configurable in Account
   Settings.
8. Select the Send Email box to send the user an email to activate
   the user account.
9. Complete the Profile Information section:
   1. Enter the user's first name in the First Name field.
   2. Enter the user's last name in the Last Name field.
   3. Enter a valid email address in the Email Address
      field.

      Note: Each new user must have a unique email address.
   4. (Optional) Supply a phone number, fax number, company, department, title, address,
      or country.
10. Select Save .

The product creates the user.

title: Upload Public Key for SFTP Authentication in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Upload Public Key for SFTP Authentication in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to upload your public key for SFTP authentication in Docusign CLM.

Administrators can upload a public key to Docusign CLM
for SFTP authentication.

Important:

- This guide assumes that you or your Docusign CLM administrator has already created your
  public/private keypair. Docusign does not provide keypairs for SFTP
  authentication. If you need assistance in creating or managing keypairs for
  your SFTP configuration, please contact your account team regarding our
  Professional Services offerings.
- This topic applies to Docusign CLM. For more information about SFTP for Docusign CLM.CM,
  see [Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for Docusign CLM.CM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=bbl1576609938274.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the SFTP self-service for CLM.CM. Super administrators can use SFTP for moving documents or folders between a local data source and CLM.CM."). To determine which kind of
  Docusign CLM account you have, see [Identify your Docusign CLM
  Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Log in to your Docusign account.
2. Select the User Icon, and choose Manage Profile.

   The My Profile screen displays.
3. Navigate to Privacy and Security  >  Certificates, and select Manage.

   The Certificates screen displays.
4. Select Upload Public Key, and navigate to the folder
   where your public key is stored.
5. Select your public key to upload it to Docusign CLM.

   Note: Your public and private key pairs should be
   formatted as follows:
   - Public
     Key:

     ```
     |  |  |
     | --- | --- |
     |  | ----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY---- |
     |  | YourPublicKeyStringGoesHere |
     |  | ----END PUBLIC KEY---- |
     ```
   - Private
     Key:

     ```
     |  |  |
     | --- | --- |
     |  | ----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY---- |
     |  | YourPrivateKeyStringGoesHere |
     |  | ----END RSA PRIVATE KEY---- |
     ```

   You have successfully uploaded your public key to your Docusign account.

title: Bulk Import Users CSV Format
text:
Look up header row values to use in the CSV for bulk importing users in
CLM.CM.

The CSV for bulk import consists of a header row and a rows of user data to add. The CSV
can contain both new and existing users, but existing users are not updated when you bulk
add users. You must run a separate bulk update to update existing users.

**Header row values**

The first row of the CSV is the header row and defines each of the columns. Header values
must match the header row values listed below. Header values are not required to be in the
order listed and are not case-sensitive.

**Required columns**

The CSV must contain the Email header row. The rest of the header row values shown below
are optional.

Table 1. Acceptable header row values

| Header Row Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Email | The user's complete email address—required column. |
| FirstName | The user's first name. |
| LastName | The user's last name. |
| Role | The user’s role. If a role is not listed, the user receives the role of "Full Subscriber.” When updating users, they maintain their current role. |
| Groups | This is a pipe (‘|’) delimited list of the group names that the user on the row is a member. The user is removed from groups they are currently a member of that are not listed and added to groups listed. If the group does not exist in the account, the group is created, and the user added as a member. |
| RemoveGroupsNotListed | Valid values for this field are "true" or "false.” If set to "true," the user is removed from all groups not listed in the Groups column. If set to "false," the user remains in existing groups and is added to groups listed in the Groups column. The default value is "true" when the Groups column has no data, and a RemoveGroupsNotListed column is not specified. |
| Active | Valid values for this field are "true" or "false.” If set to "true," the user is added to the Address Book. If set to "false," the user is removed from the Address Book. |
| Title | The user's title that displays in the address book. |
| Department | The user's department that displays in the address book. |
| Company | The user's company that displays in the address book. |
| Address1 | The user's address- first line. |
| Address2 | The user's address- second line. |
| Address3 | The user's address- third line. |
| City | The user's city name. |
| State | The user's state/province. |
| PostalCode | The user's postal code. |
| PhoneNumber | The user's phone number. |
| FaxNumber | The user's fax number that displays in the address book. |
| Persona | The user's persona should a valid name of a persona already created in the account. |
| ManagedBy | The user's manager should be a valid email address of a user in the account. |
| SendActivationEmail | Valid values for this field are "true" or "false.” Entering "true" sends an activation email to the user. |
| EnabledStartDate | The start date when this user can access the account. |
| EnabledEndDate | The end date when this user can access the account. |
| PortalUser | Valid values for this field are "true" or "false.” Indicates if they have only portal access to Docusign CLM, otherwise full access to the UI. |
| ExemptFromUserSync | Valid values for this field are "true" or "false.” Indicates if the Salesforce user sync process should not manage the user. |

title: Revoke Support User Access in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Revoke Support User Access in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to revoke Docusign Support user access.

If the Docusign Support user is no longer needed, a
Docusign CLM Administrator or Super Administrator can revoke access.

1. Log on to Docusign CLM as an Administrator.
2. Select the Help Icon
   ![Help Icon Button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/pxt1643324456371/page/images/Help-Icon-Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTg2OTUsInNoZWFmIjoicHh0MTY0MzMyNDQ1NjM3MSJ9.O4BfMfHtJdglBMvWtFA399X0mmDAS3iHxTJLAO3nwlc&_LANG=enus "Help Icon Button"), and choose Grant Support Access.

   The Grant Support Access dialog box
   opens.
3. Select Revoke access.

   You have successfully disabled access for the Docusign Support user.

title: User Management in CLM.CM
text:
Learn about user management in CLM.CM. Administrators can manage users, groups, task groups, and delegated permissions.

Note: This topic applies to CLM.CM. To see what CLM account type you have, see [Identify Your Docusign CLM Account Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=xzt1631832579827.html&_LANG=enus). Docusign CLM accounts are managed through Docusign Admin. For more information, see [User Management in Docusign Admin for Organization Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jni1583359185567.html&_LANG=enus).

In CLM.CM, administrators can manage the following items:

Users
:   Individuals who will log on to your CLM.CM account to view and edit documents. A individual user requires a user license.

Groups
:   Groups of users. This allows you to define security based on group membership rather than individual user names.

Task Groups
:   Work groups for the automated assignment of workflow tasks.

Delegated Permissions
:   Flexible ways to create lighter administrative roles. You can delegate access to specific areas without having to delegate to all areas.

Typical user management tasks include

- Adding users, contacts, and groups
- Changing group membership
- Assigning a permission profile (role) to a user
- Defining a user's persona
- Resetting user passwords

title: User Management in CLM.CM
text:
CLM user roles define the maximum access level that a user has in your environment. Each user role has a defined set of capabilities.

The following user roles exist.

Guests
:   Have the minimal user role in CLM.CM. Guests can only upload or view documents in CLM. Use the guest role when users only need to create (upload) documents or view and download documents. Guests cannot move, edit, or share documents and cannot participate in workflows. Guests can execute searches and can complete eForms but cannot edit or create them. They are limited to changing their passwords. Guests cannot view or create reports or access the address book.

Limited subscribers
:   Can execute Searches and can complete eForms but cannot edit or create them. Limited subscribers are limited to changing their passwords. Limited subscribers cannot view or create reports or contacts.

Full subscribers
:   Can view, edit, and upload documents.This role gives a user full access to documents and workflow. Full subscribers can execute but not create or edit reports. Full subscribers are the most common type of users.

User administrators
:   Can manage users and administer certain account-level preferences. User administrators cannot create or edit security groups, but they can create and edit mailing lists. Super administrators should assign this role to delegate administrative tasks within a CLM account. An account can have multiple user administrators.

Super administrators
:   Is the highest-level administrator in your CLM account. Super administrators have rights and access to everything in your CLM repository, including all users' home folders. This role serves as a repository administrator. An account can have multiple super administrators.

You assign a role for each person who logs on to your CLM account. User roles are also used when setting security on folders and documents.

title: User Management in CLM.CM
text:
Users who get provisioned can have any of the following states:

Active
:   The user has been provisioned by the super administrator. An activation email has been sent, and the user logged on to the product at least once.

Inactive
:   The user has been provisioned by the super administrator. An activation email has been sent, but the user has not logged on to the product. Inactive users can be added to task groups. Workflow tasks can be allocated to inactive users.

Enabled
:   The user can access the product with his or her provisioned username. Users can also be enabled for a specific duration.

Disabled
:   The user cannot access the product with his or her provisioned logon name. The user account is no longer active. Disabled users can still be members of task groups, but workflow tasks cannot be allocated to these users.

title: Create Active Hyperlinks for Use in Search and Reporting in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create Active Hyperlinks for Use in Search and Reporting in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to define a URL for string attributes in Docusign CLM. After an administrator defines an attribute that gets assigned a URL, users can select the hyperlink from reports or search results.

In Docusign CLM, administrators can define a hyperlink URL for string attributes. When users execute searches or run reports, the attribute shows up as a hyperlink in the results. The user can select the hyperlink to open it directly from the report or search results.

Note: To make the hyperlink visible in reporting or search results, the user might need to customize the columns.

**Examples:** You can define hyperlinks that lead to documents that are stored outside of Docusign CLM. You can also define links to Salesforce objects.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM.
2. [Create a string attribute within an attribute group](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=sos1576610120271.html&_LANG=enus).
3. Perform one of the following activities:

   - [Create a workflow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yks1643320936212&topicId=zyz1677247832120.html&_LANG=enus) that executes the Update Document Metadata Value step to change the attribute’s value to a URL.

     Note: You can also use the step to update read-only attributes.
   - [Add a URL value to any non-read-only string attribute in a document preview](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=lun1683559393884&topicId=yeo1576610114693.html&_LANG=enus)

   The product saves the URL value for the attribute.

You have successfully created active hyperlinks for use in search and reporting. A user can select the hyperlink in a report or search results to open a new browser window.

title: Create Active Hyperlinks for Use in Search and Reporting in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Create Active Hyperlinks for Use in Search and Reporting in Docusign CLM
text:
In Docusign CLM, you want to store a hyperlink that accesses a policy document. This external document exists outside of Docusign CLM. In this example, your organization uses the following process:

1. Your CLM administrator creates a string attribute within an attribute group.
2. The administrator executes the Update Document Metadata Value workflow step to change the attribute’s value to a URL.
3. A user searches for information about the policy document.
4. The user selects the hyperlink in the search results to open the document in a new browser window.

title: Folder Limits in Docusign CLM
text:
Read about folder limits in Docusign CLM. The product limits the
number of child objects in a folder and limits the number of subfolders for a folder in a
folder tree.

Docusign CLM enforces the following limits on folders:

- A maximum of 10,000 direct child objects can exist in a folder. This includes
  documents and sub-folders.
- A maximum of 100 subfolders can display under a folder in a folder tree.

The following sections discuss these limits.

title: Folder Limits in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Limiting the number of objects within a folder
text:
Docusign CLM enforces a limit of 10,000 direct child objects within
a folder (including documents and sub-folders). If you exceed this limit, the
product displays a warning message that there are too many folders to display. In a
specific folder view, the product also warns that there are more items in this
folder than the product can display at once. In this situation, the product hides
all objects in the folder.

Note: If you encounter the messages, you can search in CLM to
locate your folder contents. For information about search, see [Search in Docusign
CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsk1642969066834&topicId=aug1576609932447.html&_LANG=enus).

To access the contents of a folder that exceeded the limit, contact Customer Support to
request a one-time temporary exception. The Docusign Engineering
team can remove the limit for a maximum of seven days. You can then reorganize
objects to drop below the limit.

An administrator must submit the request to [Customer Support](http://support.docusign.com/). In the request, you must
confirm in writing that you understand that the exception will be granted for only
seven days. You must also agree that you will not request any other exceptions. The
request must also specify the root folder, the folder path, and the following
information:

- Why you are requesting the temporary exception to lift the object limit
- How the folder structure grew to 10,000 objects
- How you plan to prevent your new folder structure from exceeding the limit

After receiving your request, Customer Support will request [temporary access to your account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=elo1616712084862.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to provide temporary access to Docusign Support."). You can manage permissions as needed
for this temporary access.

title: Folder Limits in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Limiting the number of subfolders under each folder in a folder tree
text:
On the Documents page in Docusign CLM, a
maximum of 100 subfolders can be displayed under each folder in the folder tree. The
product shows the tree on the left side of the page. Enforcing the limit helps
prevent performance issues from loading excessive numbers of elements in the product
interface.

Note: Even if some folders do not display in the tree, those
folders are still accessible. To find these folders, navigate through the folder
structure in the middle portion of the Documents page.

To avoid the limit, reorganize your content so that every folder contains fewer than
100 subfolders. If desired, you can use [secure
file transfer protocol (SFTP)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=lam1619202914294.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how to connect an SFTP client to Docusign CLM for secure uploads and backups.") to help with your reorganization.
Additionally, be aware of any automated processes that are automatically creating
folders. Limiting these processes can help you avoid exceeding this limit. Examples
of automated processes are as follows:

- Salesforce mapping
- Smart rules
- Workflows

title: Manage Schedules in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to manage schedules in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use
schedules for recurring and explicit document-management events.

Administrators can manage schedules in Docusign CLM. Administrators can use schedules for recurring and explicit
document-management events.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   An administrative sidebar opens.
3. Select Schedules under Automation Tools.

   The Schedules page opens.
4. Select the checkbox next to the schedule that you want to manage.
5. Edit or delete the schedule as needed:
   - Select Schedule > Edit to make changes to the schedule.
   - Select Schedule > Delete to eliminate the schedule.

   Note: Before deleting a schedule, check your smart rules to ensure
   that the schedule is not in use. If the schedule is used in a smart rule, change the
   rule to point to a different schedule.

You are finished managing schedules.

title: Reset a User's Password in CLM.CM
heading_path: Reset a User's Password in CLM.CM
text:
Learn how to reset a user's password in CLM.CM. Super administrators or user
administrators can reset a password for a user.

CLM.CM super administrators or user administrators
can reset a password for a user.

1. Log on to CLM.CM.

   Note: For UAT accounts, use the [CLM.CM](https://loginuat.springcm.com/) UAT
   logon page.
2. Select Admin in the navigation header.

   The Admin sidebar opens.
3. Select Users under User Management.
4. Select the box next to the user whose password you want to reset.
5. Select Manage > Reset Password.

The product sends a *password reset* email to the
user’s email address. The user cannot log on with the previous password.

title: Customize a Menu in Docusign CLM
heading_path: Customize a Menu in Docusign CLM
text:
Learn how to customize menus by creating custom menu items in Docusign
CLM.

Administrators and super administrators can create
custom menu items in Docusign CLM. For example, you can add an item to
help users reach and execute a document action more easily.

Note:

- If the menu item does not need users to pre-select a document, [add the item to the global Actions menu
  instead](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pxt1643324456371&topicId=eal1576610028402.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to customize the Actions menu in Docusign CLM. Administrators can add custom actions to the menu to help users save time when executing common tasks.").
- You can remove existing custom menu items to streamline the user experience.
  From the Documents page, select View > Customize Menu, select your custom menu item, and select
  Delete.

1. Log in to Docusign CLM as an administrator or super
   administrator.
2. Select Documents in the navigation header.

   The Documents page opens.
3. Select View > Customize Menu.

   The Customize Menu window opens.
4. Select Create Menu Item.

   The product displays options for configuring the custom menu item.
5. Configure the custom menu item:
   1. Select the Follows Menu Item drop-down, and
      select the menu item that the new item will follow.
   2. Specify a name for the menu item in the Name
      field.
   3. Specify a URL for the menu item in the URL
      field.
   4. (Optional) Select the check boxes for Add Selection to
      URL and Add User Key to
      URL.
   5. Enter the email address or name for which the customizations display in
      the Menu appears for field.

      Note: If this field is left blank, the changes
      apply to all users.
   6. (Optional) Enter a description for the menu item in the
      Description field.
   7. (Optional) Enter a URL for an icon for the menu item in the
      Toolbar Icon URL field.
   8. (Optional) Add separators before or after the menu item by selecting
      the check boxes for Separator Before or
      Separator After.
   9. Select Save.

You have successfully created a custom menu item in Docusign CLM. The new menu item gets displayed for the selected user,
user group, or all users in Docusign CLM.

title: Activate Upload
text:
Learn how to upload a CSV file into a folder that contains documents to receive
attributes. Administrators perform this upload as part of assigning attribute values to multiple
documents without the need for manual entry.

Upload the CSV file into the folder which contains the documents and which was specified in the "In Folder" filter of your rule. The Bulk Attribute Assignment will begin immediately, and Docusign CLM will send e-mail notifications to the user who last modified the rule if any exceptions or failures arise.

Setting up Bulk Attribute Import requires application of Docusign CLM attribute groups,
smart rules, and attribute import mappings.

Note: For more information about creating attribute import mappings and
managing attribute groups, see the documentation about [Document Attributes in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=rxl1576795529933.html&_LANG=enus) and [Smart Rules in Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=yhx1576610044302.html&_LANG=enus).
